name: Sushi
ingredients: 2 cups uncooked short-grain Japanese rice (sushi rice)
1/2 lb sushi-grade raw salmon or tuna, sliced into strips
instructions: Rinse the rice until the water runs clear. Cook according to package instructions (usually 1:1.2 rice-to-water ratio).
While the rice is hot, gently fold in the mixture of rice vinegar, sugar, and salt. Let the rice cool to room temperature.
Slice fish and vegetables into long, thin strips (about 4 inches long).
Place a bamboo rolling mat on a flat surface. Lay a sheet of nori (shiny side down) on the mat.
Wet hands with water and spread a thin, even layer of rice over the nori, leaving a 1-inch border at the top edge.
Place fillings in a horizontal line across the center of the rice.
Using the mat, lift the edge closest to you and roll it tightly over the fillings.
Dampen the 1-inch border of nori with water to seal the roll.
Use a sharp knife to cut the roll into 6–8 equal pieces, wiping the knife with a damp cloth between cuts.
Serve immediately with soy sauce, wasabi, and ginger.

name: Sashimi
ingredients: 1/2 lb to 1 lb sushi-grade fish (Tuna, Salmon, or Yellowtail)
instructions: Keep the fish in the refrigerator until ready to slice to ensure firmness.
Peel and julienne Daikon radish into thin strips. Soak in ice water for 5 minutes, then drain and pat dry.
Place the fish block on a clean cutting board.
Using a very sharp knife, cut across the grain of the fish into slices approximately 1/4 to 1/3 inch (6-8 mm) thick using one fluid pulling motion.
Arrange the fish slices in a fanned-out, overlapping row against the Daikon and Shiso leaf garnish.
Serve immediately with individual dishes of soy sauce and a dollop of wasabi and ginger on the side.

name: Tempura Shrimp
ingredients: 12-15 Large or Jumbo Shrimp (peeled and deveined, tails on)
1/4 cup all-purpose flour or cornstarch (for dredging)
Neutral oil (vegetable or canola), for frying
instructions: Prepare shrimp by making 3-4 shallow horizontal slits across the belly. Press down on the back to straighten them until they 'pop'. Pat dry.
Heat oil to 350°F (175°C) in a pot (2-3 inches deep).
Make the batter by lightly beating the cold egg and adding ice-cold water.
Add sifted flour all at once and mix with chopsticks for only 10-20 seconds. The batter should remain lumpy; do not overmix.
Dredge each shrimp in dry flour/cornstarch, shaking off excess.
Dip shrimp into the cold batter holding the tail, and gently lower into the hot oil.
Fry for 2-3 minutes until pale golden and crispy. Drain on a wire rack or paper towels.
Serve hot with Tentsuyu dipping sauce or sea salt.

name: Takoyaki
ingredients: 4-5 oz boiled octopus, cut into 1/2-inch cubes
2 tbsp pickled ginger (Beni Shoga), finely chopped
instructions: Whisk eggs, soy sauce, and salt in a bowl. Gradually add dashi stock and flour, whisking until a thin, smooth batter forms.
Heat a takoyaki pan over medium-high heat and grease each cavity and the flat surface generously with oil.
Pour batter into the holes until they overflow slightly onto the flat surface.
Drop one piece of octopus into each hole. Sprinkle green onions, pickled ginger, and tenkasu over the entire pan.
After 2-3 minutes, use a pick to 'cut' the excess batter and flip each ball 90 degrees, tucking excess batter inside.
Continue rotating the balls frequently until they form a spherical shape and become golden brown and crispy (about 4-5 minutes).
Transfer to a plate and drizzle with takoyaki sauce and mayonnaise.
Top with aonori and bonito flakes. Serve immediately.

name: Teriyaki Salmon
ingredients: Green onions and toasted sesame seeds, for garnish
1 tsp cornstarch mixed with 1 tsp water (optional slurry)
instructions: Whisk together soy sauce, water, mirin, brown sugar, sesame oil, ginger, and garlic to make the sauce.
Optionally marinate salmon in half the sauce for 15-20 minutes. Reserve remaining sauce.
Heat oil in a non-stick skillet over medium-high heat. Season salmon with salt and pepper.
Sear salmon skin-side up for 4-5 minutes until golden. Flip and cook for another 3-4 minutes until done. Remove salmon.
Pour reserved sauce into the skillet. Bring to a simmer. If using, add cornstarch slurry to thicken.
Return salmon to the pan and coat with the glaze.
Serve immediately garnished with green onions and sesame seeds.

name: Pad Thai with Shrimp
ingredients: 5 oz dried flat rice noodles (medium width)
2-3 tbsp palm sugar or brown sugar
1/4 cup extra-firm tofu, cut into small batons
instructions: Soak rice noodles in lukewarm water for 30–40 minutes until al dente. Drain and set aside.
Whisk together fish sauce, tamarind paste, sugar, and chili flakes to make the sauce.
Heat 1 tbsp oil in a wok over high heat. Sear shrimp until pink (1-2 mins per side). Remove and set aside.
Add remaining oil, shallots, garlic, and tofu. Stir-fry for 1-2 minutes until lightly browned.
Add noodles and sauce. Stir-fry vigorously, adding 1-2 tbsp water if needed until noodles are tender.
Push noodles to the side, pour in eggs, and scramble until mostly set. Toss into the noodles.
Add shrimp, bean sprouts, and scallions. Toss for 30-60 seconds.
Serve topped with crushed peanuts and lime wedges on the side.

name: Thai Fish Curry
ingredients: 1.5 lbs firm white fish (snapper, cod, or halibut), cut into 2-inch pieces
2-3 tbsp Thai red or yellow curry paste
2-3 cups mixed vegetables (carrots, snap peas, bell peppers)
Fresh cilantro and lime wedges, for garnish
instructions: Heat oil in a large skillet over medium heat. Sauté onion until soft (3-5 mins). Add garlic and ginger and cook for 1 minute.
Stir in curry paste and cook for 1-2 minutes until fragrant.
Add coconut milk, water, fish sauce, and sugar. Bring to a gentle simmer.
Add vegetables and simmer for 3-5 minutes until they start to soften.
Nestle fish pieces into the sauce. Cover and simmer for 4-6 minutes until fish is opaque and flakes easily.
Stir in lime juice. Adjust seasoning with more fish sauce if needed.
Serve hot over jasmine rice, garnished with cilantro.

name: Tom Yum Seafood Soup
ingredients: 2 stalks lemongrass, bruised and cut into 2-inch pieces
3-6 Thai bird’s eye chilies, lightly smashed
1 lb mixed seafood (prawns, squid, mussels, fish)
1-2 tbsp Thai roasted chili paste (Nam Prik Pao)
instructions: Bring stock to a boil in a large pot. Add lemongrass, galangal, lime leaves, and shallots. Simmer for 5 minutes.
Add mushrooms, tomatoes, and chili paste. Stir until dissolved.
Add seafood and cook for 2-3 minutes until prawns are pink and mussels open.
Season with fish sauce and sugar. Taste and adjust flavors.
Turn off heat and stir in lime juice and smashed chilies.
Garnish with cilantro and serve hot.

name: Sweet and Sour Fish
ingredients: 1 lb white fish fillets, cut into bite-sized pieces
1 tbsp cornstarch dissolved in 2 tbsp water (slurry)
1 onion, 1 red bell pepper, 1 green bell pepper (cut into chunks)
1 tsp minced ginger, 2 cloves minced garlic
instructions: Marinate fish with soy sauce, salt, and pepper for 10 minutes.
Dip fish in egg, then coat with flour and cornstarch mixture.
Deep fry fish in hot oil until golden brown and crispy (3-4 mins per side). Drain and set aside.
In a clean pan, sauté ginger and garlic. Add onion, bell peppers, and pineapple. Stir-fry for 2 minutes.
Add pineapple juice, vinegar, ketchup, sugar, and soy sauce. Simmer.
Stir in cornstarch slurry to thicken.
Toss the fried fish into the sauce quickly to coat and serve immediately with rice.

name: Steamed Fish with Ginger
ingredients: 1.5 lbs whole fish (sea bass or snapper) or 2 large fillets
Fresh cilantro and sesame oil, for garnish
instructions: Rinse fish, pat dry, and make diagonal slashes if using whole fish. Rub with salt and cooking wine.
Place half the ginger and onions on a plate, lay fish on top, and scatter remaining ginger over fish.
Steam on high heat for 8-10 minutes (fillets) or 10-12 minutes (whole fish).
Drain excess liquid from the plate. Top fish with fresh shredded onions and cilantro.
Heat oil in a small pan until smoking.
Pour hot oil over the onions and ginger to sear them.
Whisk soy sauce, sugar, and water, then drizzle around the base of the fish.
Garnish with sesame oil and serve hot.

name: Shrimp Fried Rice
ingredients: 4 cups cooked white rice, preferably day-old and cold
1 lb medium shrimp, peeled and deveined
1 cup frozen peas and carrots, thawed
3 green onions, thinly sliced (separate whites and greens)
Salt and black pepper to taste
instructions: Heat 1 tbsp oil in a wok. Season shrimp with salt and pepper, cook for 1-2 mins per side until pink. Set aside.
Lower heat, add a splash of oil, and scramble eggs until just set. Set aside.
Add remaining oil, white parts of green onions, garlic, and ginger. Sauté for 30 seconds. Add peas and carrots, cook for 2 mins.
Add cold rice, breaking up clumps. Stir-fry for 3-5 minutes until heated through.
Pour soy sauce and sesame oil over rice. Add shrimp and eggs back into the pan.
Toss together for 1-2 minutes.
Stir in green parts of green onions and serve immediately.

name: Chili Crab
ingredients: 1-2 large Mud crabs (1-1.5kg), cleaned and cut into pieces (claws cracked)
1 tbsp rice vinegar or lime juice
1 tbsp cornstarch dissolved in 2 tbsp water (slurry)
Fresh cilantro and green onions, for garnish
instructions: Clean and quarter the crab; crack the claws.
Sauté shallots, garlic, ginger, and chilies in oil for 1-2 minutes until fragrant.
Add crab pieces and stir-fry for 2-3 minutes until shells turn red.
Add broth, ketchup, chili sauce, sugar, and salt. Cover and simmer for 5-8 minutes.
Stir in cornstarch slurry and simmer until thickened and glossy.
Lower heat, drizzle in the beaten egg, and gently swirl into the sauce after 10 seconds.
Stir in vinegar or lime juice. Garnish and serve immediately with mantou or rice.

name: Fish Head Curry
ingredients: 1 large fish head (600g-800g, Red Snapper or Grouper), cleaned and halved
4-5 tbsp fish curry powder (mixed into a paste with water)
3 tbsp tamarind paste diluted in 2 cups water
1 tsp mustard seeds, 1/2 tsp fenugreek seeds, 2 sprigs curry leaves
instructions: Sauté mustard seeds, fenugreek, onions, garlic, ginger, and curry leaves in oil until fragrant.
Add curry powder paste and stir-fry until oil separates.
Pour in tamarind water and coconut milk. Bring to a boil.
Add eggplant and okra; simmer for 5 minutes.
Gently add fish head and tomatoes. Cover and simmer for 10-12 minutes until fish is cooked.
Season with salt and sugar. Serve hot with rice.

name: Laksa
ingredients: Laksa Paste: 4-6 dried chilies, 3 shallots, 4 garlic cloves, 1-inch ginger/galangal, 2 stalks lemongrass, 1 tsp turmeric, 1 tsp coriander, 1 tbsp shrimp paste
Fresh cilantro/laksa leaves, fried shallots, lime wedges
1 tbsp palm sugar, fish sauce to taste
instructions: Blend laksa paste ingredients until smooth.
Fry the paste in oil for 5-8 minutes until oil separates.
Add stock, coconut milk, sugar, fish sauce, and tofu puffs. Simmer for 10-15 minutes.
Add chicken and shrimp to the broth; cook for 3-5 minutes.
Prepare noodles and place in bowls.
Ladle hot broth and proteins over noodles.
Top with bean sprouts, egg, herbs, and shallots. Serve with lime.

name: Sambal Prawns
ingredients: 500g large prawns, peeled and deveined (tails on)
10-15 dried red chilies, 3-5 fresh red chilies
1 tbsp tamarind paste mixed with 3 tbsp water
instructions: Blend chilies, shallots, garlic, ginger, and shrimp paste into a smooth sambal.
Fry the sambal in oil for 5-8 minutes until oil separates.
Stir in tamarind water, sugar, and salt; simmer to thicken.
Add prawns and toss to coat. Cook for 3-4 minutes until pink and opaque.
Serve hot with steamed rice.

name: Grilled Fish with Sambal
ingredients: 1 large whole fish (1.5-2 lbs, Snapper or Sea Bass)

Sambal: 10 shallots, 4 garlic cloves, 2 lemongrass stalks, 1 inch ginger, 1 inch galangal, 4-6 red chilies, 1 tsp turmeric, 1 tbsp shrimp paste, 2 tbsp tamarind paste, 2 tbsp sweet soy sauce, 1 tbsp palm sugar, 1 tsp salt
Lime wedges, sliced cucumber for serving
instructions: Blend sambal ingredients and fry in oil until oil separates. Let cool.
Clean fish and make diagonal slashes. Rub cooled sambal all over fish.
Marinate for at least 1-2 hours.
Wrap fish in banana leaves or foil.
Grill over medium-high heat for 10-12 minutes per side until cooked through.
Serve hot with lime and rice.

name: Sinigang na Isda
instructions: Boil water with onions and tomatoes; simmer for 5 minutes.
Stir in sinigang mix.
Add fish and green peppers; simmer for 5-8 minutes.
Add radish, okra, and eggplant; simmer until tender (3-5 mins).
Season with fish sauce.
Add kangkong leaves, turn off heat, and cover to let residual heat cook them.
Serve hot with rice.

name: Inihaw na Bangus
ingredients: Salt and black pepper to taste
Banana leaves or foil for wrapping
instructions: Clean fish, leave scales on, and rub with salt and pepper.
Mix tomatoes, onion, ginger, calamansi juice, and soy sauce to make stuffing.
Stuff the mixture into the fish cavity.
Wrap fish in banana leaves or foil.
Grill over medium heat for 10-15 minutes per side.
Serve hot with toyomansi dipping sauce.

name: Adobong Pusit
ingredients: 1 tsp peppercorns, 2 bay leaves, 1 tsp sugar
instructions: Clean squid, separating ink sacs into a small bowl. Slice bodies into rings.
Sauté garlic, onion, and tomatoes until soft.
Add soy sauce, vinegar, squid ink, bay leaves, and peppercorns. Boil without stirring for 2 minutes.
Add squid; simmer for 3-5 minutes only to prevent toughness.
Add sugar and season with salt if needed.
Serve hot with rice.

name: Kinilaw
ingredients: 1 lb fresh firm-fleshed fish (Tuna or Tanigue), cubed
1/2 cup coconut or cane vinegar (for marinade)
Salt and black pepper to taste
instructions: Rinse fish cubes in 1/2 cup vinegar for 1-2 minutes; drain and discard vinegar.
Add ginger, onion, and chilies to the fish. Season with salt and pepper.
Pour in fresh vinegar and calamansi juice. Add cucumber if using.
Toss well and chill for 10-15 minutes until fish turns opaque.
Serve chilled immediately.

name: Korean Grilled Mackerel
ingredients: 1 fresh mackerel, filleted into 2 or 4 pieces (or butterflied whole)
Sea salt (or kosher salt), to taste
Cooking oil, to lightly oil grill/pan (as needed)
instructions: Cut a few slashes on the skin side. Pat fish dry.
Drizzle with optional lemon and ginger juice. Season both sides liberally with salt and let sit 20 to 30 minutes.
Shake off excess salt.
Grill over medium-high heat, skin-side down, 2 to 3 minutes until edges brown and flesh turns opaque; flip and cook about 2 minutes more (or pan-fry/broil using the same timing cues).
Serve hot with rice and simple sides.

name: Haemul Pajeon
ingredients: 1/2 tsp kosher salt, plus a pinch
3/4 cup stock (anchovy-kelp, chicken, vegetable) or water
12 green onions, trimmed to fit skillet (about 8 to 9 inches long)
4 to 5 oz mixed seafood (shrimp, squid, clams), chopped
Dipping sauce: 2 tbsp soy sauce, 1 tbsp vinegar, 1/2 tsp gochugaru, 1 chopped green onion, 1 tsp toasted sesame seeds
instructions: Make dipping sauce by combining soy sauce, vinegar, gochugaru, chopped green onion, and sesame seeds; set aside.
Season chopped seafood with a pinch of salt and pepper.
Whisk flour, potato starch, 1/2 tsp salt, pepper, and stock/water into a smooth batter.
Heat 2 tbsp oil in a large nonstick skillet over medium-high. Coat green onions in batter and lay side by side in the pan to form a rectangle.
Mix seafood into remaining batter and spread over the scallions. Top with red pepper and pour beaten egg over the pancake.
Reduce heat to medium and cook about 6 minutes until bottom is light brown and crisp. Add remaining oil as needed and flip; cook until the other side is crisp and golden.
Cut into pieces and serve with dipping sauce.

name: Fish Curry
ingredients: Sustainable white fish fillets, skinned and cut into big chunks
Cooked rice or naan, to serve
instructions: Heat oil in a deep pan and gently fry onion and garlic about 5 minutes until soft.
Add curry paste and stir-fry 1 to 2 minutes.
Add chopped tomatoes and stock; bring to a simmer.
Add fish and gently cook 4 to 5 minutes until it flakes easily.
Serve immediately with rice or naan.

name: Prawn Masala
ingredients: 1 thumb-sized piece ginger, peeled and grated
Cooked rice and naan (optional), to serve
instructions: Blitz onion, ginger and garlic to a smooth paste.
Heat oil in a pan over medium heat. Fry the onion paste about 8 minutes until lightly golden.
Stir in curry paste and fry 1 minute. Add tomatoes, tomato puree, sugar and cardamom; simmer covered about 10 minutes.
Remove cardamom, then blend sauce smooth and return to pan.
Stir in ground almonds and prawns; cook about 5 minutes until prawns are pink and cooked through.
Season to taste, then stir in cream and coriander. Serve with rice and naan if desired.

name: Goan Fish Curry
ingredients: 6 pieces sea fish of choice (firm fish)
2 tsp kokum concentrate (or 2 tsp lemon juice)
5 Bedgi red chilies (or dried red chilies)
instructions: Marinate fish with salt, Kashmiri chilli powder, turmeric and kokum (or lemon). Set aside.
Blend coconut, onions, tomato, dried red chilies, peppercorns, cloves and garlic into a very smooth paste (add a little water if needed).
Heat oil in a pot. Add mustard seeds; when they crackle, add curry leaves.
Add blended coconut paste and cook covered about 10 minutes until the raw smell cooks off.
Add marinated fish and simmer gently about 5 minutes (or until fish is just cooked).
Serve hot with steamed rice.

name: Fish and Chips
ingredients: 2 thick skinless white fish fillets (whiting, ling, pollock, haddock or cod), about 150 g each
1 tsp malt vinegar (plus extra to serve)
Batter: 75 g self-raising flour (plus extra for dusting), 25 g cornflour, pinch ground turmeric, 125 g cold lager (or fizzy water), salt
Tartare sauce (optional): cornichons, capers, shallot, parsley, mayonnaise
instructions: Cut potatoes into chips. Soak 5 minutes, rinse until water runs clear, then simmer in salted water with 1 tsp malt vinegar 10-12 minutes until just cooked. Drain and chill on a tray in a single layer.
Make batter by quickly mixing flours and turmeric with cold beer/fizzy water and a pinch of salt; do not overmix. Keep chilled.
Heat oil to about 180 C and fry chips 8-10 minutes until golden and crisp; drain on paper.
Increase oil to about 185 C. Dust fish in flour, dip in batter, and fry about 4 minutes, turning once, until deep golden and cooked through.
Serve fish with chips, salt and vinegar (and tartare sauce if using).

name: Smoked Salmon
ingredients: Kosher salt and freshly ground black pepper, to taste
Rye toast points (or crackers), for serving
instructions: Mix cream cheese, capers, red onion, lemon zest and juice; season with salt and pepper.
Spread mixture on rye toast points or crackers.
Top with smoked salmon and serve.

name: Gravlax
ingredients: 1 cup dill sprigs (plus 1/3 cup chopped dill for coating)
instructions: Coarsely grind peppercorns, fennel and caraway; mix with salt and sugar.
Lay salmon on plastic wrap on a plate. Cover with salt-sugar mixture, dill sprigs, and aquavit if using. Wrap tightly.
Refrigerate 48-72 hours, turning every 12 hours and redistributing the brine.
Unwrap, discard brine and dill, rinse salmon briefly and pat dry.
Press the flesh side into chopped dill to coat. Slice thinly on the diagonal and serve.

name: Bouillabaisse
ingredients: 1 leek, white finely sliced (green top reserved if using for stock)
1 fennel bulb, chopped (fronds reserved for garnish)
4 large ripe tomatoes, chopped (or canned tomatoes)
Parsley (stalks for stock, leaves chopped for serving)
Fish stock (enough for a generous soup base)
Mixed seafood: firm white fish pieces plus shellfish (e.g., mussels, shrimp/prawns)
Salt and black pepper, to taste
To serve (optional but classic): rouille and toasted bread
instructions: Make the soup base: saute onion, leek and fennel in olive oil until softened. Add garlic and tomato puree and cook briefly.
Add tomatoes, saffron, bay, thyme, parsley stalks, orange peel and chilli; cook down a few minutes, then add fish stock and simmer to develop flavor.
Add firm fish pieces first and simmer gently until nearly cooked, then add quicker-cooking seafood (shrimp/mussels) and cook just until done and mussels open (discard any that stay shut).
Taste and season, then serve hot with chopped herbs (and rouille + toast if using).

name: Sole Meunière
ingredients: 4 fillets sole or plaice, skin-on (about 140 g each)
Salt and black pepper, to taste
3 tbsp light olive oil (or sunflower oil)
instructions: Season flour with salt and pepper. Coat fish in flour and shake off excess.
Heat oil in a large frying pan. Cook fish skin-side down about 2 minutes, then flip and cook 1 to 2 minutes until golden and just cooked.
Remove fish to a warm plate and season. Wipe out the pan, then melt butter until it turns light brown.
Stir in lemon juice and capers (optional). Return fish briefly and spoon sauce over.
Serve immediately.

name: Moules Marinières
ingredients: Salt and black pepper, to taste
Optional: 3 tbsp creme fraiche (to enrich the sauce)
instructions: Clean mussels: scrub shells, remove beards, and rinse quickly with cold water (do not soak).
Melt butter in a large pot. Add onion and cook about 1 minute.
Add mussels and white wine. Season and add parsley, bay and thyme.
Cook over high heat about 6 minutes, shaking/stirring regularly, until mussels open (discard any that stay shut).
Remove bay and thyme. Pour cooking juices over mussels and serve hot.

name: Paella
instructions: Heat oven to 220 C/200 C fan. Heat tomatoes, stock, paprika and saffron until steaming hot.
Roast onion and garlic with olive oil in a large ovenproof pan 20 minutes until starting to brown.
Stir in rice, chicken, chorizo and hot stock mixture; season and bake uncovered 20 minutes.
Stir in peas, add prawns, and press mussels into rice. Bake 5 to 10 minutes more until rice is cooked, prawns are pink and mussels opened (discard any that stay shut).
Serve with lemon wedges if using.

name: Gambas al Ajillo
ingredients: 12 large raw prawns, peeled and deveined
3 dried cayenne chillies (or other dried chilli)
Sea salt and black pepper, to taste
instructions: Season prawns lightly with salt.
Put garlic, olive oil and dried chillies in a small flameproof dish or frying pan over high heat.
When garlic starts to turn golden, add prawns and cook 1 to 2 minutes per side until just pink.
Sprinkle with parsley and a little black pepper and serve immediately with bread.

name: Bacalao
ingredients: 8 pieces desalted cod (or salt cod soaked/desalted for about 48 hours, changing water several times)
1 dried chilli (or chilli flakes), to taste
Salt, if needed (often not needed if cod is salted)
instructions: Warm olive oil in a pan over medium heat. Add garlic and chilli and cook until the garlic starts to brown; remove and reserve.
Take the pan off the heat and let oil cool slightly.
Add cod pieces to the oil (skin-side up) and gently cook about 5 minutes, keeping the heat low so oil doesn't bubble vigorously.

Move cod to a second pan. Add a few spoonfuls of oil, let rest briefly, then return to low heat and swirl the pan in circles to emulsify the sauce, gradually adding more oil until thickened (pil-pil).
Serve cod with the emulsified sauce and top with the reserved garlic and chilli.

name: Calamari Fritti
ingredients: 2 tbsp capers, drained and finely chopped
Vegetable or sunflower oil, for frying
Salt and black pepper, to taste
instructions: Slice squid into rings about 1/2 cm thick.
Season flour well and mix in chopped capers. Coat squid in the flour mixture and shake off excess.
Mix garlic into mayonnaise for a quick dipping sauce.
Heat frying oil in a deep pan (no more than one-third full). Fry squid in batches about 3 minutes until crisp and lightly golden.
Drain on paper towels and serve immediately with garlic mayo and lemon wedges.

name: Seafood Risotto
ingredients: Salt and black pepper, to taste
instructions: Microwave onion and fennel with olive oil 5 minutes until softened.
Stir in rice and stock, cover and microwave on High 10 to 15 minutes until rice is almost cooked.
Stir in seafood and peas, cover and microwave 2 to 3 minutes until rice is cooked and seafood is hot.
Stir in parmesan and lemon juice; rest briefly.
Mix parsley with lemon zest and scatter over to serve.

name: Spaghetti alle Vongole
ingredients: 1 small fresh red chilli, finely chopped
Salt and black pepper, to taste
instructions: Rinse clams well in several changes of cold water; discard any open or damaged.
Peel and deseed tomatoes, then chop the flesh.
Cook spaghetti according to package directions.
Heat olive oil in a large pan; briefly fry garlic and chilli. Add tomatoes, then clams and a splash of wine; bring to the boil.
Cover and cook 3 to 4 minutes until clams open (discard any that stay shut).
Drain pasta, add to the clam pan with parsley, toss and serve.

name: Baccalà alla Vicentina
ingredients: 1 kg soaked baccalà (salt cod), boned and cut into pieces
instructions: Lightly dredge baccalà pieces in flour and brown briefly in a pan with olive oil; remove and keep warm.
Gently saute chopped onion in olive oil. Add baccalà pieces, keeping them intact.
Mix garlic, anchovies and parsley; combine with breadcrumbs and Parmigiano and sprinkle over the fish.
Add about 1 cup water, then cook uncovered on very low heat 45 to 60 minutes, adding a little water if it dries out.
Check and adjust salt near the end. Serve with polenta.

name: Fish Soup (Halászlé)
ingredients: 500 g carp tail (or other freshwater fish on the bone)
500 g catfish fillet (or another firm fish), cut into portions
instructions: Stir paprika into oil/lard in a soup pot (off the heat briefly if needed so paprika doesn't burn).
Add water, chopped onion, salt, and the carp pieces. Simmer until the fish is tender and the meat falls from the bone.
Remove fish pieces and press the cooked fish meat and broth through a strainer back into the pot to enrich the soup base.
Add catfish fillets and simmer a couple of minutes until just cooked.
Serve hot with white bread.

name: Pickled Herring
ingredients: 1 kg fresh herring fillets, cut into bite-size pieces
To serve: chopped dill and chives (optional)
instructions: Pre-salt the herring: combine 500 ml water, 100 ml vinegar and salt and soak herring in the fridge 4 to 5 hours; drain well.

Make pickling brine: combine remaining vinegar with sugar, onions, carrots, bay leaves, peppercorns and juniper berries plus 500 ml water; bring to a boil and stir to dissolve sugar, then cool completely.
Pour cooled brine over herring in a non-reactive container and refrigerate overnight (ideally 24 hours).
Serve chilled with chopped dill/chives if desired.

name: Shrimp Cocktail
ingredients: 1 to 1.5 lbs large shrimp, peeled and deveined, tails left on
1 tbsp Old Bay seasoning or pickling spice
instructions: Fill a large pot with water. Squeeze in lemon juice and drop in the halves. Add seasoning and salt. Bring to a boil.
Add shrimp to boiling water. Turn off heat, cover, and let sit for 3–4 minutes until pink and opaque.
Immediately transfer shrimp to a bowl of ice water to stop the cooking. Once cold, drain and pat dry.
Whisk cocktail sauce ingredients (ketchup, horseradish, lemon juice, Worcestershire, and hot sauce) in a small bowl.
Arrange shrimp on a platter or in glasses with the sauce in the center. Garnish with extra lemon wedges.

name: Fish Pie
ingredients: 600g mixed fish fillets (cod, salmon, and smoked haddock), cut into bite-sized chunks
Handful of fresh parsley, finely chopped
Salt and black pepper to taste
instructions: Boil chopped potatoes in salted water for 15–20 minutes until soft. Drain and mash with butter and milk until smooth.
Melt 30g butter in a saucepan, whisk in flour to form a roux and cook for 1 minute. Gradually whisk in milk until the sauce thickens. Stir in mustard, parsley, salt, and pepper.
Preheat oven to 200°C (400°F). Place fish chunks and peas in a large ovenproof baking dish. Pour the white sauce over and stir gently.
Spoon mashed potatoes over the fish mixture, spread evenly, and create peaks with a fork. Sprinkle with grated cheddar cheese.
Bake for 20–25 minutes until the sauce is bubbling and the top is golden brown and crispy.

name: Lobster Thermidor
ingredients: 1/2 cup dry white wine or 2 tbsp Cognac
Salt and black pepper to taste
1/2 cup Gruyère or Parmesan cheese, grated
instructions: Cut lobsters in half lengthwise. Remove meat from tails and claws, chop into bite-sized chunks, and set aside. Place empty shells on a baking sheet.
Melt butter in a skillet over medium heat. Sauté shallots and garlic until soft. Stir in flour and cook for 1–2 minutes.
Pour in white wine (or Cognac) and reduce by half. Slowly whisk in heavy cream and stock, simmering until thickened.
Stir in Dijon mustard, tarragon, parsley, lemon juice, salt, and pepper. Remove from heat and gently fold in the lobster meat.
Divide the mixture among the lobster shell halves and sprinkle with grated cheese.
Place under a preheated broiler for 3–5 minutes until the cheese is melted and golden brown.

name: Crab Bisque
ingredients: 1 small yellow onion or 3 large shallots, finely minced
Fresh chives or parsley, finely chopped
instructions: Melt butter in a large pot. Sauté onion or shallots until soft (about 3–5 minutes).
Whisk in flour and cook for 1–2 minutes to create a blond roux.
Slowly whisk in fish stock and bring the mixture to a gentle simmer.
Stir in heavy cream, Old Bay, salt, pepper, and nutmeg. Simmer on low heat for 10 minutes.
Gently fold in crabmeat and stir in the dry sherry. Heat through for 3–5 minutes.
Ladle into bowls and garnish with fresh herbs.

name: Seafood Pasta
ingredients: 1/2 lb large shrimp, peeled and deveined
Salt and black pepper to taste
instructions: Cook pasta in salted water until al dente. Reserve 1 cup of pasta water before draining.
Sear seasoned shrimp and scallops in 1 tbsp butter and 1 tbsp olive oil for 2 minutes per side until golden. Remove and set aside.
Sauté shallot in remaining butter and oil, then add garlic and red pepper flakes for 30 seconds.
Pour in white wine, add clams and mussels, cover and steam for 5–7 minutes until shells open. Discard any closed shells.
Return shrimp and scallops to the pan. Add cooked pasta, lemon zest, juice, and parsley.
Toss together over low heat, adding reserved pasta water as needed to coat the noodles.
Serve immediately in warm bowls.

name: Grilled Sea Bass
instructions: Preheat grill to medium-high heat (375°F–400°F).
Blot fillets dry and make shallow diagonal cuts in the skin. Coat with 1 tbsp olive oil and season with salt, garlic powder, paprika, onion powder, and lemon pepper.
Place fillets on the grill flesh-side down and cook for 7 minutes.
In a saucepan, melt butter with 2 tbsp olive oil. Whisk in minced garlic, white wine, cracked pepper, red pepper flakes, lemon juice, capers, and zest. Keep warm.
Flip fillets and grill for another 7 minutes. Whisk parsley into the butter sauce during the final 2 minutes.
Remove fish when the internal temperature reaches 145°F and spoon sauce over the fillets to serve.

name: Octopus Salad
ingredients: 3 lbs cooked octopus tentacles, cut into bite-sized pieces
instructions: Combine octopus, garlic, celery, parsley, vinegar peppers, and olives in a large mixing bowl.
Pour in olive oil and lemon juice. Add salt, pepper, and oregano, then toss thoroughly.
Cover and refrigerate for at least 4 hours (or overnight) to let flavors marry.
Remove from the refrigerator 20 minutes before serving. Toss again and serve with crusty bread.

name: Stuffed Squid
ingredients: 800g whole squid, cleaned (tubes and tentacles separated)
Small bunch of fresh parsley, minced
Salt and black pepper to taste
instructions: Finely chop the squid tentacles and sauté with minced garlic in 1 tbsp olive oil for 2–3 minutes. Stir in breadcrumbs, olives, capers, parsley, oregano, salt, and pepper.
Spoon the mixture into each squid tube (fill 3/4 full) and seal with a toothpick.
In a large sauté pan, heat remaining olive oil with the whole garlic clove. Once fragrant, add tomatoes (and wine if using).
Remove the whole garlic clove and place the stuffed squid in the pan. Cover and simmer gently for 20–25 minutes.
Remove toothpicks and serve the squid with the tomato sauce.

name: Fried Anchovies
ingredients: 1 lb fresh or thawed anchovies, cleaned (heads and innards removed)
1–2 cups high-smoke point oil for frying
Lemon wedges and chopped parsley for garnish
instructions: Rinse cleaned anchovies and pat completely dry with paper towels.
Combine flour, salt, and pepper in a shallow bowl. Dredge the anchovies in the mixture and shake off any excess.
Heat oil in a skillet to 350°F (175°C).
Fry anchovies in batches for 2–3 minutes, flipping once, until golden brown and crispy.
Drain on paper towels and sprinkle with extra salt while hot.
Garnish with parsley and serve immediately with lemon wedges.

name: Sardines on Toast
ingredients: 1 can (4 oz) sardines in olive oil
Salt and freshly ground black pepper
Optional: red chili flakes and fresh parsley
instructions: Toast the bread until golden brown.
While still hot, lightly rub the raw garlic clove over the surface of the toast.
Place sardines directly onto the toast (or mash with lemon juice and pepper first).
Drizzle a teaspoon of oil from the sardine can over the fish.
Top with a squeeze of lemon juice, zest, salt, pepper, and optional chili flakes or parsley.
Serve immediately.

name: Cajun Shrimp Boil
ingredients: 1 lb smoked sausage (Andouille or Kielbasa), cut into 1-inch rounds
4 ears of sweet corn, husked and snapped in half
1/2 cup Cajun seasoning (e.g., Old Bay or Zatarain’s)
1 whole head of garlic, halved crosswise
instructions: Fill a large stockpot with water. Add Cajun seasoning, lemons (squeeze juice in first), garlic, onion, and bay leaves. Bring to a boil and simmer for 10 minutes.
Add the potatoes and boil for 10–15 minutes until just starting to become tender.
Add the sausage and corn. Continue to boil for another 8–10 minutes.
Add the shrimp and boil for 2–3 minutes until pink and opaque. Do not overcook.
Drain immediately or let soak for 5 minutes for extra spice.
Serve on a platter or paper-lined table. Drizzle with seasoned melted butter and garnish with parsley and lemon wedges.

name: Shrimp and Grits
ingredients: 1 lb large shrimp, peeled and deveined
4 green onions, sliced (whites and greens separated)
Salt and black pepper to taste
instructions: Bring water, milk, and salt to a gentle boil in a saucepan. Whisk in grits. Reduce heat to low, cover, and simmer (5-7 mins for quick grits, 40-45 mins for stone-ground) until creamy. Stir in butter and cheeses.
In a large skillet, fry diced bacon until crispy. Remove bacon and leave 1-2 tbsp of fat in the pan.
Sauté mushrooms and red bell pepper in the bacon fat for 3 minutes.
Add shrimp, garlic, thyme, and the white parts of green onions. Cook for 2-3 minutes until shrimp turn pink.
Pour in chicken broth, heavy cream, lemon juice, and hot sauce. Simmer for 2-3 minutes to thicken sauce. Stir in crispy bacon and green onion tops.
Spoon cheesy grits into bowls and top with the shrimp and gravy mixture.

name: Clam Chowder
ingredients: 1.5 lbs Russet or Yukon Gold potatoes, peeled and diced
Salt and freshly ground black pepper to taste
Garnish: Crispy bacon bits, fresh parsley, or oyster crackers
instructions: Drain canned clams, reserving the juice. Add the juice, chicken broth, diced potatoes, and bay leaves to a stockpot. Simmer until potatoes are tender (15–20 minutes).
In a separate skillet, heat butter and olive oil. Sauté onion, celery, and garlic until soft (4–5 minutes).
Sprinkle flour over the vegetables and stir for 1 minute. Slowly whisk in milk and half-and-half until smooth and thickened.
Combine the creamy vegetable mixture into the stockpot with the potatoes and broth.
Add the reserved clams, salt, pepper, and red wine vinegar. Simmer for 5 minutes (do not boil vigorously).
Remove bay leaves and serve hot with garnishes.

name: Lobster Roll
ingredients: 1 lb cooked lobster meat, chopped into large bite-sized chunks
1 tbsp fresh chives or parsley, finely chopped
Salt and freshly ground black pepper to taste
Optional: Pinch of Old Bay seasoning or lettuce leaves
instructions: In a bowl, whisk together mayonnaise, lemon juice, celery, and herbs. Gently fold in the lobster meat. Season with salt and pepper. Refrigerate for 30 minutes.
Spread softened butter on the outer sides of the split-top buns.
Heat a skillet over medium heat and toast the buns on their sides until golden brown (1–2 minutes per side).
Fill the warm buns with the chilled lobster salad, piling it high. Use a lettuce leaf as a barrier if desired.
Serve immediately with potato chips and a lemon wedge.

name: Crab Cakes
ingredients: 1 lb lump crab meat, picked over for shells
2 tbsp butter or neutral oil (for frying)
Serving: Lemon wedges and tartar sauce
instructions: In a large bowl, whisk egg, mayonnaise, mustard, Worcestershire sauce, and Old Bay.
Gently fold in the crab meat, breadcrumbs, and parsley. Avoid breaking up large crab lumps.
Form the mixture into 6–8 patties (about 1 inch thick).
Refrigerate the patties for 30–60 minutes to help them stay together.
Heat butter/oil in a skillet over medium heat. Fry crab cakes for 4–5 minutes per side until golden brown.
Alternatively, bake at 425°F (220°C) for 12–15 minutes.
Serve immediately with lemon and tartar sauce.

name: Jambalaya
ingredients: 1 lb boneless skinless chicken (thighs or breast), bite-sized
1 lb Andouille sausage, sliced into rounds
1 lb medium shrimp, peeled and deveined
1 can (14.5 oz) crushed or diced tomatoes
Garnish: Green onions and fresh parsley
instructions: Brown chicken and sausage in a large Dutch oven with oil. Remove and set aside.
Sauté onion, bell pepper, and celery in the same pot for 5 minutes. Add garlic and cook for 1 minute.
Stir in rice and Creole seasoning, toasting the rice for 1–2 minutes.
Return chicken and sausage to the pot. Add chicken broth, tomatoes, Worcestershire, and bay leaves. Bring to a boil.
Reduce heat to low, cover, and simmer for 20–25 minutes until rice is tender.
Gently fold in the raw shrimp, cover, and cook for another 5 minutes until pink.
Remove bay leaves and garnish with green onions and parsley.

name: Shrimp Étouffée
ingredients: 2 lbs medium shrimp, peeled and deveined
Garnish: Green onions and fresh parsley
instructions: Melt butter in a heavy skillet. Gradually whisk in flour and stir constantly for 10–15 minutes to make a peanut butter-colored roux.
Add the 'holy trinity' (onion, bell pepper, celery) to the roux and sauté for 5–8 minutes. Add garlic and cook for 1 minute.
Slowly whisk in stock. Add Cajun seasoning, Worcestershire, hot sauce, and bay leaf. Simmer for 15–20 minutes until thickened.
Add shrimp and cook for 3–5 minutes until pink and opaque.
Remove bay leaf. Adjust seasoning and stir in some green onions and parsley.
Serve over warm white rice and garnish with remaining herbs.

name: Fried Catfish
ingredients: 1 tbsp Old Bay or Cajun seasoning
Serving: Lemon wedges and tartar sauce
instructions: In one bowl, whisk eggs, buttermilk, and hot sauce. In another bowl, combine cornmeal, flour, and dry seasonings.
Dip each catfish fillet into the egg mixture, then dredge thoroughly in the cornmeal mixture, pressing gently to adhere.
Heat 2–3 inches of oil in a heavy pot or cast-iron skillet to 350°F (175°C).
Fry fillets 2–3 at a time for 3–5 minutes per side until golden brown and flaky.
Drain on a wire rack or paper towel-lined plate.
Serve hot with lemon wedges, tartar sauce, and traditional sides like hush puppies.

name: Fish Tacos
ingredients: 1 lb white flaky fish (Cod, Halibut, or Tilapia), cut into strips
instructions: Mix sour cream, mayo, chipotle, and lime juice for the crema. Toss cabbage, onion, cilantro, and lime juice for the slaw.
Whisk flour, baking powder, and salt. Add cold beer and whisk into a smooth batter.
Heat oil to 350°F (175°C). Pat fish dry, dip in batter, and fry for 2–3 minutes per side until golden and crispy.
Warm tortillas. Place fried fish on each, top with slaw and crema drizzle.
Serve immediately with extra lime wedges.

name: Ceviche
ingredients: 1.5 lbs fresh firm white fish (Sea Bass, Halibut, or Mahi-Mahi)
1 large red onion, very thinly sliced
1–2 chili peppers (Aji Limo or Habanero), halved or diced
2–3 sprigs fresh cilantro, plus chopped for garnish
Traditional sides: Boiled sweet potato, large-kernel corn (choclo), toasted corn (cancha)
instructions: Cut chilled fish into 1-inch cubes. Place in a glass or ceramic bowl.
Soak sliced onions in ice water for 5–10 minutes, then drain.
Season fish with a generous amount of salt and let sit for 1–2 minutes.
Add lime juice, chili peppers, and cilantro sprigs. The juice should almost cover the fish.
Cover and refrigerate to marinate for 5–15 minutes for Peruvian style (longer if you prefer fish more 'cooked' by acid).
Remove chili halves and cilantro sprigs. Fold in half of the onions.
Serve on chilled plates with lettuce, sweet potato, corn, and remaining onions.

name: Aguachile
ingredients: 1 lb Raw Shrimp (large or medium, peeled and deveined)
instructions: Butterfly the shrimp by slicing along the back almost all the way through.
Place shrimp in a shallow glass dish, pour half the lime juice over them, sprinkle with salt, and marinate in the fridge for 10-15 minutes.
Blend the remaining lime juice, serrano peppers, and cilantro until smooth.
Pour the green sauce over the shrimp. Add red onions and cucumbers.
Chill in the fridge for another 5-10 minutes.
Top with avocado slices and serve immediately with tostadas or crackers.

name: Seafood Gumbo
instructions: Make a dark roux by cooking oil and flour over medium-low heat for 20-30 minutes, stirring constantly until chocolate brown.
Add onions, bell peppers, and celery to the roux and sauté for 5-8 minutes. Add garlic and cook for 2 more minutes.
Slowly whisk in seafood stock and chicken broth. Add Cajun seasoning, thyme, bay leaves, and Worcestershire sauce.
Simmer for 1 to 1.5 hours.
Add okra and simmer for 15 minutes.
Add shrimp and crab meat; cook for 5-10 minutes until shrimp are opaque.
Serve over warm white rice.

name: Blackened Fish
ingredients: 4 (6-oz) Fillets of firm white fish (Redfish, Mahi-mahi, or Snapper)
instructions: Whisk together paprika, salt, garlic powder, onion powder, thyme, oregano, black pepper, and cayenne in a small bowl.
Pat fish fillets completely dry with paper towels.
Dip each fillet into melted butter, then coat generously with the spice mix on both sides.
Heat a large cast-iron skillet over high heat until extremely hot.
Sear fillets for 2-3 minutes per side until the crust is dark brown and fish is opaque.
Serve immediately with lemon wedges.

name: Grilled Salmon
ingredients: 2-4 Salmon Fillets (4-6 oz each, skin-on preferred)
1-2 tbsp Olive Oil or Avocado Oil
instructions: Pat salmon fillets completely dry with paper towels.
Drizzle with oil and season with salt and pepper.
Preheat grill to medium-high (375°F-450°F) and oil the grates.
Place salmon flesh-side down and grill for 3-4 minutes undisturbed.
Flip and grill skin-side down for another 3-5 minutes until internal temperature reaches 125°F-130°F.
Remove from grill, drizzle with melted butter and lemon juice, and rest for 2-3 minutes before serving.

name: Seafood Paella (Latin style)
ingredients: Fresh Cilantro and Lemon wedges (for garnish)
instructions: Sauté onion and bell pepper in oil until soft. Add garlic and tomatoes, cooking until jam-like.
Stir in achiote powder, cumin, and rice. Toast rice for 2 minutes.
Add white wine and hot seafood stock. Distribute rice evenly and do not stir again.
Simmer covered for 15 minutes.
Arrange seafood and peas on top of the rice. Cover tightly.
Cook for another 8-10 minutes until seafood is cooked and shells open.
Rest for 5 minutes, then garnish and serve.

name: Coconut Shrimp
ingredients: 1 lb Jumbo Raw Shrimp (peeled and deveined, tails on)
2 cups Canola or Vegetable Oil (for frying)
instructions: Set up three bowls: 1) flour with salt/pepper, 2) beaten eggs, 3) Panko mixed with shredded coconut.
Dredge shrimp in flour, dip in egg, and press firmly into coconut-panko mixture.
Heat oil in a skillet to 350°F.
Fry shrimp in batches for about 2 minutes per side until golden brown.
Drain on paper towels and serve with sweet chili sauce.

name: Escovitch Fish
ingredients: 2 lbs Whole Red Snapper (cleaned and scaled)
instructions: Rinse fish with lime juice, pat dry, and score sides. Season with salt and pepper.
Fry fish in hot oil for 5-7 minutes per side until very crispy. Place on a platter.
In 2 tbsp of oil, sauté onions, carrots, bell peppers, and Scotch bonnet for 2-3 minutes.
Add pimento berries, thyme, vinegar, and sugar. Simmer for 2-3 minutes.
Pour hot pickled vegetables and liquid over the fried fish.
Let sit for 15-30 minutes before serving.

name: Conch Fritters
instructions: Whisk flour, baking powder, and dry seasonings in a large bowl.
Mix eggs and milk/beer in a separate bowl, then combine with dry ingredients to form a batter.
Fold in minced conch, onion, bell peppers, garlic, and Scotch bonnet.
Heat oil to 350°F-365°F.
Drop rounded spoonfuls of batter into oil and fry for 5-7 minutes until deep golden brown.
Drain on paper towels and serve with Calypso sauce.

name: Moqueca
ingredients: 2 tbsp Olive Oil or Dendê Oil
instructions: Marinate fish in lime juice and salt for 15-20 minutes.
Sauté onion, garlic, and bell peppers in oil until soft.
Add tomato paste, paprika, cumin, and diced tomatoes. Cook until tomatoes break down.
Pour in coconut milk and bring to a gentle simmer.
Nestle fish chunks into the broth. Cover and simmer for 8-12 minutes until fish is opaque.
Stir in cilantro and scallions and serve over white rice.

name: Peruvian Seafood Stew
ingredients: Fresh Cilantro, Parsley, and Lime wedges
instructions: Sauté red onion until translucent. Add garlic and cook for 1 minute.
Stir in ají panca and ají amarillo pastes, then add tomatoes and cook until thick.
Add white wine and simmer for 2 minutes, then add seafood stock and simmer for 10 minutes.
Add mussels first and cook for 3-4 minutes.
Add fish, shrimp, scallops, and squid. Simmer for 3-5 minutes until cooked.
Stir in cilantro and parsley. Serve in deep bowls with lime wedges.

name: Sayadieh
ingredients: 1.5 to 2 lbs (approx. 800g) of firm white fish fillets (Cod, Sea Bass, Snapper, or Halibut)
1 tsp cumin, 1/2 tsp salt, 1/2 tsp black pepper (for fish)
Vegetable or sunflower oil for frying
2 cups Basmati or Jasmine rice (rinsed and drained)
3 to 3.5 cups water or fish stock
1/4 cup pine nuts and/or slivered almonds
instructions: Fry the pine nuts and almonds in a little oil until golden brown. Set aside.
Lightly coat the fish fillets in a mixture of flour, cumin, salt, and pepper. Fry until golden and crispy. Set aside.
In the same pot, cook sliced onions over medium-high heat until they reach a deep dark brown color (the secret to the rice's color).
Add the rinsed rice to the onions and stir for a minute. Add cumin, 7-spice, turmeric, and salt.
Pour in the water or stock. Bring to a boil, then reduce heat to low, cover, and simmer for 15-20 minutes until the liquid is absorbed.
Fluff the rice with a fork. Place the fish on top of the rice and garnish with toasted nuts and fresh parsley. Serve with lemon wedges.

name: Grilled Hammour
ingredients: 1 kg Hammour (4 large fillets or 1 medium whole fish, cleaned and scaled)
Salt and black pepper to taste
Optional: Garlic butter sauce (melted butter, minced garlic, lemon juice)
instructions: Prepare the fish: make diagonal slits if whole, or pat dry if fillets.
Whisk together olive oil, lemon juice, garlic, and all spices to create a marinade.
Rub the marinade over the fish and refrigerate for at least 30 minutes.
Preheat grill to medium-high and oil the grates.
Grill fillets for 4-5 minutes per side, or whole fish for 8-10 minutes per side until opaque and flaky.
Let rest for 3-5 minutes, drizzle with lemon or garlic butter, garnish with parsley and serve.

name: Fish Tagine
ingredients: 1.5 to 2 lbs firm white fish (cod, halibut, sea bass, or tilapia), cut into large chunks
1 tbsp ground cumin, 2 tsp paprika, 1 tsp salt
2 large potatoes, sliced into 1/4 inch rounds
instructions: Create chermoula marinade by mixing cilantro, garlic, cumin, paprika, salt, 3 tbsp olive oil, and lemon juice. Marinate fish for 30 minutes.
In a tagine or heavy skillet, pour 1/4 cup olive oil and arrange onion rings as a base.
Layer carrots then potatoes over the onions. Season with turmeric, ginger, salt, and pepper.
Place tomato slices over potatoes, then arrange the marinated fish on top. Pour remaining marinade over.
Garnish with bell pepper strips, olives, and lemon slices.
Carefully pour water down the side, cover, and simmer over medium-low heat for 45-60 minutes until vegetables are tender.
Rest for 5 minutes before serving.

name: Chermoula Fish
ingredients: 1.5 cups fresh cilantro and 1.5 cups fresh flat-leaf parsley
Zest and juice of 1 large lemon
1 tbsp paprika, 1 tsp ground cumin, 1/4 tsp cayenne pepper
1/2 tsp sea salt and 1/2 tsp black pepper
4 (5-6 oz) firm white fish fillets (halibut, cod, sea bass, or tilapia)
instructions: In a food processor, pulse garlic, cilantro, parsley, and lemon zest until finely chopped.
Add lemon juice, olive oil, paprika, cumin, cayenne, salt, and pepper; process until it forms a thick paste.
Pat fish dry and spread half the chermoula over both sides. Marinate for 15-30 minutes.
Broil on high for 5-7 minutes or pan-sear for 4-5 minutes per side until fish flakes easily.
Serve topped with the remaining fresh chermoula and lemon wedges.

name: Shrimp Tagine
ingredients: 1.5 lbs large shrimp, peeled and deveined
4 large ripe tomatoes (grated) or 14 oz crushed tomatoes
2 tbsp each of fresh flat-leaf parsley and cilantro, chopped
1 tsp sweet paprika, 1/4 tsp ground cumin, 1/4 tsp cayenne pepper
Salt and freshly ground black pepper to taste
3-4 lemon slices and extra cilantro for garnish
instructions: Heat olive oil in a tagine or skillet. Sauté grated tomatoes and garlic for 10-12 minutes until thickened.
Reduce heat and stir in parsley, cilantro, paprika, cumin, cayenne, and bay leaf. Season with salt and pepper.
Arrange shrimp in a single layer in the sauce. Cook for 1 minute, then flip.
Place lemon slices around the edges and add 2 tbsp water for steam. Cover and cook for 8-10 minutes until shrimp are pink.
Serve hot directly from the pot with crusty bread.

name: Egyptian Fish Sayadeya
ingredients: 4 whole Tilapia or Sea Bass, cleaned and scaled (or fillets)
4 cloves garlic (crushed), 1 tsp ground cumin, 1 tsp salt, 1/2 tsp black pepper, juice of 1 lemon (for fish)
3 to 3.5 cups hot water or fish stock
1 tsp cumin, 1 tsp salt, 1/2 tsp sugar (for rice)
instructions: Marinate fish with garlic, cumin, salt, pepper, and lemon juice for 30 minutes.
For the rice: Heat 3 tbsp oil and caramelize chopped onions until they are a very dark brown (chocolate color), but not burnt.
Pour hot water over onions, add salt, cumin, and sugar. Boil for 2-3 minutes.
Add rice, bring to a boil, then cover and simmer on low for 15-20 minutes.
While rice cooks, coat marinated fish in flour and fry in hot oil until golden brown and crispy (5-7 minutes per side).
Plate the brown rice with the fried fish on top, garnished with parsley and lemon.

name: Spicy Fish Stew
ingredients: 1 lb cod, halibut, or tilapia fillets, cut into 1-inch pieces
12 oz jar roasted red bell peppers, drained and chopped
14.5 oz can diced tomatoes with juice
1 tsp minced fresh garlic, 1/4 tsp red pepper flakes
1/2 tsp fresh lemon zest, 2 tsp fresh lemon juice
1 tbsp olive oil, salt and pepper to taste
instructions: Sauté red onion in olive oil for 3-4 minutes until soft.
Stir in roasted peppers, diced tomatoes, garlic, and red pepper flakes. Simmer for 10 minutes.
Add lemon zest, juice, and cilantro to the tomato mixture.
Gently fold in fish pieces and simmer for 5-7 minutes until opaque and flaky.
Season with salt and pepper and serve with crusty bread or over rice.

name: Prawn Peri-Peri
ingredients: 1 Red bell pepper, charred and peeled
3 tbsp Red wine vinegar or lemon juice
1 tbsp Smoked paprika, 1 tsp dried oregano
instructions: Blend charred pepper, chilies, garlic, olive oil, vinegar, paprika, oregano, salt, and pepper to create Peri-Peri sauce.
Marinate prawns in half the sauce for 1-4 hours in the refrigerator.
Grill for 2-3 minutes per side or pan-sear for 2 minutes per side until opaque and charred.
Baste with remaining sauce during the last minute of cooking. Serve with savory rice or baguette.

name: Grilled Tilapia
ingredients: 1 large Lemon (half juiced, half in wedges)
1 tsp salt, 1/2 tsp black pepper, 1/2 tsp paprika
instructions: Preheat grill to medium-high (400°F-450°F) and oil the grates.
Whisk olive oil, lemon juice, garlic, salt, pepper, and paprika together.
Pat fillets dry and brush both sides with the mixture.
Grill for 3-4 minutes per side until opaque and flakes easily with a fork.
Garnish with parsley and serve with lemon wedges.

name: Fish Couscous
ingredients: 1.5 lbs (675g) firm white fish, cut into 2-inch cubes
1 medium onion, 1 red bell pepper, 1 medium zucchini (all diced)
2 tbsp tomato paste, 2 tbsp lemon juice, 1 tsp harissa sauce
19 oz can chickpeas, rinsed and drained
3/4 cup fresh flat-leaf parsley, finely chopped
1 tsp ground cumin, 1/2 tsp paprika, 1/2 tsp ground turmeric
instructions: Sear fish cubes in half the olive oil for 2 minutes per side; remove and set aside.
Sauté onion, bell pepper, and dry spices in remaining oil until soft (5 minutes).
Stir in zucchini, garlic, tomato paste, lemon juice, and harissa; cook for 2 minutes.
Add broth and chickpeas; bring to a boil and simmer for 2 minutes.
Stir in dry couscous and seared fish. Cover tightly and remove from heat.
Let sit for 5-7 minutes until liquid is absorbed and fish is cooked through.
Fluff with a fork, stir in parsley, and serve with lemon wedges.

name: Sardine Balls
ingredients: 1 lb (500g) fresh sardines (cleaned, deboned, minced) or 2-3 cans of sardines in olive oil (drained)
1/2 cup cooked white rice or 1/4 cup breadcrumbs
1/2 tsp harissa paste or pinch of cayenne pepper (optional)

Tomato Sauce: 2 tbsp olive oil, 1 small onion (chopped), 3 cloves garlic (minced), 1 can (15 oz) crushed tomatoes, 1 tsp cumin, 1 tsp paprika, 1/2 tsp salt, pinch of sugar, 1/2 cup water, 1 bay leaf, 1/2 cup green olives (optional)
instructions: Prepare the Sardine Mixture: Pulse fresh sardines in a food processor or mash canned sardines. Combine with rice/breadcrumbs, garlic, herbs, spices, lemon juice, and olive oil.
Form the Balls: Roll the mixture into walnut-sized balls (approx. 1.5 inches).
Make the Sauce: Sauté onion and garlic in olive oil. Stir in tomatoes, spices, sugar, water, and bay leaf. Simmer for 10 minutes.
Cook the Sardine Balls: Gently add balls to the sauce in a single layer. Cover and simmer on low for 15-20 minutes. Add olives in the last 5 minutes if using.
Garnish with lemon juice and extra herbs before serving.

name: Seafood Harira
ingredients: 1/2 lb (250g) firm white fish (cod, monkfish, or sea bass), cut into chunks
1/2 cup dried green or brown lentils, rinsed
1 can (15 oz) cooked chickpeas, drained and rinsed
1 can (14 oz) crushed tomatoes or 4 large fresh tomatoes (grated)
2 stalks celery with leaves, finely chopped
Thickener: 3 tbsp flour mixed with 1/2 cup water
instructions: Sauté onion and celery in olive oil for 5 minutes.
Add ginger, turmeric, cinnamon, salt, pepper, and half of the herbs. Cook for 1-2 minutes.
Add tomatoes, tomato paste, lentils, and fish stock. Simmer for 30 minutes until lentils are tender.
Stir in chickpeas and vermicelli (if using). Cook for 5-10 minutes.
Add white fish, shrimp, and other seafood. Simmer for 3-5 minutes until cooked.
Slowly pour in the flour-water slurry while stirring. Simmer for 2-3 minutes to thicken.
Stir in remaining herbs and lemon juice. Serve with lemon wedges and dates.

name: Fried Red Snapper
ingredients: 2 lbs Red Snapper (whole cleaned or large fillets)
Marinade: 1/4 cup fresh lemon juice, 4 cloves garlic (minced), 1 tsp ground cumin, 1 tsp ground coriander, 1/2 tsp turmeric, 1/2 tsp paprika or red pepper flakes, salt and black pepper
Coating: 1 cup all-purpose flour, 1/4 cup cornmeal (optional), 1/2 tsp cumin, 1/2 tsp salt
instructions: Prepare the Fish: Pat dry. If whole, make 2-3 diagonal slits on each side.
Marinate: Mix marinade ingredients and rub over fish. Refrigerate for 30-60 minutes.
Dredge: Coat fish thoroughly in the seasoned flour mixture, shaking off excess.
Fry: Heat oil to 350°F (175°C). Fry for 5-7 minutes per side (whole) or 3-4 minutes per side (fillets) until golden and crispy.
Drain on a wire rack or paper towels. Serve with tahini sauce and lemon wedges.

name: Fish Pepper Soup
ingredients: 2 lbs (1 kg) fresh fish (catfish, tilapia, croaker, or snapper), cut into steaks
1-2 tbsp Pepper Soup Spice Mix (Ehuru, Uda, Uziza blend)
1-2 Scotch Bonnet or Habanero peppers, chopped
Handful of Scent Leaves or Utazi Leaves (or basil/parsley)
instructions: Prepare Fish: Wash fish with lime/lemon. For catfish, pour hot water over it for 30 seconds to remove slime, then rinse.
Start Broth: Place fish, water, onions, ginger, garlic, and peppers in a large pot.
Season: Add pepper soup spice, crayfish, seasoning cubes, and salt.
Simmer: Bring to a boil, then reduce heat. Simmer covered for 15-20 minutes. Jiggle the pot instead of stirring to avoid breaking the fish.
Final Touch: Adjust seasoning, add scent leaves, and simmer for 2-3 minutes. Serve hot.

name: Coconut Fish Curry
ingredients: 1.5 lbs (700g) firm white fish fillets, cut into large chunks
1 dried black lime (Loomi), pierced, or 1 tbsp tamarind paste
Garnish: Fresh cilantro, parsley, lime wedges
instructions: Marinate the Fish: Toss fish with turmeric, lemon juice, salt, and pepper. Sit for 15 minutes.
Sauté Aromatics: Heat oil/ghee. Sauté onions until soft. Add garlic, ginger, and chili; cook for 2 minutes.
Toast Spices: Add Baharat and dried lime (or tamarind). Stir for 1 minute. Add tomato paste and tomatoes; cook until softened.
Simmer Base: Add coconut milk and stock. Simmer and thicken for 10 minutes.
Poach Fish: Nestle fish into sauce. Cover and cook for 5-7 minutes until opaque. Do not overstir.
Finish: Remove dried lime. Stir in fresh herbs and serve with rice or chapati.

name: Seafood Pizza
ingredients: Thai Green Curry Pesto: 2 tbsp green curry paste, 1/2 cup cilantro, 1/4 cup Thai basil, 1 clove garlic, 1 tbsp lime juice, 2 tbsp olive oil
1/2 lb (225g) medium shrimp, peeled and deveined
1/4 lb (115g) bay scallops or calamari rings
1 tbsp butter and 1 clove garlic for sautéing
Toppings: 1/4 red onion (sliced), 1/2 red bell pepper (julienned), 1/4 cup matchstick carrots
Garnish: 2 tbsp crushed roasted peanuts, fresh cilantro, lime wedges
instructions: Preheat oven to 450°F (230°C).
Make Pesto: Blend green curry paste, cilantro, basil, garlic, lime juice, and olive oil.
Flash-Sear Seafood: Sauté shrimp and scallops in butter and garlic for 60-90 seconds. Drain.
Assemble: Roll out dough. Spread pesto, then mozzarella. Add seafood and vegetables. Top with feta.
Bake: Cook for 10-12 minutes until crust is golden and cheese bubbles.
Garnish: Sprinkle with peanuts and cilantro. Serve with lime wedges.

name: Seafood Pasta Alfredo
ingredients: 1 lb large shrimp, peeled and deveined
1/2 lb to 1 lb sea scallops, patted dry
Salt and white pepper to taste
instructions: Cook Pasta: Boil fettuccine until al dente. Reserve 1/2 cup pasta water, then drain.
Sear Seafood: Sauté seasoned shrimp (2-3 mins) and scallops (90 secs per side) in olive oil. Set aside.
Sauté Aromatics: Melt butter, sauté shallots and garlic for 1-2 minutes.
Deglaze: Add white wine (if using) and reduce by half.
Make Sauce: Simmer heavy cream, then whisk in Parmesan and nutmeg until thickened (3-5 mins).
Combine: Toss pasta and seafood into the sauce. Use reserved pasta water to adjust thickness if needed.
Garnish with parsley and extra Parmesan before serving.

name: Garlic Butter Shrimp
ingredients: 1 lb large raw shrimp, peeled and deveined
instructions: Prep: Pat shrimp dry and season with salt, pepper, and paprika.
Melt Butter: Heat butter in a large skillet over medium-high heat.
Sauté Garlic: Cook garlic for 30-60 seconds until fragrant.
Cook Shrimp: Add shrimp in a single layer. Cook 2-3 minutes per side until pink and opaque.
Finish: Stir in lemon juice and red pepper flakes. Toss to coat.
Garnish with parsley and serve immediately.

name: Honey Garlic Salmon
ingredients: Salt and black pepper to taste
instructions: Prep: Pat salmon dry and season with salt and pepper.
Sauce: Whisk honey, garlic, soy sauce, and lemon juice in a bowl.
Sear: Heat olive oil. Cook salmon flesh-side down for 3-4 minutes until golden. Flip fillets.
Glaze: Reduce heat. Add butter and honey garlic sauce. Baste salmon with bubbling sauce for 2-3 minutes until cooked through.
Serve immediately with extra glaze and garnishes.

name: Chili Garlic Crab
ingredients: 3 large crabs (Blue or Mud), cleaned and halved/quartered
2-3 tbsp fish sauce or soy sauce
Slurry: 1 tbsp cornstarch in 2 tbsp water
instructions: Sauté Aromatics: Fry garlic, ginger, and onion in oil until golden and soft.
Cook Crab: Add crab and water. Cover and steam for 5-7 minutes until shells turn red.
Add Sauce: Stir in ketchup, sweet chili sauce, fish sauce, and sugar. Toss to coat and simmer for 2-3 minutes.
Thicken: Stir in cornstarch slurry until the sauce glazes.
Add Egg: Slowly pour in beaten egg, stirring gently for 30 seconds to create ribbons.
Garnish and serve hot with white rice or mantou buns.

name: Seafood Stir-Fry
ingredients: 1 lb mixed seafood (shrimp, scallops, and squid or crab meat)
3-4 green onions, sliced (whites and greens separated)
1 red or green bell pepper, sliced into strips
1 cup snap peas or green beans, trimmed
1 tbsp cornstarch mixed with 1 tbsp water (slurry)
instructions: In a small bowl, whisk together soy sauce, oyster sauce, sesame oil, sugar, and chicken stock.
Heat 1 tbsp oil in a wok over high heat. Stir-fry seafood for 2-3 minutes until opaque; remove and set aside.
Add remaining oil to the pan. Sauté ginger, garlic, and white parts of green onions for 30 seconds.
Add bell peppers, snap peas, and mushrooms. Stir-fry for 3-4 minutes until tender-crisp.
Return seafood to the pan and pour in the sauce mixture.
Stir in the cornstarch slurry and cook for 1 minute until the sauce thickens and becomes glossy.
Toss in green parts of onions and serve immediately over rice.

name: Seafood Fried Rice
ingredients: 6 oz raw shrimp, peeled and deveined
6 oz scallops or firm white fish chunks
3 cups cooked jasmine rice (preferably day-old and cold)
1 cup mixed vegetables (peas, carrots, and corn)
Salt and white pepper to taste
instructions: Heat 1 tbsp oil in a wok over medium-high heat. Stir-fry shrimp and scallops for 2-3 minutes until cooked; remove and set aside.
Scramble the eggs in the same pan until just set; remove and set aside.
Add remaining oil and sauté garlic and mixed vegetables for 2 minutes.
Turn heat to high, add cold rice, and stir-fry for 3 minutes, breaking up any clumps.
Add seafood and eggs back to the pan. Pour in soy sauce, oyster sauce, and sesame oil.
Toss everything together for 1-2 minutes until steaming hot.
Season with salt and white pepper. Garnish with green onions.

name: Mixed Seafood Grill
ingredients: 1 lb firm white fish (halibut, swordfish, or cod), cut into 1.5-inch pieces
1 lb extra-large shrimp, peeled and deveined (tails on)
Lemon wedges and fresh parsley for garnish
instructions: Preheat a gas or charcoal grill to medium-high heat. If using a grill basket, preheat it on the grill.
In a large bowl, combine melted butter, lemon-pepper seasoning, fennel seeds, and salt.
Add fish and shrimp to the bowl and toss gently to coat evenly.
Place seafood in the preheated grill basket (or on skewers).
Grill for 5-10 minutes, shaking the basket occasionally, until the fish flakes easily and shrimp are opaque.
Serve immediately with lemon wedges and fresh parsley.
serving_size: 6 servings (approx. 5-6 oz cooked seafood per person)

name: Seafood Chowder
ingredients: 3 oz salt pork or thick-cut bacon, finely diced
1/2 lb raw shrimp, peeled and deveined
1/2 lb firm white fish chunks (cod or haddock)
Salt and black pepper to taste
A pinch of Old Bay seasoning
Fresh parsley or chives for garnish
instructions: Fry salt pork or bacon in a large pot until crisp; remove bits and set aside for garnish.
Sauté onions in the rendered fat until soft (about 5 minutes).
Add potatoes and stock. Simmer, covered, for 10-12 minutes until potatoes are tender.
Add fish, shrimp, and scallops. Simmer for 5-7 minutes until cooked through.
Stir in milk and heavy cream. Heat slowly until steaming, but do not let it boil.
Season with salt, pepper, and Old Bay.
Ladle into bowls and garnish with reserved bacon bits and fresh herbs.

name: Seafood Platter
ingredients: 1 lb jumbo shrimp, cooked and chilled
2-3 cooked lobsters, split or tails only
1 lb crab legs or cracked crab
12 raw clams on the half shell (optional)
Lemon wedges and fresh dill for garnish
instructions: Fill a large platter or tiered stand with a thick layer of crushed ice.
Arrange the largest items like lobster and crab clusters on the ice first.
Nestle shucked oysters and clams into the ice so they remain level.
Arrange chilled shrimp in the remaining gaps.
Tuck lemon wedges and fresh dill sprigs around the seafood.
Place small bowls of sauces on the platter or serve alongside.
Keep refrigerated until the moment of serving.
serving_size: 6 people (Main course: 0.5 - 0.75 lb total seafood per person)

name: Baked Salmon with Herbs
ingredients: 1 tbsp fresh chives or basil, chopped
Salt and black pepper to taste
instructions: Preheat oven to 400°F (200°C) and line a baking sheet with parchment paper.
In a small bowl, whisk together olive oil, garlic, lemon zest, and chopped herbs.
Pat salmon dry and place on the baking sheet. Season with salt and pepper.
Spoon the herb mixture over the fillets and spread evenly.
Place lemon slices on top of each fillet.
Bake for 12-15 minutes until salmon is opaque and flakes easily.
Let rest for 2-3 minutes before serving.

name: Lemon Butter Fish
ingredients: 4 white fish fillets (approx. 6 oz each, e.g., cod or halibut)
Salt and black pepper to taste
instructions: Pat fillets dry and season with salt, pepper, garlic powder, onion powder, and paprika. Lightly dust with flour if desired.
Heat a skillet over medium-high heat and melt 2-3 tbsp of butter.
Sear fish for 2-3 minutes per side until golden brown.
Add remaining butter, lemon zest, and half the lemon juice to the pan.
Spoon the melting lemon butter over the fish for the final 2 minutes of cooking.
Remove from heat, add remaining lemon juice, and garnish with parsley.

name: Seafood Kebabs
ingredients: 1 lb firm white fish (halibut or swordfish), cut into 1-inch chunks
12-16 large raw shrimp, peeled and deveined
1 red bell pepper, 1 zucchini, and 1 red onion (all cut for skewering)
1 tsp dried oregano, salt, and pepper
instructions: Soak wooden skewers in water for 30 minutes.
Whisk olive oil, lemon juice, garlic, and seasonings to make a marinade.
Toss seafood and vegetables with half the marinade; let sit for 15-20 minutes.
Thread seafood, vegetables, and folded lemon slices onto skewers.
Grill over medium-high heat for 8-10 minutes, turning once.
Baste with the remaining marinade during the last 2 minutes of grilling.
Serve hot over rice or salad.

name: Seafood Salad
ingredients: 1 lb imitation crab, chopped into bite-sized pieces
1/4 cup finely diced red or green onion
Salt and black pepper to taste
instructions: In a large bowl, combine the chopped imitation crab, shrimp, celery, and onions.
In a small bowl, whisk together mayonnaise, lemon juice, Old Bay, and dill.
Pour the dressing over the seafood mixture and fold gently until evenly coated.
Season with salt and pepper to taste.
Cover and refrigerate for at least 30-60 minutes before serving to allow flavors to meld.
Serve chilled on lettuce or in rolls.

name: Seafood Spring Rolls
ingredients: 1/2 cup shredded carrots and 1/2 cup shredded cabbage
1 tsp soy sauce, 1/2 tsp sesame oil, salt, and white pepper
1 tbsp cornstarch mixed with 1 tbsp water (slurry)
instructions: Combine chopped seafood, vegetables, aromatics, and seasonings in a large bowl.
Place a wrapper in a diamond shape and put 2 tbsp of filling on the lower third.
Fold the bottom corner over, fold in the sides, and roll up tightly.
Seal the final corner with a dab of cornstarch slurry.
Heat 2 inches of oil to 350°F (175°C).
Fry rolls in batches for 3-5 minutes until golden brown and crisp.
Drain on paper towels and serve hot with sweet chili sauce.
serving_size: Makes 12-15 rolls (2-3 per person as appetizer)

name: Chocolate Mug Cake
instructions: In a large microwave-safe mug (at least 12 oz), whisk together the flour, sugar, and cocoa powder until no clumps remain.
Add the egg and whisk thoroughly into the dry mixture.
Stir in the milk and vegetable oil until the batter is smooth and well-combined.
Microwave on high for 1 minute 30 seconds to 2 minutes. The cake is done when it has risen and the top looks set.
Let the cake rest for 1-2 minutes before serving.

name: Banana Ice Cream
instructions: Slice frozen bananas into small pieces.
Place frozen banana slices in a high-powered blender or food processor and pulse until they look like small crumbs.
Add honey and peanut butter if using.
Process on high until the mixture becomes smooth and reaches a soft-serve consistency, scraping down the sides as needed.
Serve immediately for soft-serve or freeze for 1-2 hours for a firmer, scoopable texture.

name: Chocolate Mousse
instructions: Melt the dark chocolate in a heatproof bowl over simmering water or in the microwave in 30-second bursts. Let it cool slightly.
Whisk the egg yolks into the melted chocolate one at a time until smooth.
In a separate bowl, whip the heavy cream until stiff peaks form. Set aside.
In another clean bowl, beat the egg whites while gradually adding the sugar until stiff, glossy peaks form.
Gently fold the whipped cream into the chocolate mixture.
Carefully fold in the beaten egg whites in batches until no white streaks remain, being careful not to deflate the mixture.
Spoon into serving dishes and refrigerate for at least 2-4 hours before serving.

name: Peanut Butter Cookies
instructions: Preheat oven to 350°F (175°C). Line a baking sheet with parchment paper.
In a medium bowl, stir together the peanut butter, sugar, egg, and vanilla extract until smooth.
Roll the dough into 1-inch balls and place them 2 inches apart on the baking sheet.
Flatten each ball with a fork, making a criss-cross pattern.
Bake for 8-10 minutes until the edges are lightly browned.
Let the cookies cool on the baking sheet for 5 minutes before transferring to a wire rack.

name: Brownies
instructions: Preheat oven to 350°F (175°C). Grease an 8x8 inch square baking pan.
Melt the butter and stir in the sugar and eggs until well combined.
Whisk in the cocoa powder, flour, and salt until the batter is smooth.
Spread the batter evenly into the prepared pan.
Bake for 25-30 minutes, or until a toothpick inserted into the center comes out with a few moist crumbs.
Allow the brownies to cool completely in the pan before cutting into squares.

name: Rice Krispie Treats
instructions: In a large saucepan, melt the butter over low heat.
Add the marshmallows and stir until completely melted and smooth. Remove from heat.
Add the rice cereal and stir gently until all cereal is evenly coated with the marshmallow mixture.
Press the mixture evenly into a greased 13x9 inch pan using a buttered spatula or wax paper.
Let the treats cool completely at room temperature before cutting into squares.

name: Strawberry Shortcake
instructions: Preheat oven to 425°F (220°C) and grease a baking sheet.
In a large bowl, whisk together the flour, baking powder, and sugar.
Cut the cold, cubed butter into the flour mixture using a pastry cutter or fork until it resembles coarse crumbs.
Stir in the milk until the dough just comes together.
Drop large spoonfuls of dough onto the baking sheet and bake for 12-15 minutes until golden brown.
Allow shortcakes to cool slightly, then split them in half.
Assemble by layering the bottom half with sliced strawberries and whipped cream, then replacing the top half.

name: Creme Brulee
instructions: Preheat oven to 325°F (163°C). Place 6 ramekins in a large baking dish.
Heat the heavy cream in a saucepan over medium heat until it begins to simmer. Stir in the vanilla extract and remove from heat.
In a bowl, whisk the egg yolks and 1/2 cup sugar until pale and well blended.
Slowly whisk a small amount of the hot cream into the egg mixture to temper them, then gradually add the remaining cream while whisking constantly.
Divide the custard among the ramekins. Pour hot water into the baking dish until it reaches halfway up the sides of the ramekins.
Bake for 30-35 minutes until the edges are set but the center still jiggles slightly.
Cool to room temperature, then refrigerate for at least 4 hours.
Before serving, sprinkle the extra sugar over the chilled custards and caramelize with a kitchen torch or under the broiler until golden brown.

name: Tiramisu
instructions: In a medium bowl, whisk the egg yolks and sugar until thick and pale. Fold in the mascarpone cheese until smooth.
In a separate clean bowl, whisk the egg whites until stiff peaks form. Gently fold them into the mascarpone mixture.
Quickly dip 10 ladyfingers one by one into the cooled espresso and layer them in the bottom of a small rectangular dish.
Spread half of the mascarpone cream over the ladyfingers.
Repeat with a second layer of dipped ladyfingers and the remaining cream.
Cover and refrigerate for at least 4-6 hours (overnight is best).
Just before serving, dust the top generously with cocoa powder.

name: Lemon Bars
instructions: Preheat oven to 350°F (175°C). Grease or line an 8x8 inch square baking pan.
Mix the softened butter, 1 cup flour, and 1/4 cup powdered sugar until crumbly. Press into the bottom of the pan.
Bake the crust for 15-20 minutes until lightly golden.
Whisk together the eggs, 1 cup sugar, lemon juice, and 2 tbsp flour until smooth.
Pour the lemon mixture over the hot crust and return to the oven.
Bake for 18-22 minutes until the filling is set.
Cool completely at room temperature, then refrigerate for at least 2 hours before slicing into bars.

name: Classic Cheesecake
instructions: Preheat oven to 325°F (160°C) and grease a 7-inch springform pan.
Mix crushed graham crackers with melted butter and press firmly into the bottom of the pan.
In a large bowl, beat softened cream cheese and sugar until smooth and creamy.
Add eggs one at a time, mixing on low speed just until combined. Stir in vanilla extract.
Pour the cream cheese mixture over the prepared crust.
Bake for 45-55 minutes until the edges are set but the center still jiggles slightly.
Turn off the oven, crack the door open, and let the cheesecake cool inside for 1 hour to prevent cracking.
Refrigerate for at least 6 hours or overnight before serving.

name: Apple Crumble
instructions: Preheat oven to 375°F (190°C).
In a large bowl, toss the sliced apples with a portion of the sugar and cinnamon. Spread them evenly into a baking dish.
In another bowl, combine the rolled oats, flour, and remaining sugar.
Cut the cold butter into small pieces and work it into the oat mixture using your fingers or a pastry cutter until it resembles coarse crumbs.
Sprinkle the crumble mixture evenly over the apples.
Bake for 40-45 minutes until the topping is golden brown and the apples are tender.
Serve warm, ideally with a scoop of vanilla ice cream.

name: Panna Cotta
instructions: In a small bowl, sprinkle the gelatin over 2 tablespoons of cold water. Let it sit for 5-10 minutes to bloom.
In a saucepan, combine the heavy cream and sugar. Heat over medium-low, stirring constantly, until the sugar is dissolved (do not let it boil).
Remove the saucepan from the heat and stir in the bloomed gelatin and vanilla extract until completely dissolved.
Pour the mixture into ramekins or small serving bowls.
Refrigerate for at least 4-6 hours, or until the panna cotta is firm and set.
Serve chilled, optionally topped with fresh berries or fruit sauce.

name: Chocolate Truffles
instructions: Finely chop the dark chocolate and place it in a heat-proof bowl with the butter.
In a small saucepan, heat the heavy cream over medium heat until it just begins to simmer.
Pour the hot cream over the chocolate and butter. Let it sit for 5 minutes undisturbed.
Gently stir the mixture until it is completely smooth and glossy.
Cover the bowl and refrigerate for 2-3 hours until the mixture is firm enough to handle.
Use a spoon or small scoop to portion the ganache and roll quickly between your palms to form 1-inch balls.
Roll each truffle in cocoa powder until evenly coated. Store in the refrigerator.

name: Vanilla Pudding
instructions: In a medium saucepan, whisk together the sugar and cornstarch.
Gradually whisk in the milk, ensuring there are no lumps.
Place the saucepan over medium heat and cook, stirring constantly, until the mixture thickens and begins to bubble.
Once thickened, remove from the heat and stir in the butter and vanilla extract until smooth.
Pour the pudding into individual serving bowls.
To prevent a skin from forming, press plastic wrap directly onto the surface of the pudding.
Refrigerate for at least 2 hours before serving.

name: Banana Bread
instructions: Preheat oven to 350°F (175°C) and grease an 8x4-inch loaf pan.
In a large bowl, mash the ripe bananas with a fork until smooth.
Stir the melted butter into the mashed bananas. Add the sugar, beaten egg, vanilla, and baking soda. Mix well.
Gently stir in the flour until just combined. Do not overmix.
Pour the batter into the prepared loaf pan.
Bake for 50-60 minutes, or until a toothpick inserted into the center comes out clean.
Let the bread cool in the pan for 10 minutes, then transfer to a wire rack to cool completely.

name: Fruit Parfait
instructions: Wash and slice the fresh fruits into bite-sized pieces.
In two tall glasses or jars, start by spooning a layer of yogurt (about 1/2 cup) into the bottom of each.
Add a layer of mixed fruits, followed by a layer of granola.
Repeat the layers with the remaining yogurt, fruits, and granola.
Drizzle 1 tablespoon of honey over the top of each parfait just before serving for extra sweetness.

name: Coconut Macaroons
instructions: Preheat oven to 325°F (165°C) and line a baking sheet with parchment paper.
In a large bowl, stir together the shredded coconut, sweetened condensed milk, and vanilla extract until well combined.
In a separate clean bowl, beat the egg whites with a pinch of salt until stiff peaks form.
Gently fold the whipped egg whites into the coconut mixture until just combined.
Using a tablespoon or small cookie scoop, drop rounded mounds of the mixture onto the prepared baking sheet.
Bake for 20-25 minutes until the edges are golden brown.
Allow the macaroons to cool completely on the baking sheet before moving them.

name: Chia Pudding
