instructions: In a mason jar or bowl, combine the chia seeds, coconut milk, honey, and vanilla extract.
Whisk or stir the mixture thoroughly to ensure the chia seeds are evenly distributed.
Let the mixture sit for 10 minutes, then stir again to break up any clumps of seeds.
Cover and refrigerate for at least 2 hours, or preferably overnight, until it reaches a thick pudding consistency.
Serve chilled, optionally topped with fresh fruit or nuts.

name: Affogato
instructions: Place one large scoop of vanilla ice cream or gelato into a small chilled glass or bowl.
Brew a fresh, hot shot of espresso (about 1 ounce).
Slowly pour the hot espresso directly over the ice cream.
Serve immediately with a small spoon before the ice cream melts completely.

name: Churros
instructions: Prepare the coating by whisking together 1/2 cup sugar and cinnamon in a shallow bowl. Set aside.
In a small saucepan over medium heat, combine water, 2 tbsp sugar, salt, and 2 tbsp of oil. Bring to a boil.
Remove from heat and immediately stir in the flour until the mixture forms a smooth, thick ball of dough. Let it cool for 5 minutes.
Heat oil in a deep pot to 375°F (190°C).
Transfer the dough into a piping bag fitted with a large star tip.
Pipe 4- to 6-inch strips of dough directly into the hot oil, using scissors to snip the end.
Fry 3–4 churros at a time for 2–3 minutes per side until golden brown and crisp.
Drain on paper towels for a few seconds, then roll in the cinnamon-sugar mixture while still hot.

name: Glazed Donuts
instructions: In a small bowl, whisk together warm milk, yeast, and 1 tsp of sugar. Let sit for 5-10 minutes until foamy.
In a large bowl, whisk together the softened butter, egg, and the remaining sugar. Stir in the yeast mixture.
Gradually stir the flour and salt into the wet ingredients until a shaggy dough forms.
Knead the dough on a floured surface for about 5 minutes until smooth and elastic.
Place in an oiled bowl, cover, and let rise in a warm spot until doubled (about 1-1.5 hours).
Punch down the dough and roll it out to 1/2-inch thickness. Cut into donut shapes.
Place on parchment paper, cover, and let rise again for 30-60 minutes until puffy.
Heat oil to 350°F (175°C) and fry donuts for about 1 minute per side until golden.
Whisk powdered sugar with a little milk and vanilla to make a glaze. Dip warm donuts into the glaze.

name: Mochi
ingredients: 1/2 cup filling of choice (red bean paste, ice cream)
instructions: In a microwave-safe bowl, whisk together the glutinous rice flour and sugar.
Gradually add the water and whisk until the batter is smooth and lump-free.
Cover the bowl loosely with plastic wrap and microwave on high for 2 minutes.
Stir the mixture with a wet spatula; it will be sticky and partially translucent.
Cover again and microwave for another 1 to 2 minutes until fully translucent and very sticky.
Generously dust a work surface with cornstarch.
Transfer the hot mochi onto the starch and dust the top. Flatten and cut into 12 pieces.
While still warm, place a small amount of filling in the center of each piece and pinch the edges to seal.

name: S'mores Bars
instructions: Preheat oven to 350°F (175°C) and grease an 8x8-inch baking pan.
In a bowl, combine graham cracker crumbs and melted butter. Press half of the mixture firmly into the bottom of the pan.
Evenly spread the chocolate chips over the graham cracker base.
Spread the mini marshmallows over the chocolate chips.
Sprinkle the remaining graham cracker mixture over the marshmallows, pressing down lightly.
Bake for 25–30 minutes or until the top is lightly golden and marshmallows are melty.
Allow the bars to cool completely in the pan before slicing into 16 squares.

name: Chocolate Fondue
instructions: In a small saucepan over low heat, combine the chopped chocolate, butter, and heavy cream.
Stir constantly until the chocolate is completely melted and the mixture is smooth and glossy.
Be careful not to let the mixture boil to avoid seizing the chocolate.
Remove from heat and stir in any optional flavorings like vanilla extract.
Transfer to a fondue pot or a warm bowl.
Serve immediately with your choice of dippers such as fruit, marshmallows, and cake cubes.

name: Nutella Crepes
instructions: In a blender, combine flour, melted butter, eggs, sugar, and milk. Blend until smooth.
Let the batter rest in the refrigerator for at least 30 minutes.
Heat a non-stick skillet over medium-low heat and lightly grease with butter.
Pour about 1/4 cup of batter into the pan, tilting it to spread the batter into a thin circle.
Cook for 1-2 minutes until the edges brown, then flip and cook for another 30 seconds.
Remove the crepe and spread a tablespoon of Nutella over half of it while still warm.
Fold the crepe into quarters or roll it up and serve immediately.

name: Caramel Flan
instructions: Preheat oven to 350°F (175°C).
In a saucepan over medium heat, melt the 1 cup of sugar until it turns into a golden-brown caramel. Pour immediately into an 8-inch round baking dish, coating the bottom.
In a blender, combine evaporated milk, condensed milk, eggs, and vanilla. Blend until smooth.
Pour the custard mixture over the hardened caramel in the baking dish.
Place the baking dish inside a larger roasting pan and fill the larger pan with hot water until it reaches halfway up the side of the flan dish.
Bake for 60-75 minutes until set but still slightly jiggly in the center.
Remove from the water bath and let cool, then refrigerate for at least 4 hours or overnight.
To serve, run a knife around the edge and invert onto a rimmed plate.

name: Blueberry Muffins
instructions: Preheat oven to 400°F (200°C) and grease a 12-cup muffin tin or line with paper liners.
In a large bowl, whisk together flour, sugar, baking powder, and salt.
In another bowl, whisk together the vegetable oil, egg, and milk.
Pour the wet ingredients into the dry ingredients and stir until just combined (batter will be thick).
Gently fold in the fresh blueberries.
Spoon the batter into the muffin cups, filling them nearly to the top.
Bake for 20-25 minutes or until a toothpick inserted in the center comes out clean.
Cool in the tin for 5 minutes before transferring to a wire rack.

name: No-Bake Cheesecake
instructions: In a bowl, mix graham cracker crumbs, melted butter, and a bit of the powdered sugar until moistened.
Press the mixture firmly into the bottom of a 9-inch springform pan and refrigerate while preparing the filling.
In a large bowl, beat the softened cream cheese with the remaining powdered sugar and vanilla until smooth.
Gently fold the whipped cream into the cream cheese mixture until light and fluffy.
Pour the filling over the prepared crust and smooth the top with a spatula.
Cover and refrigerate for at least 4-6 hours (ideally overnight) until firm.
Serve chilled, optionally topped with fruit or chocolate sauce.

name: Sticky Toffee Pudding
ingredients: Toffee sauce: butter, brown sugar, heavy cream
instructions: Preheat oven to 350°F (175°C) and grease a 9-inch square baking dish.
Place chopped dates in a bowl and pour boiling water over them. Stir in baking soda and let sit for 15-20 minutes.
In a large bowl, cream together butter and sugar until light and fluffy. Beat in the eggs one at a time, then add vanilla.
Gently fold in the flour and a pinch of salt, then stir in the date mixture (including the liquid).
Pour batter into the dish and bake for 30-35 minutes until a toothpick comes out clean.
For the sauce: combine butter, brown sugar, and heavy cream in a saucepan. Simmer for 3-5 minutes until thickened.
Poke holes in the warm cake and pour about 1/3 of the sauce over it. Serve remaining sauce on the side.

name: Spring Rolls
ingredients: 1 tsp cornstarch mixed with 1 tbsp water (thickener)
1 tbsp cornstarch mixed with 1 tbsp water (sealing paste)
instructions: Sauté garlic, ginger, and minced meat in oil until cooked.
Add cabbage, carrots, mushrooms, and bamboo shoots; stir-fry for 2-3 minutes.
Add soy sauce, oyster sauce, sesame oil, sugar, and pepper. Stir in cornstarch slurry.
Let filling cool completely.
Place 2 tbsp filling on a diamond-shaped wrapper.
Fold bottom corner over filling, fold sides in, and roll upward tightly.
Seal with cornstarch paste.
Heat oil to 350°F (175°C).
Fry rolls for 3-5 minutes until golden brown and crispy.
Drain on wire rack or paper towels.
serving_size: 2-3 rolls per person as an appetizer

name: Fresh Summer Rolls
ingredients: Fresh mint, cilantro, and/or Thai basil leaves
instructions: Cook rice noodles, drain, and rinse with cold water.
Prep all vegetables and herbs.
Dip rice paper in warm water for 10-15 seconds until pliable.
Place 2-3 shrimp halves in a row in the center of the wrapper.
Layer herbs, noodles, and vegetables on top of a lettuce leaf, then place on wrapper.
Fold bottom of wrapper up, fold sides in, and roll tightly.
Whisk peanut sauce ingredients together, adding water to desired consistency.
Serve immediately.
serving_size: 2-3 rolls per person as an appetizer

name: Dumplings (Jiaozi)
ingredients: Soy sauce, black vinegar, chili oil (for dipping sauce)
instructions: Salt minced cabbage, let sit for 15 mins, then squeeze out excess water.
Mix pork, cabbage, scallions, ginger, garlic, and seasonings in one direction until sticky.
Place 1 tbsp filling in center of wrapper, moisten edges with water.
Fold in half, pinch center, and create pleats on each side to seal.
To boil: Drop in boiling water, add cold water when they float (repeat twice), cook until translucent.
To pan-fry: Fry in oil for 2 mins, add 1/2 cup water, cover and steam for 5 mins, then crisp bottoms.
Serve with dipping sauce.

name: Gyoza
ingredients: 1 cup garlic chives or green onions, finely chopped
Pinch of salt and black pepper
instructions: Toss cabbage with salt, let sit for 10 mins, then squeeze out water.
Mix cabbage, meat, chives, garlic, ginger, and seasonings until tacky.
Place 1 tbsp filling in center of wrapper, wet edges.
Fold and create 3-5 pleats on one side, pressing against the flat back.
Heat 1 tbsp oil in skillet, sear gyoza for 2-3 mins until golden.
Add 1/4 cup water, cover, and steam for 3-4 mins.
Remove lid and cook for 1 min to re-crisp bottoms.
Serve with dipping sauce.

name: Takoyaki
ingredients: Takoyaki sauce, Japanese mayonnaise, Aonori, Katsuobushi (toppings)
instructions: Whisk eggs, soy sauce, flour, cornstarch, and dashi into a thin batter.
Heat and oil takoyaki pan generously.
Fill holes to the brim with batter.
Drop octopus piece into each hole; sprinkle onions, ginger, and tenkasu over pan.
Cut overflow batter between holes and flip balls 90 degrees.
Rotate balls frequently until golden brown and round.
Top with sauce, mayo, aonori, and bonito flakes.
serving_size: 6-8 balls per person as a snack

name: Edamame
ingredients: Sea salt and toasted sesame seeds (garnish)
instructions: Boil edamame in salted water for 3-5 minutes, then drain.
Heat sesame oil in a skillet, sauté garlic and ginger for 30-60 seconds.
Add edamame pods to skillet.
Toss with soy sauce, sweetener, and chili for 1-2 minutes until coated.
Garnish with sea salt and sesame seeds.

name: Chicken Satay
ingredients: 1.2 lbs boneless skinless chicken thighs, in strips
instructions: Grind coriander, cumin, and peppercorns into powder.
Mix marinade ingredients with spices and marinate chicken for at least 2 hours.
For sauce: Simmer 1/4 cup coconut milk with curry paste until oil separates.
Add remaining coconut milk, peanuts, sugar, and tamarind; simmer for 5 mins.
Thread chicken onto skewers.
Grill for 2-3 minutes per side until cooked through.
Serve with peanut sauce.
serving_size: 3-4 skewers per person as an appetizer

name: Chicken Karaage
ingredients: 1 lb boneless chicken thighs, in 1-inch pieces
instructions: Mix soy sauce, sake, mirin, ginger, garlic, sesame oil, salt, and pepper.
Marinate chicken pieces for at least 30 minutes.
Dredge chicken in potato starch, shaking off excess.
Heat oil to 350°F (175°C).
Fry chicken for 3-4 minutes until light golden brown.
Rest chicken for 2 minutes.
Optionally flash-fry at 375°F (190°C) for 45-60 seconds for extra crunch.
Serve with lemon and mayonnaise.

name: Tempura
ingredients: Sweet potato, lotus root, broccoli, mushrooms, etc.
100ml dashi, 2 tbsp mirin, 2 tbsp soy sauce (dipping sauce)
instructions: Wash and dry all seafood and vegetables thoroughly.
Slice dense vegetables thinly; dust ingredients lightly with cornstarch.
Heat oil to 180°C (350°F).
Mix chilled egg, water, and soda water; sift in flour and cornstarch.
Mix briefly with chopsticks, leaving the batter lumpy.
Dip pieces into batter and fry in small batches for 2-3 minutes.
Drain on wire rack and serve with dipping sauce.
serving_size: Assorted pieces for one person as a starter

name: Kimchi
ingredients: 1/4 to 1/2 cup Gochugaru (Korean red pepper flakes)
instructions: Cut cabbage into 1-2 inch squares, toss with salt, and let sit for 1.5-2 hours.
Rinse cabbage 3 times and drain well.
Mix garlic, ginger, sugar, and liquid, then stir in gochugaru to make paste.
Combine cabbage, vegetables, and paste; mix well (use gloves).
Pack into clean jars, pressing down to remove air; leave headspace.
Ferment at room temperature for 1-5 days, burping daily.
Store in refrigerator once tangy.

name: Korean Pancakes
ingredients: 1 bunch green onions, cut into 2-3 inch lengths
Dipping Sauce: 2 tbsp soy sauce, 1 tbsp rice vinegar, 1 tbsp water, 1 tsp sugar, 1/2 tsp sesame oil, 1/2 tsp sesame seeds, optional gochugaru
instructions: Whisk all dipping sauce ingredients in a small bowl and set aside.
In a medium bowl, whisk together flour, starch, salt, garlic powder, and onion powder.
Add ice-cold water and the beaten egg; mix until just combined (a few lumps are okay).
Heat 2-3 tablespoons of oil in a large non-stick skillet over medium-high heat.
Pour half of the batter into the pan and arrange half of the scallions on top.
Cook for 3-4 minutes until the bottom is golden brown and crispy.
Carefully flip and cook for another 2-3 minutes, adding more oil if needed.
Slice into wedges and serve hot with the dipping sauce.
serving_size: 2 large pancakes (serves 2 as a main or 4 as an appetizer)

name: Thai Fish Cakes
ingredients: 1 lb white fish fillets (Basa, Tilapia, or Cod)
1/2 cup green beans, thinly sliced into rounds
Dipping Sauce: Thai sweet chili sauce, chopped cucumber, crushed peanuts
instructions: Place fish chunks, red curry paste, egg, fish sauce, sugar, and starch in a food processor.
Pulse until the mixture becomes a smooth, sticky paste.
Transfer to a bowl and stir in the lime leaves and green beans by hand.
Wet your hands and shape the mixture into small discs (about 2 inches wide).
Heat oil in a skillet over medium heat.
Fry the cakes in batches for 2-3 minutes per side until deep golden brown.
Drain on paper towels and serve hot with dipping sauce.

name: Tom Yum Soup
ingredients: 2 stalks lemongrass, bruised and cut into pieces
1 lb large shrimp, peeled and deveined
1 small white onion, cut into wedges
Fresh cilantro and green onions for garnish
instructions: Bring chicken stock to a boil; add lemongrass, galangal, lime leaves, and chilies.
Simmer for 5 minutes until fragrant.
Add onions, mushrooms, and tomatoes; simmer for 2-3 minutes.
Add shrimp and cook for 1-2 minutes until pink and opaque.
Stir in fish sauce, sugar, and chili paste; then turn off the heat.
Stir in fresh lime juice after removing from heat to avoid bitterness.
Ladle into bowls and garnish with cilantro and green onions.

name: Pad Thai Spring Rolls
ingredients: 1/2 lb firm tofu (matchsticks) or cooked shrimp
Pad Thai Sauce: 2 tbsp tamarind paste, 2 tbsp fish sauce, 1 tbsp lime juice, 1 tbsp sugar
Peanut Sauce: 1/4 cup peanut butter, 1 tbsp soy sauce, 1 tbsp lime juice, 1 tsp honey, 1 tsp ginger
instructions: Whisk the Pad Thai sauce ingredients and the peanut dipping sauce ingredients in separate bowls.
Prepare all fillings: cook noodles, sear tofu, and julienne vegetables.
Dip a rice paper sheet in warm water for 5-10 seconds until soft.
Place noodles, protein, vegetables, and herbs in the lower third of the wrapper.
Drizzle 1 tsp of Pad Thai sauce over the filling and sprinkle with peanuts.
Fold the bottom and sides over the filling and roll up tightly.
Serve immediately with the peanut dipping sauce.

name: Green Mango Salad
ingredients: 1 sweet red bell pepper, thinly sliced
Dressing: 2 tbsp lime juice, 4 tsp fish sauce, 4 tsp sugar, 1 tbsp oil, 1/4 tsp chili sauce
instructions: Peel and julienne the green mangoes into thin matchsticks.
Whisk together all dressing ingredients until the sugar dissolves.
In a large bowl, combine mango, red pepper, onion, cilantro, and mint.
Pour the dressing over the salad and toss gently to coat.
Top with toasted nuts just before serving to maintain crunch.

name: Samosa
ingredients: Pastry: 2 cups all-purpose flour, 1/2 tsp carom seeds, 1/2 tsp salt, 4 tbsp ghee, 5-6 tbsp water
Filling: 3-4 medium boiled potatoes, 1/2 cup green peas, 2 tbsp oil, 1 tsp cumin seeds, 1 tsp ginger, 1-2 green chilies
Spices: 1 tsp coriander powder, 1/2 tsp garam masala, 1/2 tsp amchur, 1/4 tsp turmeric, salt
instructions: Mix flour, carom seeds, and salt; rub in ghee until crumbly, then add water to form a stiff dough. Rest for 30 minutes.
Heat oil, sauté cumin, ginger, and chilies. Add peas and mashed potatoes with all spices.
Cook for 3-4 minutes, stir in cilantro, and let the filling cool completely.
Divide dough into balls, roll into ovals, and cut in half to make semi-circles.
Form a cone with a semi-circle, fill with potato mixture, and seal the edges with water.
Deep fry on low-medium heat for 10-12 minutes until golden and crisp.

name: Pakora
ingredients: Vegetables: 1 onion (sliced), 1 potato (thinly sliced), 1 cup spinach (chopped), 1/2 cup cauliflower florets
instructions: Whisk besan, rice flour, and spices in a large bowl.
Gradually add water to create a thick, smooth batter.
Fold in the prepared vegetables and chilies until thoroughly coated.
Heat 2 inches of oil in a deep pan over medium-high heat.
Drop small clumps of the mixture into the oil and fry for 3-5 minutes.
Turn occasionally until crisp and deep golden brown.
Drain on paper towels and serve with chutney.

name: Chicken Lollipop
instructions: Marinate the frenched chicken with ginger-garlic paste, soy sauce, chili paste, pepper, salt, and vinegar for 30 minutes.
Add cornstarch, flour, egg, and food color to the marinade and mix well to form a thick coating.
Heat oil in a deep pan over medium-high heat.
Fry the chicken in batches for 5-7 minutes until cooked through.
Increase heat for the last minute for extra crispiness.
Drain on paper towels and serve with Szechuan sauce.

name: Dim Sum
ingredients: 4-5 dried shiitake mushrooms, soaked and minced
Seasonings: 1 tbsp soy sauce, 1 tbsp Shaoxing wine, 2 tsp sesame oil, 1 tsp sugar, 1/2 tsp salt, 1/4 tsp white pepper, 1 tbsp cornstarch, 1 egg white
instructions: Combine pork, shrimp, mushrooms, and water chestnuts in a bowl.
Add all seasonings and stir vigorously in one direction for 3-5 minutes until sticky.
Place a tablespoon of filling in the center of a wrapper and form an open-top cylinder.
Flatten the top with a spoon and garnish with a dot of minced carrot.
Place in a lined steamer basket and steam over high heat for 8-10 minutes.
Serve hot with chili oil or soy sauce.

name: Wonton Soup
ingredients: 3 cups fresh spinach or baby bok choy
1 bunch green onions, whites and greens separated
Optional: 1 tbsp Shaoxing wine, 1/2 tsp white pepper
instructions: Sauté green onion whites, garlic, and ginger in sesame oil for 1-2 minutes.
Add carrots, soy sauce, and chicken broth; bring to a rolling boil.
Add wontons, reduce heat, and simmer for 5-6 minutes until they float.
Stir in spinach or bok choy during the last 2 minutes until wilted.
Ladle into bowls and garnish with green onion tops, sesame seeds, and chili crisp.

name: Bruschetta
ingredients: 1.5 lbs ripe Roma or vine tomatoes, diced
1 large baguette or loaf of crusty Italian bread
2-3 cloves garlic (1 minced, 1-2 whole for rubbing)
1/4 cup fresh basil leaves, thinly sliced
Salt and freshly ground black pepper to taste
instructions: Prepare the Topping: Combine diced tomatoes, minced garlic, sliced basil, olive oil, and balsamic vinegar in a medium bowl. Season with salt and pepper.
Marinate: Let the mixture sit at room temperature for 15–30 minutes to allow flavors to meld.
Toast the Bread: Preheat oven to 400°F (200°C). Brush bread slices with olive oil and toast for 5–8 minutes until golden and crisp.
Rub Garlic: While bread is warm, rub a peeled whole garlic clove across the surface of each slice.
Assemble: Top each slice of bread with a generous portion of the tomato mixture just before serving.
Serve: Drizzle with extra olive oil or balsamic glaze if desired.

name: Caprese Salad
ingredients: 1 bunch fresh basil (about 1/3 cup whole leaves)
Flaky sea salt and freshly cracked black pepper to taste
instructions: Layer: Alternate slices of tomato and mozzarella on a large platter or individual plates.
Add Basil: Tuck whole basil leaves between the tomato and mozzarella slices.
Season: Sprinkle generously with sea salt and black pepper.
Drizzle: Finish by drizzling extra virgin olive oil and balsamic glaze evenly over the top.
Serve: Serve immediately at room temperature.
serving_size: 3-4 stacks (1 stack = 1 slice tomato, 1 slice mozzarella, 1 basil leaf)

name: Antipasto Platter
ingredients: 6 oz Prosciutto, thinly sliced and rolled
4 oz Sharp Provolone, cut into wedges
4 oz Parmigiano Reggiano, broken into chunks
Fresh rosemary or basil for garnish
instructions: Prepare Bowls: Place wet items (olives, artichokes, peppers) into small individual serving bowls.
Anchor Platter: Place bowls on a large board or platter as anchor points.
Arrange Meats: Fold or roll sliced meats and place in piles around the bowls.
Add Cheeses: Fill gaps with cheese selections.
Fill Gaps: Use fresh vegetables and tomatoes to fill remaining empty spaces.
Garnish & Serve: Tuck in fresh herbs and serve at room temperature with bread.

name: Arancini
ingredients: 5 cups chicken or vegetable broth, warm
4 oz fresh mozzarella, cut into 1/2-inch cubes
Salt and black pepper to taste
instructions: Prepare Rice: Sauté onion in olive oil and butter. Add rice and toast for 2 minutes. Add wine and stir until absorbed.
Cook Risotto: Add warm broth one ladle at a time, stirring until absorbed. Once tender, stir in Parmesan, salt, and pepper.
Cool: Spread rice on a baking sheet and refrigerate for at least 2 hours until completely cold.
Form Balls: With damp hands, flatten 1/4 cup of rice, place a mozzarella cube and peas in the center, and roll into a tight ball.
Bread: Dredge balls in flour, dip in beaten eggs, and roll in breadcrumbs.
Fry: Heat oil to 350°F (175°C). Fry balls in batches for 3-5 minutes until golden brown.
Serve: Drain on paper towels and serve warm with marinara sauce.

name: Croquettes
ingredients: 2.2 lbs starchy potatoes (Russet or Yukon Gold)
3.5 oz mozzarella, cut into small strips (for filling)
instructions: Cook Potatoes: Boil whole, unpeeled potatoes until tender. Peel and mash while warm until smooth. Let cool completely.
Mix: Combine cooled mashed potatoes, egg yolks, Parmesan, salt, and nutmeg. Mix until smooth.
Shape: Form small portions into cylinders. If using cheese, insert a strip of mozzarella and seal.
Coat: Roll each croquette in flour, dip in beaten egg, and roll in breadcrumbs.
Chill: Refrigerate for at least 1 hour to prevent falling apart during frying.
Fry: Heat oil to 350°F (175°C). Fry in batches for 2-3 minutes until golden brown.
Serve: Drain on paper towels and serve warm.

name: Patatas Bravas
ingredients: 1 tbsp Smoked Spanish Paprika (mix of sweet and spicy)
Optional: 1 clove garlic, splash of sherry vinegar, and alioli for serving
instructions: Prepare Potatoes: Peel and cut potatoes into bite-sized chunks. Soak in water for 15 minutes, then pat completely dry.
Make Sauce: Heat 3 tbsp oil, stir in flour for 1 minute, then add paprika (do not burn). Gradually whisk in broth and simmer until thickened.
First Fry: Fry potatoes at 325°F (160°C) for 5-7 minutes until tender. Drain.
Second Fry: Increase heat to 375°F (190°C) and fry for 2-3 minutes until deep golden and crispy.
Assemble: Toss hot potatoes with salt, drizzle with Salsa Brava, and top with alioli if desired.

name: Gazpacho
ingredients: 1 slice stale white bread, soaked and squeezed dry
Optional garnishes: diced cucumber, pepper, onion, and croutons
instructions: Blend: Place tomatoes, pepper, cucumber, garlic, onion, and bread in a blender. Blend until liquefied.
Emulsify: Slowly drizzle in olive oil while blending on low. Add vinegar and salt.
Strain: Pour through a fine-mesh sieve for a smooth texture, discarding skins and seeds.
Chill: Refrigerate for at least 2 hours or overnight.
Serve: Serve very cold in bowls or glasses with garnishes.

name: Tapas
ingredients: 1 lb large shrimp, peeled and deveined
2 tbsp dry sherry or white wine
instructions: Prep: Pat shrimp completely dry and season with salt.
Infuse Oil: Heat olive oil, garlic, and red pepper flakes over medium-low heat until garlic is fragrant and pale golden.
Sauté Shrimp: Increase heat to medium. Add shrimp and paprika. Cook for 2-3 minutes until opaque.
Finish: Remove from heat. Stir in sherry, lemon juice, and parsley.
Serve: Serve immediately while sizzling with bread for dipping.
serving_size: Serves 4-6 people as an appetizer (tapas style)

name: French Onion Soup
ingredients: 1/2 cup dry white wine or dry sherry
Salt, pepper, and 1 tsp balsamic vinegar to taste
instructions: Caramelize Onions: Melt butter and oil. Add onions and cook slowly for 45-60 minutes until deep mahogany brown.
Simmer: Add garlic, then deglaze with wine. Add stock, thyme, and bay leaf. Simmer for 20-30 minutes.
Finish Soup: Discard herbs. Stir in vinegar and season with salt and pepper.
Assemble: Ladle soup into oven-safe bowls. Top with toasted bread and a generous layer of Gruyère.
Broil: Broil for 2-4 minutes until cheese is melted and bubbly.

name: Escargots
ingredients: 24 canned French snails, rinsed and drained
24 snail shells or an escargot dish
3–4 cloves garlic, minced into a paste
1/2 tsp salt and 1/4 tsp black pepper
instructions: Prepare Garlic Butter: Blend softened butter, garlic, shallot, parsley, wine, salt, and pepper until smooth.
Assemble: Place a bit of butter in each shell/indentation, add a snail, and top with more butter to seal.
Bake: Preheat oven to 400°F (200°C). Place shells on a bed of sea salt to stay upright.
Cook: Bake for 10-12 minutes until butter is bubbling and sizzling.
Serve: Serve immediately with baguette slices.

name: Quiche
ingredients: 6–8 slices of bacon, cooked and crumbled
1 cup (4 oz) Gruyère or Swiss cheese, shredded
1/4 cup shallots or yellow onion, finely minced
instructions: Preheat oven to 375°F (190°C). Blind bake the pie crust for 8–10 minutes until set. Lower oven to 350°F (175°C).
Cook bacon until crisp, then crumble. Sauté shallots in a teaspoon of bacon fat until translucent.
Layer the crumbled bacon, sautéed shallots, and shredded cheese in the bottom of the warm crust.
In a bowl, whisk together eggs, heavy cream, salt, pepper, and nutmeg until smooth.
Pour the egg mixture over the fillings in the pie crust.
Bake for 40–50 minutes until the edges are set and golden brown, but the center still has a slight jiggle.
Let the quiche rest for 10–15 minutes before slicing.

name: Cheese Soufflé
ingredients: 3 tbsp unsalted butter (plus extra for greasing)
1 ½ cups shredded sharp cheese (Gruyère, Cheddar, or Comté)
instructions: Preheat oven to 375°F (190°C). Butter a 1.5-quart soufflé dish and coat with Parmesan cheese.
In a saucepan, melt 3 tbsp butter, whisk in flour and cook for 1-2 minutes.
Slowly whisk in milk and cook until thickened and smooth (3-4 minutes). Remove from heat.
Stir in salt, cayenne, and shredded cheese until melted. Whisk in egg yolks one at a time.
In a separate bowl, beat egg whites and cream of tartar until stiff peaks form.
Gently fold one-quarter of the whites into the cheese sauce, then carefully fold in the rest.
Pour into the prepared dish and bake for 25–30 minutes until puffed and golden brown. Serve immediately.

name: Smoked Salmon Canapés
ingredients: 6–8 slices of pumpernickel or cocktail rye bread
4–6 oz smoked salmon, cut into thin strips
1 tbsp fresh dill, finely chopped (plus sprigs for garnish)
instructions: Cut the bread into 1.5-inch rounds or squares using a cutter or knife.
In a small bowl, mix softened cream cheese, chopped dill, lemon juice, and lemon zest until smooth.
Spread a teaspoon of the cream cheese mixture onto each bread round.
Fold a piece of smoked salmon and place it on top of the cream cheese.
Garnish each canapé with a small sprig of dill and 2-3 capers.
Serve immediately or chill for up to 1 hour.

name: Deviled Eggs
ingredients: Salt and black pepper to taste
instructions: Slice the hard-boiled eggs in half lengthwise and remove the yolks.
Mash the yolks in a bowl with a fork until fine.
Stir in mayonnaise, mustard, vinegar, salt, and pepper until smooth and creamy.
Spoon or pipe the yolk mixture back into the egg white halves.
Garnish with a sprinkle of paprika. Refrigerate for at least 30 minutes before serving.

name: Stuffed Mushrooms
ingredients: 20 whole cremini or white button mushrooms
instructions: Preheat oven to 350°F (175°C). Clean mushrooms and remove stems.
Finely chop the stems. Sauté stems and minced garlic in olive oil for 3-5 minutes until tender.
In a bowl, combine cream cheese, Parmesan, pepper, onion powder, parsley, and the sautéed stems.
Stuff each mushroom cap generously with the cream cheese mixture.
Mix Panko breadcrumbs with melted butter. Press the top of each mushroom into the breadcrumbs.
Bake on a baking sheet for 20 minutes until tender and golden brown.

name: Garlic Bread
ingredients: 1 large loaf of French or Italian bread
instructions: Preheat oven to 375°F (190°C).
In a small bowl, combine softened butter, minced garlic, parsley, and salt.
Slice the loaf in half lengthwise or into 1-inch thick accordion slices.
Spread the garlic butter generously over the cut sides or between slices.
For crispy bread, place halves face-up on a baking sheet. For soft bread, wrap the loaf in foil.
Bake for 10–15 minutes until the butter is melted and edges are golden. Broil for 1-2 minutes if extra crispiness is desired.

name: Prawn Cocktail
ingredients: Salt and black pepper to taste
Paprika and lemon wedges for garnish
instructions: In a small bowl, whisk together mayonnaise, ketchup, Worcestershire sauce, and lemon juice. Season with salt and pepper.
Pat the prawns dry and toss them with two-thirds of the sauce until well coated.
Divide the shredded lettuce between four chilled glasses or bowls.
Spoon the coated prawns over the lettuce and drizzle with the remaining sauce.
Garnish with a dusting of paprika and a lemon wedge. Serve immediately.

name: Herring Salad
ingredients: 8–12 oz jarred pickled herring fillets, drained and diced
2 medium waxy potatoes, boiled, peeled, and diced
1 large tart apple, peeled and diced
½ small red onion, finely chopped
instructions: Dice herring, potatoes, beets, apple, pickles, and onion into uniform 1/2-inch cubes.
In a small bowl, whisk together sour cream, mayonnaise, vinegar, mustard, and fresh dill.
Gently toss the diced ingredients in a large bowl with the dressing until evenly coated.
Cover and refrigerate for 1–2 hours to allow flavors to meld.
Garnish with sliced hard-boiled eggs and extra dill before serving.

name: Potato Rösti
instructions: Peel and coarsely grate the potatoes.
Place grated potatoes in a kitchen towel and squeeze firmly to remove as much liquid as possible.
Toss the dry potato shreds with salt and pepper.
Melt 2 tbsp butter in a non-stick skillet over medium heat.
Add potatoes to the pan, spread evenly and press down firmly to form a compact cake.
Cook for 10–12 minutes until the bottom is deeply golden. Flip the Rösti onto a plate.
Add remaining 1 tbsp butter to the pan, slide the Rösti back in, and cook for another 8–10 minutes.
serving_size: 1/4 wedge (as a side) or 1/2 wedge (as a main)

name: Beef Carpaccio
ingredients: Flaky sea salt and freshly cracked black pepper
instructions: Wrap the beef tenderloin in plastic and freeze for 60–90 minutes to firm up.
Slice the beef across the grain into very thin rounds (1/8 inch or less).
Pound the slices between plastic wrap until paper-thin and translucent.
Arrange slices in a single layer on chilled plates.
Top with arugula, Parmesan shavings, and capers.
Whisk oil and lemon juice together, drizzle over the dish, and season with salt and pepper. Serve immediately.

name: Buffalo Wings
ingredients: 2½ to 3 lbs chicken wings (split into flats and drums)
Salt and black pepper to taste
½ cup Frank’s RedHot Original Cayenne Pepper Sauce
1 tbsp white vinegar or cider vinegar
¼ tsp cayenne pepper (optional, for extra heat)
instructions: Pat the chicken wings completely dry with paper towels; season lightly with salt and pepper.
Heat 3–4 inches of vegetable oil in a heavy pot to 375°F (190°C).
Fry the wings in small batches for 12–15 minutes until golden brown and very crispy.
Drain wings on a wire rack or paper towels.
In a small saucepan, melt the butter over medium-low heat; whisk in the hot sauce, vinegar, garlic powder, and cayenne pepper.
Simmer the sauce for 1–2 minutes until well combined.
Place the hot wings in a large bowl, pour the sauce over them, and toss vigorously until thoroughly coated.
Serve immediately with cold celery sticks and blue cheese dressing.

name: Chicken Tenders
ingredients: 1.5–2 lbs chicken tenders (or boneless skinless chicken breasts sliced into 1-inch strips)
Vegetable, canola, or peanut oil (for frying)
Optional: Panko breadcrumbs (replace half the flour for extra crunch)
instructions: Heat about 2 inches of oil in a large skillet or Dutch oven to 365°F (185°C).
In one shallow bowl, mix flour, salt, and pepper. In a second bowl, whisk the egg and water together.
Dredge each chicken strip in the flour mixture, dip into the egg wash, and then place back into the flour (or Panko) for a final coating.
Let the breaded chicken sit on a plate for about 5 minutes to help the coating adhere.
Carefully fry the chicken in batches for 3–5 minutes per side until golden brown and internal temperature reaches 165°F.
Transfer tenders to a paper towel-lined plate to drain and sprinkle with a pinch of salt while hot.

name: Nachos
ingredients: 1 large bag (approx. 13 oz) tortilla chips (thick or restaurant-style)
2–3 cups shredded cheese (Cheddar and Monterey Jack mix)
1 can (15 oz) black beans (drained) or refried beans
½ cup salsa (pico de gallo or chunky)
¼ cup pickled or fresh jalapeño slices
¼ cup red or green onions (finely diced)
instructions: Preheat oven to 400°F (200°C).
Brown the ground beef in a skillet, drain fat, and stir in taco seasoning with a splash of water; simmer for 5 minutes.
Spread half of the tortilla chips in an even layer on a large rimmed baking sheet.
Top the chips with half of the cooked beef, half of the beans, and half of the shredded cheese.
Repeat with the remaining chips, beef, beans, and cheese to create a second layer.
Bake for 10–15 minutes until the cheese is completely melted and bubbling.
Remove from oven and immediately add fresh toppings: salsa, sour cream, avocado, onions, jalapeños, and cilantro.
Serve immediately.

name: Quesadillas
ingredients: 1 cup shredded cheese (Cheddar, Monterey Jack, or Mexican blend)
Optional: Salsa, sour cream, or guacamole (for serving)
instructions: Heat a large non-stick skillet over medium heat and add half the butter or oil.
Place one tortilla in the pan and sprinkle the shredded cheese evenly over its surface.
Place the second tortilla on top of the cheese.
Cook for 2–3 minutes until the bottom tortilla is golden brown and crispy.
Carefully flip the quesadilla and cook the other side for another 2–3 minutes until golden and cheese is melted.
Let the quesadilla rest for one minute on a cutting board, then slice into 6–8 triangles.
Serve warm with optional toppings.

name: Guacamole with Chips
ingredients: ½ cup red or white onion (finely diced)
instructions: Place avocado flesh in a medium bowl and mash with a fork or potato masher to desired consistency.
Stir in lime juice immediately to prevent browning.
Add minced garlic, salt, pepper, and cumin; stir well.
Gently fold in the diced onion, cilantro, jalapeño, and tomatoes.
Taste and adjust seasonings (salt, lime, or jalapeño) as needed.
Transfer to a serving bowl and serve with tortilla chips.
serving_size: 4-6 people (¼ to ℓ cup guacamole per person)

name: Jalapeño Poppers
ingredients: ½ cup shredded sharp cheddar cheese (optional)
Optional: Fresh chives or green onions (chopped for garnish)
instructions: Preheat oven to 400°F (200°C) and line a baking sheet with foil or parchment paper.
Slice each jalapeño in half lengthwise and scrape out the seeds and white membranes (use gloves).
In a medium bowl, combine softened cream cheese, shredded cheddar, garlic powder, and black pepper.
Spoon about 1 tablespoon of the cheese mixture into each jalapeño half.
Cut each bacon slice in half widthwise and wrap one piece tightly around each stuffed pepper half.
Place poppers on the baking sheet and bake for 15–20 minutes until bacon is crispy and peppers are tender.
Cool for 5 minutes before serving; garnish with chives if desired.

name: Corn Dogs
ingredients: 8–10 wooden skewers or sturdy popsicle sticks
instructions: Heat about 3 inches of vegetable oil in a large pot to 350°F (175°C).
Pat hot dogs completely dry with paper towels; insert a skewer into each hot dog.
Optional: Lightly dust the hot dogs with cornstarch.
In a large bowl, whisk together cornmeal, flour, sugar, baking powder, and salt.
In a separate bowl, whisk egg, buttermilk, honey, and 1 tbsp oil; combine with dry ingredients into a thick batter.
Pour batter into a tall drinking glass for easy dipping.
Dip each hot dog into the batter to coat completely, then carefully lower into hot oil.
Fry 2–3 at a time for about 3 minutes, turning occasionally until golden brown.
Drain on a wire rack or paper-towel-lined plate.

name: Sliders
ingredients: 6–12 slices of cheese (Cheddar, American, or Havarti)
Optional toppings: Pickles, caramelized onions, lettuce, tomato, condiments
instructions: In a large bowl, gently mix ground beef with salt, pepper, garlic powder, onion powder, and Worcestershire sauce.
Divide beef into 12 equal portions (1.5–2 oz each) and shape into small patties slightly larger than the buns.
Heat a large skillet or griddle over medium-high heat and cook patties for 3–4 minutes per side.
Place a small piece of cheese on each patty during the last minute of cooking; cover pan to melt.
Place patties on the bottom buns, add optional toppings, and cover with top buns.
Alternatively, bake the entire seasoned beef mixture in a 9x13 pan, place on split rolls, top with cheese and bun tops, brush with butter, and bake at 350°F for 5 minutes.

name: Mac and Cheese Bites
ingredients: Salt and black pepper to taste
instructions: Boil macaroni until al dente; drain and set aside.
Melt butter in a saucepan, whisk in ¼ cup flour, then slowly whisk in milk until thickened. Stir in cheese and seasonings until smooth.
Fold in macaroni, then spread into a greased 9x13 dish and refrigerate for 3–4 hours until firm.
Scoop and roll the mixture into 1 to 1.5-inch balls.
Dredge balls in flour, dip in egg wash (eggs + 2 tbsp milk), and coat with Panko breadcrumbs.
Heat oil to 350°F (175°C) and fry in batches for 3–4 minutes until golden brown.
Drain on a paper towel-lined plate and serve hot.

name: Clam Chowder
ingredients: 2–3 cans (6.5 oz each) chopped clams (reserve juice)
3–4 medium Russet potatoes (peeled and cut into ½-inch cubes)
Salt and freshly ground black pepper to taste
Fresh parsley and oyster crackers (for serving)
instructions: Cook bacon in a large pot until crisp; remove bacon and set aside, leaving fat in the pot.
Add butter to bacon fat; sauté onion and celery for 5–7 minutes. Add garlic and cook for 1 minute.
Stir in flour and cook for 1–2 minutes to remove raw taste.
Gradually whisk in reserved clam juice and chicken broth. Add potatoes, bay leaf, and thyme.
Simmer for 15–20 minutes until potatoes are tender.
Stir in half-and-half and chopped clams; heat through for 3–5 minutes (do not boil).
Remove bay leaf, season with salt and pepper, and serve with bacon bits, parsley, and crackers.

name: Shrimp Cocktail
ingredients: 1.5 to 2 lbs large or jumbo shrimp (16/20 or 21/25 count), peeled and deveined (tails left on)
Freshly ground black pepper to taste
instructions: Prepare the cocktail sauce by whisking together ketchup, horseradish, lemon juice, Worcestershire sauce, hot sauce, and black pepper. Refrigerate for at least 30 minutes.
In a large pot, combine water, lemon halves (squeeze juice in first), onion, garlic, celery, salt, peppercorns, bay leaves, and Old Bay. Bring to a boil, then simmer for 10 minutes.
Add the shrimp to the pot. Cook for 2–3 minutes until pink and opaque.
Immediately drain the shrimp and plunge them into an ice water bath for 5 minutes until completely cold.
Drain the shrimp well and pat dry with paper towels.
Arrange the chilled shrimp on a platter or glasses and serve with the chilled cocktail sauce, garnished with lemon wedges and parsley.

name: Onion Rings
ingredients: 1 large sweet onion (like Vidalia), sliced into 1/2-inch thick rings
instructions: Slice the onion into 1/2-inch thick rings and separate them.
Set up three shallow bowls: Bowl 1 with flour, cornstarch, salt, pepper, garlic powder, and paprika; Bowl 2 with buttermilk; Bowl 3 with Panko breadcrumbs.
Coat each onion ring first in the flour mixture (shake off excess), then dip in buttermilk, and finally press into the Panko breadcrumbs.
Heat 2 inches of oil in a heavy pot to 375°F (190°C).
Fry the rings in small batches for 2–3 minutes, flipping halfway, until deep golden brown.
Transfer to a wire rack to drain. Sprinkle with extra salt and serve hot.

name: Mozzarella Sticks
ingredients: 12 string cheese sticks, cut in half crosswise
Salt and black pepper to taste
instructions: Prepare three bowls: flour with garlic powder, salt, and pepper; whisked eggs and milk; breadcrumbs with Italian seasoning.
Coat each cheese stick in flour, then dip in the egg mixture, then roll in breadcrumbs. For a thicker crust, dip back into the egg and then the breadcrumbs again.
Place the breaded sticks on a parchment-lined baking sheet and freeze for at least 45–60 minutes to prevent leaking.
Heat 2 inches of oil in a skillet to 350°F (175°C).
Fry in small batches for 1–2 minutes until golden brown.
Drain on paper towels and serve immediately with warm marinara sauce.

name: Chili Con Carne
ingredients: 1 can (28 oz) crushed or diced tomatoes
1 can (15 oz) red kidney beans, drained and rinsed
1 tsp sugar or a small square of dark chocolate (optional)
Salt and black pepper to taste
Toppings: shredded cheddar, sour cream, green onions, cilantro
instructions: In a large pot, brown the ground beef over medium-high heat. Drain excess fat.
Add onion, bell pepper, and jalapeño. Sauté for 5–7 minutes until soft. Stir in garlic and cook for 1 minute.
Stir in tomato paste and all spices (chili powder, cumin, paprika, oregano, cayenne). Toast for 2 minutes.
Pour in crushed tomatoes and beef broth. Bring to a boil, then reduce heat and simmer covered for at least 30–60 minutes.
Stir in the kidney beans and optional sugar or chocolate. Simmer uncovered for 10–15 minutes until thickened.
Season with salt and pepper. Serve hot with desired toppings.

name: Mini Tacos
ingredients: 24 mini corn tortillas (4-inch street taco size)
1 to 2 cups shredded cheese (Monterey Jack or Mexican blend)
2–3 tbsp vegetable oil or cooking spray
Optional toppings: sour cream, diced onions, cilantro, salsa, guacamole
instructions: Brown the ground beef in a skillet over medium-high heat. Drain grease.
Stir in taco seasoning and water. Simmer for 3–5 minutes until liquid is absorbed.
Preheat oven to 400°F (200°C). Line a baking sheet with oil and lay out the tortillas.
Place a small amount of cheese and a tablespoon of meat on each tortilla.
Bake for 2–3 minutes until cheese melts and tortillas are pliable.
Remove and fold each tortilla in half. Return to the oven for 5–8 minutes until crispy.
Serve immediately with fresh toppings.

name: Empanadas
ingredients: 1 cup unsalted butter, cold and cubed
1 tsp cumin, 1 tsp smoked paprika, 1/2 tsp dried oregano
2 cups shredded cheese (Monterey Jack or Mozzarella)
Egg wash (1 egg beaten with 1 tbsp water)
instructions: Prepare dough by mixing flour and salt, cutting in butter, then adding whisked egg, water, and vinegar. Knead briefly and refrigerate for 30–60 minutes.
Make filling by sautéing onion, pepper, and garlic, then browning the beef. Drain fat and stir in spices. Let cool and stir in cheese.
Roll dough to 1/8-inch thickness and cut into 5-6 inch circles.
Place 2 tablespoons of filling in the center of each circle, fold into a half-moon, and seal edges with a fork.
To Bake: Preheat oven to 400°F (200°C). Brush with egg wash and bake for 20–25 minutes until golden.
To Fry: Heat oil to 350°F (175°C) and fry for 2–3 minutes per side.

name: Ceviche
ingredients: 1 lb fresh, sushi-grade white fish (Snapper or Sea Bass), cut into 1/2-inch cubes
1/2 cup red onion, very thinly sliced
1 jalapeño or serrano pepper, seeded and minced
instructions: Place uniform cubes of fish in a non-reactive bowl.
Pour lime juice over the fish until submerged. Add sliced red onions and salt.
Cover and refrigerate for 30 to 60 minutes until the fish turns opaque.
Add diced tomatoes, minced jalapeño, and cilantro. Gently toss.
Just before serving, fold in the diced avocado.
Serve immediately while very cold with tortilla chips or crackers.

name: Arepas
instructions: In a large bowl, combine warm water and salt. Gradually stir in the Masarepa.
Let the dough rest for 5 minutes, then knead for a minute until smooth.
Divide into 8–10 portions and shape into disks about 1/2 to 3/4 inch thick.
Heat a lightly oiled skillet over medium heat and cook arepas for 5–7 minutes per side until golden spots appear.
Optional: Finish in a 350°F (175°C) oven for 10 minutes until they sound hollow when tapped.
Slice open and fill with cheese, chicken, or beans.

name: Fried Pickles
ingredients: 2 cups dill pickle chips, patted dry
1 tsp garlic powder, 1 tsp smoked paprika, 1/2 tsp onion powder
1/4 tsp cayenne pepper, 1/2 tsp black pepper
instructions: Drain pickles and pat them thoroughly dry with paper towels.
Mix flour, cornmeal, and spices in one bowl; put buttermilk in a second bowl.
Heat oil in a skillet to 375°F (190°C).
Dredge pickles in the flour mixture, dip in buttermilk, then coat again in the flour mixture.
Fry in batches for 1.5 to 2 minutes per side until golden brown and crispy.
Drain on paper towels and serve hot with ranch.

name: BBQ Chicken Skewers
ingredients: 2 lbs boneless, skinless chicken breasts or thighs, 1-inch cubes
Salt and black pepper to taste
instructions: If using wooden skewers, soak in water for at least 30 minutes.
Marinate chicken cubes in 1/2 cup BBQ sauce, olive oil, garlic, paprika, salt, and pepper for 15–30 minutes.
Thread chicken and vegetables onto skewers, alternating pieces.
Heat grill to medium-high (400°F / 200°C).
Grill skewers for 10–15 minutes, turning every few minutes.
Baste with the remaining BBQ sauce during the last 5 minutes of cooking.
Cook until chicken internal temperature reaches 165°F (74°C). Let rest for 5 minutes before serving.

name: Hummus
ingredients: 1 (15 oz) can chickpeas, drained and rinsed
Garnish: Paprika, sumac, fresh parsley, and a few whole chickpeas
instructions: Process tahini and lemon juice in a food processor for 1 minute, scrape sides, and process for 30 seconds more.
Add olive oil, minced garlic, cumin, and salt. Process for 30 seconds.
Add half the chickpeas and process for 1 minute. Add remaining chickpeas and process until thick and smooth.
If too thick, drizzle in cold water while the processor is running until reaching desired consistency.
Transfer to a bowl, drizzle with olive oil, and garnish with paprika, parsley, and reserved chickpeas.

name: Baba Ganoush
ingredients: Optional: 1/2 tsp ground cumin, smoked paprika, fresh parsley
instructions: Preheat oven to 400°F (200°C). Prick eggplants with a fork.
Roast eggplants for 45-60 minutes until skin is charred and flesh has collapsed.
Let cool, then scoop out flesh and discard skin.
Drain flesh in a strainer for 10-20 minutes to remove excess liquid.
Mash eggplant with a fork (rustic) or pulse in a food processor (smooth) with tahini, lemon juice, garlic, olive oil, and salt.
Garnish with olive oil, parsley, and paprika before serving.

name: Falafel
ingredients: 1 cup dried chickpeas (soaked 12-24 hours, do NOT use canned)
instructions: Drain soaked chickpeas and pat very dry.
Pulse chickpeas, herbs, onion, garlic, and spices in a food processor until it reaches a coarse sand consistency.
Stir in flour and baking powder. Refrigerate for 30-60 minutes.
Form into balls or small patties using about 2 tablespoons of mixture each.
Fry in 350°F (175°C) oil for 3-4 minutes until deep golden brown and crispy.
Drain on paper towels and serve warm.

name: Tabbouleh
ingredients: 2-3 large bunches fresh parsley (about 3 cups minced)
1/4 cup fresh mint leaves, finely chopped
Optional: Pinch of allspice or Lebanese 7-Spice
instructions: Soak bulgur in lemon juice for 15-20 minutes until softened.
Finely mince parsley by hand with a sharp knife (avoid food processor).
Combine minced parsley, mint, green onions, and diced tomatoes in a large bowl.
Add soaked bulgur (with any remaining lemon juice), olive oil, salt, and pepper.
Toss gently and refrigerate for at least 30 minutes before serving to let flavors meld.

name: Fattoush Salad
ingredients: 1-2 large pita pockets, torn into 1-inch pieces
Dressing: 1/3 cup olive oil, 3 tbsp lemon juice, 1-2 tsp pomegranate molasses, 1-2 cloves garlic (minced), 1 tbsp sumac, salt and pepper
instructions: Toast pita pieces at 375°F (190°C) with olive oil and salt for 5-10 minutes until crispy.
Whisk dressing ingredients (olive oil, lemon juice, molasses, garlic, sumac, salt, pepper) in a small bowl.
Combine lettuce, cucumbers, tomatoes, radishes, onion, and herbs in a large bowl.
Add half the pita chips and the dressing just before serving. Toss gently.
Top with remaining pita chips and an extra sprinkle of sumac.

name: Kibbeh
ingredients: Shell: 2 cups fine bulgur, 1 lb very lean ground beef/lamb, 1 grated onion, 1.5 tsp 7-spice, 1 tsp salt
Filling: 1 lb ground beef/lamb, 1 diced onion, 1/4 cup toasted pine nuts, 1 tsp 7-spice, salt and pepper, 2 tbsp olive oil
instructions: Soak bulgur for 20 minutes, then squeeze out all water.
Make filling: Sauté onions, brown meat, stir in spices and pine nuts. Let cool.
Make shell: Process onion into paste, add meat and spices, pulse until smooth. Knead in bulgur.
Form: Take egg-sized dough ball, hollow it out with finger, stuff with filling, and seal into football shape.
Fry in 350°F (175°C) oil for 3-5 minutes until deep golden brown.
Serve warm with yogurt or tahini sauce.

name: Shawarma Bites
ingredients: 1 lb chicken breast or thighs, 1/2-inch cubes
Marinade: 2 tbsp olive oil, 1 tbsp lemon juice, 3 cloves garlic, 1 tsp cumin, 1 tsp paprika, 1/2 tsp turmeric/cinnamon/salt
Wraps: 3-4 large tortillas or lavash bread
Sauce: 1/2 cup Greek yogurt, 1 tbsp tahini, 1 tsp lemon juice, 1 clove garlic
Vegetables: Chopped cucumber, tomato, red onion, parsley, pickles
instructions: Marinate chicken cubes for at least 30 minutes.
Cook chicken in a skillet over medium-high heat for 8-10 minutes until browned and cooked through.
Spread sauce on tortilla, add chicken and chopped vegetables.
Roll up tightly and toast in a dry pan for 1-2 minutes per side until crispy.
Slice rolls into 1.5-inch 'sushi-style' bites and serve with extra sauce.

name: Stuffed Grape Leaves
ingredients: Filling: 1 lb ground beef/lamb, 1 cup uncooked short-grain rice, 1 minced onion, 1/2 cup parsley, 2 tbsp mint, 1 tsp salt, spices (allspice, cinnamon, pepper)
Cooking: 2 cups broth, 1/2 cup lemon juice, 1/4 cup olive oil
instructions: Mix filling ingredients (meat, rice, onion, herbs, spices, olive oil) in a bowl.
Place leaf flat (vein-side up), add 1 tbsp filling, and roll tightly like a small burrito.
Line pot bottom with potato/tomato slices and pack rolls tightly, seam-side down.
Add broth, lemon juice, and olive oil. Place a heavy plate on top to keep rolls submerged.
Simmer on low for 45-60 minutes until rice is tender. Rest for 20 minutes before serving.

name: Labneh Dip
ingredients: 1 tbsp fresh dill or mint, chopped
Toppings: Extra virgin olive oil, za'atar, red pepper flakes
instructions: Whisk together labneh, garlic, lemon juice, and fresh herbs in a bowl.
Season with salt and pepper to taste.
Spread into a shallow bowl, creating swirls with a spoon.
Drizzle generously with olive oil and sprinkle with za'atar and red pepper flakes.
Serve with warm pita or fresh vegetables.

name: Pita with Dips
ingredients: Pita Chips: 4 large pitas, 3 tbsp olive oil, 1 tsp garlic powder, 1/2 tsp salt
Hummus: 1 can chickpeas, 1/4 cup tahini, 2 tbsp lemon juice, 1 clove garlic, 2 tbsp olive oil, 1/2 tsp cumin
Tzatziki: 1 cup Greek yogurt, 1/2 grated cucumber (squeezed dry), 1 tbsp lemon juice, 1 clove garlic, 1 tbsp dill
instructions: Chips: Cut pitas into wedges, toss with oil/spices, and bake at 375°F for 8-12 minutes.
Hummus: Blend all ingredients in a food processor until smooth, adding water or chickpea liquid for consistency.
Tzatziki: Squeeze liquid from grated cucumber. Mix with yogurt, lemon, garlic, oil, and dill. Chill before serving.
Arrange chips on a platter with bowls of hummus and tzatziki.

name: Harira Soup
ingredients: 1 lb lamb or beef, cut into 1/2-inch cubes (optional)
1 (28 oz) can crushed tomatoes (or 2 lbs fresh, pureed)
1/2 cup dried green or brown lentils, rinsed
1 (15 oz) can chickpeas, drained and rinsed
1/2 cup dried vermicelli noodles (broken) or orzo
instructions: Heat olive oil in a large pot. Brown the meat if using, then set aside. Sauté onion and celery for 5-7 minutes until softened. Add garlic and cook for 1 minute.
Stir in ginger, turmeric, cumin, paprika, cinnamon, pepper, and cayenne. Cook for 1 minute until fragrant.
Add broth, tomatoes, lentils, and the browned meat. Bring to a boil, then reduce heat to low. Cover and simmer for 30-40 minutes until lentils are tender.
Stir in chickpeas and vermicelli (or orzo). Cook for 10 more minutes until pasta is al dente.
Stir in lemon juice, honey (if using), and half of the fresh cilantro and parsley. Adjust salt to taste.
Serve hot in bowls, garnished with remaining fresh herbs and lemon wedges. Traditionally served with dates.

name: Samosa (African)
ingredients: 2 tbsp flour mixed with water (sealing paste)
instructions: Brown the ground meat in a skillet, breaking it into fine crumbles. Drain excess fat.
Add onions, garlic, ginger, and chilies. Sauté for 3-5 minutes until onions are translucent.
Stir in cumin, coriander, garam masala, turmeric, salt, and pepper. Cook for 2 minutes until fragrant.
Remove from heat, stir in cilantro and peas. Let the filling cool completely.
For the pastry: Mix flour, salt, oil, and water into a smooth dough. Rest for 20 minutes. Roll thin, lightly toast on a dry pan, and cut into strips.
Fold a strip into a cone, fill with 1-2 tbsp of mixture, and seal with the flour-water paste.
Deep fry in oil at 350°F (180°C) for 3-5 minutes until golden brown and crispy.
Drain on paper towels and serve hot with fresh lime wedges.

name: Suya
ingredients: 1 kg (2.2 lbs) Beef (Sirloin, Flank, or Topside)
instructions: Slice the beef against the grain into very thin, wide fillets (about 1/8 inch thick).
Mix ground peanuts, ginger, garlic, onion powder, cayenne, paprika, bouillon, and salt to make the Yaji (Suya spice).
Thread the beef strips onto soaked skewers in a zigzag fashion.
Brush meat with oil, then coat heavily with the Yaji spice mix, pressing it into the meat.
Marinate for at least 30 minutes.
Grill over medium-high heat for 15-20 minutes, turning frequently, or bake at 400°F (200°C) for 15-20 minutes.
Brush with oil and sprinkle extra spice 5 minutes before finishing.
Serve with sliced red onions, tomatoes, and extra Yaji spice.
serving_size: 4-6 people (3-4 skewers per person as a snack)

name: Mandazi
ingredients: 1 to 1 1/2 cups Warm milk or coconut milk
instructions: Whisk together flour, sugar, baking powder, salt, and cardamom in a large bowl.
Mix the egg, melted butter, and 1 cup of warm milk in a separate bowl.
Combine wet and dry ingredients. Gradually add more milk if needed to form a soft, smooth, non-sticky dough.
Knead the dough for 5-10 minutes until smooth and elastic.
Cover and let the dough rest for 30-60 minutes.
Divide dough into portions, roll to 1/2 inch thick, and cut into triangles or squares.
Deep fry in oil at 350°F (180°C) for 2-3 minutes per side until golden brown.
Drain on paper towels and serve warm with tea or coffee.

name: Kofta Balls
ingredients: 1 small onion, grated or finely minced
1 tbsp Baharat (or 1 tsp cumin, 1/2 tsp coriander, 1/2 tsp cinnamon, 1/2 tsp allspice)
instructions: Preheat oven to 425°F (220°C) and grease a baking sheet.
In a bowl, combine meat, onion, garlic, parsley, breadcrumbs, egg, and all spices. Mix until just combined.
Shape into balls about the size of a golf ball (should make 16-20).
Place on baking sheet and bake for 12-15 minutes until browned and cooked through (160°F internal).
Let rest for 5 minutes before serving.
Serve with pita bread, hummus, or a fresh salad.

name: Lentil Soup
ingredients: Salt and black pepper to taste
instructions: Heat olive oil in a large pot. Sauté onion, carrots, and potato for 5-7 minutes until softened.
Stir in garlic, cumin, and turmeric. Cook for 1 minute until fragrant.
Add rinsed lentils and broth. Bring to a boil, then reduce heat to low.
Cover and simmer for 20-30 minutes until lentils and vegetables are tender.
Optional: Use an immersion blender to puree the soup until smooth.
Stir in lemon juice, salt, and pepper. Adjust consistency with water if too thick.
Garnish with parsley and serve with warm pita or lemon wedges.

name: Sambousek
ingredients: 1 tsp Lebanese Seven Spices (or allspice/cinnamon mix)
instructions: Prepare dough: Mix flour and salt, rub in oil, then gradually add water while kneading until smooth. Rest for 30 minutes.
Prepare filling: Sauté pine nuts until golden, set aside. Cook onion until translucent, then add meat and cook until browned.
Stir in spices, salt, and pomegranate molasses. Let filling cool completely before adding pine nuts.
Roll out dough very thin (1/8 inch) and cut into 3-4 inch circles.
Place 1 tbsp of filling in center, fold into a half-moon, and seal edges by pinching or using a fork.
Deep fry in oil at 350°F (175°C) for 2-4 minutes until golden and bubbly.
Drain on paper towels and serve warm.

name: Mutabbaq
ingredients: 1 cup spring onions or leeks, finely chopped
1 tsp cumin, 1/2 tsp turmeric, 1 tbsp coriander powder
1/2 tsp black pepper, 1/2 tsp red chili powder, salt to taste
instructions: Dough: Mix flours and salt, rub in oil, and gradually add water while kneading for 10 minutes. Divide into 6 balls, coat with oil, and rest for 30-60 minutes.
Filling: Sauté garlic and onions, add meat and spices. Cook until browned and dry. Add tomatoes, cook 2 minutes, then let cool completely.
Assembly: Whisk eggs, mix with cooled meat and spring onions. Roll dough ball until translucent thin. Place filling in center.
Fold dough edges over the filling to form a square pocket.
Fry on a lightly oiled griddle over medium-low heat for 3-4 minutes per side until golden and the egg inside is cooked.
Serve hot, cut into squares, with lemon wedges and green chilies.

name: Spicy Chickpeas
ingredients: 2 cans (15 oz each) chickpeas, drained and rinsed
Salt and black pepper to taste
Fresh cilantro or parsley for garnish
instructions: Sauté onion in olive oil until softened (5 minutes). Add garlic and cook for 1 minute.
Stir in tomato paste and all spices (paprika, cumin, chili, turmeric, coriander). Cook for 1-2 minutes.
Add chickpeas, diced tomatoes, and broth. Bring to a simmer.
Reduce heat and cook for 10-15 minutes until sauce thickens.
Stir in lemon juice and season with salt and pepper.
Serve hot, garnished with fresh herbs, as a side or over couscous.

name: Zaalouk
ingredients: 3-4 large ripe tomatoes, grated or diced
Juice of 1/2 lemon or 1 tsp white vinegar
instructions: Heat olive oil in a skillet. Add cubed eggplant and 1/4 cup water. Cover and cook for 10-15 minutes until soft.
Add tomatoes, garlic, paprika, cumin, salt, and pepper.
Simmer uncovered for 10-15 minutes, mashing the eggplant and tomatoes with a spoon into a jam-like consistency.
Once thickened and liquid has evaporated, stir in fresh herbs and lemon juice.
Cook for 1-2 more minutes, then transfer to a bowl.
Serve warm or at room temperature, drizzled with olive oil, with crusty bread.

name: Mini Meat Pies
ingredients: 1 tbsp Cornflour (cornstarch) mixed with 2 tbsp water
2 sheets Frozen shortcrust pastry, thawed (for the bases)
2 sheets Frozen puff pastry, thawed (for the lids)
instructions: Sauté: Heat oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat. Add the onion and cook until softened (about 3 minutes).
Brown the Meat: Add the ground beef and cook, breaking it up with a wooden spoon, until browned. Drain any excess fat.
Simmer: Stir in the beef stock, tomato sauce, and Worcestershire sauce. Season with salt and pepper. Bring to a simmer for 5–10 minutes.
Thicken: Stir in the cornflour slurry. Continue to simmer for 1–2 minutes until the sauce is thick and glossy.
Cool: Remove from heat and let the filling cool completely.
Preheat: Preheat oven to 200°C (400°F). Grease a 12-hole muffin tin or mini pie moulds.
Cut the Bases: Use a round cutter to cut 12 circles from shortcrust pastry and press them into the tin.
Fill: Spoon the cooled meat mixture into each pastry case until nearly full.
Cut the Lids: Use a slightly smaller round cutter to cut 12 circles from puff pastry.
Seal: Place puff pastry lids over the filling and press the edges together to seal.
Bake: Brush with egg wash, poke a vent hole, and bake for 15–20 minutes until golden brown.

name: Prawn Skewers
ingredients: 2 ½ pounds extra-large prawns, peeled and deveined
¼ tsp crushed red pepper flakes (optional)
instructions: Prepare Skewers: If using wooden skewers, soak them in water for at least 15–30 minutes.
Make Marinade: In a large bowl, whisk together olive oil, lemon zest, juice, garlic, salt, pepper, and red pepper flakes.
Marinate: Add prawns to the marinade and toss well. Refrigerate for 15 minutes.
Thread: Remove prawns from marinade and thread 4 to 5 onto each skewer.
Grill: Preheat grill or grill pan to medium-high. Cook for 2–3 minutes per side until bright pink and opaque.
Serve: Garnish with fresh parsley and a squeeze of lemon. Serve immediately.

name: Fish Cakes
ingredients: 1 lb (450g) white fish fillets (cod, haddock, or pollock), cooked and flaked
2 large potatoes (about 2 cups), boiled and mashed
½ tsp dry mustard or Old Bay seasoning (optional)
2–3 tbsp vegetable or olive oil for frying
instructions: Combine: In a large bowl, mix mashed potatoes, flaked fish, egg, green onions, parsley, and seasonings.
Shape: Divide the mixture into 8 equal portions. Shape each into a patty about 1 inch thick.
Coat: Press each patty into Panko breadcrumbs, coating both sides evenly.
Chill: Refrigerate the patties for 30 minutes to help them firm up before frying.
Fry: Heat oil in a large non-stick skillet over medium heat. Fry for 3–4 minutes per side until golden brown.
Serve: Serve hot with tartar sauce and lemon wedges.

name: Sweet Potato Fries
ingredients: 2 tablespoons olive, avocado, or canola oil
instructions: Prep: Preheat oven to 425°F (220°C). Peel and cut sweet potatoes into 1/4 to 1/2 inch matchsticks.
Soak: Soak cut fries in cold water for 30 minutes, then drain and pat completely dry.
Coat: Toss dried fries with cornstarch, then add oil and spices and toss again to coat.
Arrange: Spread fries in a single layer on a parchment-lined baking sheet, ensuring they do not touch.
Bake: Bake for 25–30 minutes, flipping halfway through, until edges are browned and centers are tender.
Rest: Let fries rest for 5 minutes before serving to help them firm up.

name: Avocado Toast Bites
ingredients: 1 whole grain baguette, sliced into rounds
Salt and black pepper to taste
Optional Toppings: Everything bagel seasoning, red pepper flakes, crumbled feta, chopped walnuts, or cherry tomatoes
instructions: Toast: Preheat oven to 350°F (175°C). Bake baguette slices for 7–10 minutes until golden and crisp.
Prepare Avocado: Mash avocado with lime juice, salt, and pepper until creamy but slightly chunky.
Assemble: Spread a generous spoonful of avocado onto each toasted baguette slice.
Garnish: Top with your chosen ingredients (e.g., feta, walnuts, or seasoning).
Serve: Arrange on a platter and serve immediately.

name: Sushi Rolls
ingredients: Fillings: 1 cucumber (julienned), 1 avocado (sliced), 1/2 lb imitation crab or fish
For serving: Soy sauce, wasabi, pickled ginger
instructions: Rice: Rinse rice until water runs clear. Cook with 2 cups water, then let sit for 10 minutes.
Season: Fold in a mixture of rice vinegar, sugar, and salt into the warm rice. Let cool to room temperature.
Prepare Fillings: Slice vegetables and proteins into long, thin strips.
Assemble: Place nori on a bamboo mat. Spread rice evenly, leaving a 1-inch border at the top.
Roll: Arrange fillings in the center and roll firmly using the mat. Seal the edge with a little water.
Slice: Use a sharp, damp knife to slice the roll into 6–8 pieces.

name: Poke Bowl
ingredients: 1 lb sushi-grade ahi tuna, cut into 1-inch cubes
Toppings: Avocado, cucumber, edamame, spicy mayo (mayo, sriracha, lime)
instructions: Marinate: Whisk soy sauce, vinegar, sesame oil, ginger, and garlic. Toss with tuna and green onions. Refrigerate 15–30 minutes.
Sauce: Mix mayonnaise, sriracha, and lime juice for spicy mayo.
Assemble: Divide rice into bowls. Top with marinated tuna.
Add Toppings: Arrange avocado, cucumber, and edamame around the fish.
Serve: Drizzle with spicy mayo and garnish with extra sesame seeds.

name: Teriyaki Chicken Skewers
ingredients: 2 lbs boneless, skinless chicken thighs, cut into 1-inch cubes
1 bunch scallions (cut into 1-inch pieces) or bell peppers
1 tbsp cornstarch mixed with 1 tbsp water
instructions: Sauce: Simmer soy sauce, sugar, water, honey, sesame oil, vinegar, garlic, and ginger until sugar dissolves.
Thicken: Whisk in cornstarch slurry and simmer until thickened. Reserve some sauce for basting.
Marinate: Pour cooled sauce over chicken and marinate for 30 minutes.
Assemble: Thread chicken and scallions/peppers onto soaked skewers.
Cook: Grill for 10–12 minutes, turning occasionally. Baste with reserved sauce in the last 2 minutes.
Finish: Cook until chicken reaches 165°F and garnish with sesame seeds.

name: Fusion Tacos
ingredients: Shredded cabbage or kimchi, cilantro, and lime wedges
instructions: Marinate: Whisk soy sauce, sesame oil, sugar, garlic, and ginger. Marinate beef for 30 minutes.
Sauce: Mix sour cream, gochujang, and lime juice.
Cook: Sear beef in a hot skillet for 1–2 minutes per side until caramelized.
Warm: Briefly heat tortillas in a dry pan.
Assemble: Place beef on tortillas, top with cabbage/kimchi and cilantro. Drizzle with sauce.
Serve: Serve immediately with lime wedges.

name: Crispy Calamari
ingredients: 1 lb cleaned calamari tubes (cut into rings) and tentacles
Lemon wedges and marinara sauce for serving
instructions: Tenderize: Soak calamari in milk and salt for 30 minutes in the refrigerator.
Coating: Whisk together flour, cornstarch, oregano, pepper, and cayenne.
Heat Oil: Heat 2–3 inches of oil in a pot to 350°F–375°F.
Dredge: Drain calamari and toss in the flour mixture to coat. Shake off excess.
Fry: Fry in batches for 2–3 minutes until light golden and crispy.
Drain: Transfer to a wire rack and season with salt immediately. Serve with lemon and sauce.

name: Stuffed Bell Peppers
ingredients: 6 large bell peppers (any color), tops removed and seeds/ribs cleaned out
1 lb lean ground beef (or ground turkey/sausage)
1 ½ cups cooked white or brown rice
1 can (15 oz) tomato sauce or fire-roasted diced tomatoes
1 cup shredded mozzarella or sharp cheddar cheese
Salt and black pepper to taste
instructions: Preheat oven to 350°F (175°C).
Optional: Par-cook peppers by placing them in a baking dish with ¼ cup water, cover with foil, and bake for 10–15 minutes (or boil for 3 minutes).
In a large skillet over medium-high heat, brown the ground meat and diced onion until no longer pink. Drain excess grease.
Stir in minced garlic and cook for 1 minute.
Add cooked rice, half of the tomato sauce, Italian seasoning, garlic powder, salt, and pepper. Stir until well combined.
Stand peppers upright in a baking dish and spoon the filling generously into each.
Top each pepper with the remaining tomato sauce.
Cover with foil and bake for 25–30 minutes.
Remove foil, sprinkle with shredded cheese, and bake for another 5–10 minutes until cheese is melted.
Garnish with fresh parsley and serve warm.

name: Mini Pizza Bites
ingredients: ½ cup diced pepperoni (or sausage, olives, onions)
Marinara sauce or Ranch dressing for dipping
instructions: Preheat oven to 350°F (175°C). Grease a 12-cup muffin tray.
Cut each biscuit into 6 small pieces.
In a large bowl, toss biscuit pieces with olive oil, cheese, pepperoni, and seasonings until coated.
Divide the mixture equally into the muffin cups.
Bake for 15–20 minutes until golden brown and puffy.
Let cool for 2 minutes, then remove from pan. Serve warm with dipping sauce.

name: Spinach Cheese Puffs
ingredients: 1 package (17.3 oz) frozen puff pastry sheets, thawed (2 sheets)
1 package (10 oz) frozen chopped spinach, thawed and squeezed very dry
½ cup crumbled feta cheese (or shredded mozzarella)
½ tsp dried dill or oregano (optional)
Salt and black pepper to taste
Egg wash: 1 egg beaten with 1 tbsp water
instructions: Preheat oven to 400°F (200°C). Lightly grease a mini muffin tin.
Combine squeezed-dry spinach, cream cheese, feta, Parmesan, one egg, minced garlic, and seasonings in a bowl. Mix until creamy.
Unfold puff pastry sheets on a floured surface and cut each into 12 equal squares (3x4 grid).
Press each pastry square into a cavity of the muffin tin.
Spoon about 1 tablespoon of the spinach mixture into the center of each square.
Bring the four corners of the pastry together over the filling and pinch firmly to seal.
Brush the tops with egg wash.
Bake for 18–20 minutes until deep golden brown.
Cool in pan for 5 minutes before serving.

name: Baked Brie
ingredients: 1 wheel of Brie (8 oz to 16 oz)
2 tbsp honey, maple syrup, or fruit jam (fig, apricot, or raspberry)
1 sprig of fresh thyme or rosemary (optional)
Sliced baguette, sturdy crackers, apple slices, or grapes for serving
instructions: Preheat oven to 350°F (175°C).
Remove Brie from packaging. Optionally, lightly score the top in a crosshatch pattern.
Place Brie in a small, shallow ovenproof dish or on a parchment-lined baking sheet.
Bake for 12–15 minutes until the center feels very soft and starts to ooze slightly.
Immediately drizzle with honey or jam and sprinkle with fresh herbs.
Serve warm with accompaniments.

name: Crab Cakes
ingredients: 1 lb lump crabmeat, drained and picked for shells
½ cup panko breadcrumbs (or crushed saltine crackers)
2 tbsp butter or neutral oil for frying
Lemon wedges and tartar sauce for serving
instructions: Whisk egg, mayonnaise, Dijon mustard, Worcestershire sauce, Old Bay, parsley, and lemon juice in a large bowl.
Gently fold in crabmeat and panko breadcrumbs, being careful not to break up large lumps.
Shape mixture into 6 to 8 patties (about ½ cup each).
Chill patties in the refrigerator for at least 30 minutes to help them stay together.
Heat butter or oil in a large non-stick skillet over medium heat.
Cook crab cakes for 4–5 minutes per side until golden brown and heated through.
Serve immediately with lemon wedges and tartar sauce.

name: Chicken Satay (Fusion)
ingredients: 1.5 lbs boneless, skinless chicken thighs or breasts, cut into 1-inch strips
¼ cup full-fat coconut milk (for marinade)
2 stalks lemongrass, white parts only, minced
12–16 wooden skewers, soaked in water for 30 minutes
Peanut Sauce: ½ cup creamy peanut butter, ½ cup coconut milk, 1 tbsp soy sauce, 1 tbsp lime juice, 1 tsp red curry paste (optional), 1 tbsp honey or brown sugar
instructions: Whisk together coconut milk, garlic, ginger, lemongrass, spices, soy sauce, fish sauce, and sugar in a large bowl.
Add chicken, toss to coat, and marinate for at least 1 hour (ideally 4–6 hours).
Thread marinated chicken onto pre-soaked skewers in a zigzag pattern.
Preheat grill or grill pan to medium-high and lightly oil the grates.
Grill skewers for 3–5 minutes per side until charred and cooked through (165°F/74°C).
To make sauce: Combine all peanut sauce ingredients in a small saucepan over low heat. Whisk until smooth and thickened.
Serve skewers warm with peanut sauce.

name: Zucchini Fritters
ingredients: ½ cup grated Parmesan or shredded Cheddar
2 tbsp fresh parsley or chives, finely chopped
2–3 tbsp olive oil or vegetable oil for frying
Sour cream or Greek yogurt for serving
instructions: Toss grated zucchini with ½ tsp salt in a colander; let sit for 10–15 minutes.
Squeeze zucchini in a clean towel or cheesecloth until very dry.
In a large bowl, combine eggs, flour, cheese, garlic, herbs, pepper, and remaining ½ tsp salt.
Stir in dried zucchini until well combined.
Heat oil in a large non-stick skillet over medium heat.
Drop 2 tablespoons of batter per fritter and flatten slightly.
Fry for 3–4 minutes per side until golden brown and crispy.
Drain on paper towels and serve warm with dipping sauce.

name: Eggplant Parmesan Bites
ingredients: 2 medium eggplants, peeled and cut into 1-inch cubes
3 large eggs, beaten with 2 tbsp water
Salt and black pepper to taste
Vegetable or canola oil for frying
1 cup marinara sauce, warmed, for dipping
instructions: Toss eggplant cubes with 1½ tsp salt in a colander; let sit for 30 minutes. Pat thoroughly dry.
Set up three bowls: 1) Flour with salt and pepper, 2) Beaten eggs, 3) Breadcrumbs mixed with Parmesan.
Coat each eggplant cube in flour, dip in egg wash, then roll in breadcrumb mixture.
Heat 3 inches of oil to 350°F (175°C) in a heavy pot.
Fry breaded cubes in batches for 5–8 minutes until golden and crispy.
Transfer to a paper towel-lined plate and sprinkle with salt.
Garnish with parsley and serve with warm marinara sauce.

name: Tuna Tartare
ingredients: 1 lb sushi-grade tuna fillet, cut into ¼-inch cubes
Wonton chips or crackers for serving
instructions: Cut the tuna into uniform ¼-inch cubes (freezing for 15 minutes first helps).
In a medium bowl, whisk together soy sauce, sesame oil, lime juice, ginger, and jalapeño.
Gently fold diced tuna into the dressing until coated.
If using avocado, fold it in gently just before serving.
Serve immediately or refrigerate for up to 1 hour.
Garnish with toasted sesame seeds and chives. Serve chilled with chips.

name: Stuffed Jalapeños
ingredients: 12 large jalapeño peppers, halved lengthwise, seeds and membranes removed
Salt and black pepper to taste
12 slices of bacon, cut in half crosswise
instructions: Preheat oven to 400°F (200°C). Line a baking sheet with parchment paper.
Slice jalapeños in half lengthwise and remove seeds/membranes (wear gloves!).
Mix cream cheese, cheddar, garlic powder, paprika, salt, and pepper in a bowl until smooth.
Fill each jalapeño half with about 1–2 tablespoons of the cheese mixture.
Wrap one half-slice of bacon around each pepper and secure with a toothpick if needed.
Place on baking sheet cheese-side up.
Bake for 18–22 minutes until bacon is crispy and cheese is bubbly.
Let cool for 5 minutes before serving.

name: Crispy Pork Belly
ingredients: 2 lb (1 kg) slab of skin-on pork belly
1/2 lb (200g) rock salt (for the salt crust)
instructions: Prick hundreds of tiny holes all over the pork skin using a sharp skewer or meat tenderizer, being careful not to pierce the fat or meat.
Flip the pork and rub the meat side with Shaoxing wine, five-spice powder, sea salt, and white pepper.
Place the pork on a plate, skin-side up, and refrigerate uncovered for at least 12 hours to air-dry the skin.
Preheat oven to 350°F (180°C). Create a foil 'box' around the pork, leaving only the skin exposed.
Brush the skin with white vinegar and cover with a 1/4-inch layer of rock salt.
Roast for 60 minutes. Remove from oven and discard the hardened salt crust.
Increase oven temperature to 450°F (230°C) or turn on the broiler.
Place pork on a wire rack and roast/broil for 15-25 minutes until the skin is bubbled and crispy.
Rest for 15 minutes before slicing skin-side down.

name: Tonkatsu
ingredients: 2 boneless pork chops (loin or rib-end), 3/4 to 1 inch thick
Salt and freshly ground black pepper
For sauce: 1 tbsp Worcestershire sauce, 1/2 tbsp ketchup, 1/2 tbsp soy sauce, pinch of sugar
instructions: Pound pork chops to an even 1/2-inch thickness. Score the connective tissue between meat and fat to prevent curling.
Season both sides with salt and pepper.
Dredge the pork in flour, shaking off excess.
Dip into the beaten egg, then press firmly into the Panko to coat evenly.
Fry for 3-4 minutes per side until deep golden brown.
Drain on a wire rack for 1-2 minutes, then slice into strips.
Whisk sauce ingredients together and serve with shredded cabbage and lemon.

name: Pork Schnitzel
ingredients: 4 boneless pork loin chops or steaks (6 oz each)
Lemon wedges and fresh parsley for serving
instructions: Place each pork chop between plastic wrap and pound to 1/4 inch thickness.
Season both sides with salt and pepper.
Prepare three bowls: flour with salt, beaten eggs, and breadcrumbs.
Dredge each cutlet in flour, then egg, then breadcrumbs.
Heat oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat.
Fry cutlets in batches for 2-3 minutes per side until golden brown and crispy.
Drain on paper towels and serve immediately with fresh lemon juice.

name: Pork Nuggets
ingredients: 1.5 to 2 lbs pork loin or tenderloin, cut into bite-sized pieces
1.5 cups crushed cheese crackers (e.g., Cheez-Its) or corn flakes
4 eggs whisked with 1/4 cup water or buttermilk
Salt, pepper, garlic powder, and smoked paprika to taste
Vegetable or canola oil for frying
instructions: Prepare crumbs by pulsing crackers or corn flakes in a food processor until fine.
Season pork pieces with salt, pepper, and desired spices.
Dip each piece into the egg mixture, then roll in the crumbs until fully coated.
To fry: Heat 1.5 inches of oil to 350°F and fry for about 3 minutes until internal temperature is 145°F.
To air fry: Cook at 375°F for 6-8 minutes.
To bake: Cook at 400°F on a parchment-lined sheet for 10-12 minutes.
Serve warm with dipping sauces like BBQ or honey mustard.

name: Breaded Pork Chops
ingredients: 4 boneless or bone-in pork loin chops, 1-inch thick
2 large eggs, beaten with 1 tbsp water
1 tsp garlic powder, 1/2 tsp onion powder, 1/2 tsp paprika
Salt and black pepper to taste
