instructions: Pat pork chops dry and season with salt and pepper.
Mix flour with garlic powder, onion powder, and paprika in a shallow bowl.
Set up breading station: flour mixture, egg wash, and breadcrumbs.
Dredge each chop in flour, dip in egg wash, and coat thoroughly with breadcrumbs.
Heat oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat.
Fry for 4-6 minutes per side until golden brown and internal temperature is 145°F (63°C).
Rest for 3-5 minutes before serving.

name: Chicharrón
ingredients: 3 to 4 lbs skin-on pork belly, cut into 1-inch strips or 1.5-inch cubes
1 tsp baking soda (optional for extra blistered skin)
2 cups pork lard or neutral oil for frying
Lime wedges and hot sauce for serving
instructions: Score the skin side of the pork belly in a crosshatch pattern without cutting through to the meat.
In a large pot, combine pork, water, salt, garlic, and bay leaves. Bring to a boil, then simmer for 40-50 minutes until water evaporates.
Remove pork and pat extremely dry with paper towels.
Heat lard or oil in the pot to 375°F (190°C).
Carefully fry pork in batches for 10-12 minutes, using a splatter screen, until skin is golden and blistered.
Drain on a wire rack and sprinkle with salt immediately.
Serve with fresh lime wedges.

name: Lechon Kawali
instructions: Boil pork belly in a pot with enough water to submerge, plus garlic, onion, bay leaves, peppercorns, and salt.
Simmer for 45-60 minutes until fork-tender. Drain and pat dry.
Rub the skin with extra salt and air-dry (refrigerate uncovered for 3+ hours or use a fan).
Heat oil to 350°F (175°C).
Carefully deep-fry the pork (skin side down first) for 7-10 minutes per side until skin is blistered.
Rest for 5-10 minutes before slicing. Serve with Lechon sauce or spicy vinegar.

name: Pork Karaage
ingredients: 200g (7 oz) pork loin or shoulder, bite-sized pieces
Marinade: 1/2 clove grated garlic, 1/2 knob grated ginger, 1/2 tbsp sake, 1 tsp soy sauce, pinch white pepper
Coating: 1 beaten egg, 2 tbsp all-purpose flour, 1 tbsp potato starch or cornstarch
Lemon wedges and Japanese mayonnaise for serving
instructions: Combine pork with marinade ingredients and let sit for 15-30 minutes.
Mix flour and starch in a shallow dish.
Knead the beaten egg into the marinated pork.
Dredge each piece of pork in the flour-starch mixture, shaking off excess.
Fry in batches for 4-5 minutes until golden brown.
Drain on a wire rack and serve hot with lemon.

name: Pork Milanese
ingredients: 4 boneless pork loin chops (5 oz each)
Salt and black pepper to taste
Arugula salad and lemon wedges for serving
instructions: Pound pork chops between plastic wrap to 1/4-inch thickness. Season with salt and pepper.
Set up breading station: flour, egg wash, and breadcrumbs (mixed with Parmesan if using).
Dredge chops in flour, dip in egg, and coat with breadcrumbs.
Heat oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat.
Fry cutlets for 2-3 minutes per side until deep golden brown.
Drain on paper towels and sprinkle with salt.
Serve topped with a light arugula salad and lemon wedges.

name: Crispy Pork Strips
ingredients: 1 lb boneless pork chops or pork tenderloin, cut into 1/2-inch strips
Vegetable or canola oil for frying
Dipping sauce (e.g., Tonkatsu sauce, ranch, or honey mustard)
instructions: Soak pork strips in buttermilk for at least 30 minutes in the refrigerator.
Take each strip from the buttermilk and coat thoroughly in Panko, pressing to adhere.
Heat 1/2 inch of oil in a large frying pan over medium-high heat.
Fry in small batches for about 2 minutes per side until golden brown.
Drain on a wire rack or paper towels.
Serve immediately with your favorite dipping sauce.

name: Pork Sinigang
ingredients: 2 lbs (1kg) Pork (belly, spare ribs, or shoulder), cut into 2-inch cubes
1 packet (approx. 40g) Tamarind Soup Base Mix or 4–5 tbsp tamarind paste
1 bunch String Beans (Sitaw), cut into 2-inch lengths
1 bunch Kangkong (Water Spinach) or baby spinach/bok choy
instructions: In a large pot, combine the pork and water. Bring to a boil and skim off any foam.
Add the onions, tomatoes, and fish sauce. Lower the heat, cover, and simmer for 30–45 minutes until the pork is tender.
Add the taro (gabi) and simmer for about 8–10 minutes until it starts to soften.
Stir in the tamarind soup mix or paste. Taste and adjust seasoning.
Add the radish and finger chilies; simmer for 2 minutes.
Add the string beans, okra, and eggplant; simmer for another 3–5 minutes.
Add the kangkong (or spinach), turn off the heat, and cover the pot for 1 minute to let the greens wilt.
Serve hot with steamed white rice.

name: Pork Nilaga
ingredients: 1 kg (2.2 lbs) pork belly, ribs, or shoulder, cut into chunks
2 tbsp fish sauce (patis) or salt to taste
2 ears of sweet corn, cut into pieces (optional)
1 small head of cabbage, cut into wedges
1 bunch of pechay (bok choy), ends trimmed
instructions: In a large pot, combine pork and water. Bring to a boil and skim off the scum.
Add onion, peppercorns, and fish sauce. Simmer for 45–60 minutes until pork is tender.
Add corn, potatoes, and saba bananas. Cook for 10–12 minutes until potatoes are soft.
Add green beans, cabbage, and pechay. Simmer for 2–3 minutes.
Season with more fish sauce or salt if needed.
Serve hot with steamed rice and a side of fish sauce with calamansi and chili.

name: Pork Tinola
ingredients: 500g to 1kg pork ribs or pork belly, cut into bite-sized pieces
2 tbsp ginger, sliced into thin strips
1 large sayote (chayote) or green papaya, cut into wedges
1 cup chili leaves (sili leaves) or malunggay (moringa) leaves
Salt and black pepper to taste
instructions: Heat oil in a large pot. Sauté ginger, garlic, and onion until fragrant.
Add the pork pieces and sauté until lightly browned.
Pour in the fish sauce and add black pepper. Stir for 1–2 minutes.
Pour in water (or rice washing) and add the broth cube. Bring to a boil, then simmer for 30–40 minutes until pork is tender.
Add sayote or green papaya wedges and simmer for 5–8 minutes.
Stir in the chili or malunggay leaves, let wilt for 1 minute with heat off.
Serve hot with steamed white rice.

name: Pork Menudo
ingredients: 2 lbs pork shoulder, cut into small cubes
1 cup tomato sauce or 2 chopped tomatoes
Salt, pepper, and sugar to taste
instructions: Marinate pork in soy sauce and lemon juice for 30 minutes.
Sauté garlic, onion, and tomatoes in oil until softened.
Add marinated pork and brown slightly.
Pour in tomato sauce and water. Add bay leaves. Simmer for 40–60 minutes until pork is tender.
Add liver and hotdogs. Cook for 5 minutes.
Add potatoes, carrots, and raisins. Simmer for 10–15 minutes until tender.
Add bell peppers, chickpeas, and green peas. Cook for 3–5 minutes.
Season with salt, pepper, and sugar. Serve hot with rice.

name: Pork Caldereta
ingredients: 1 kg (2.2 lbs) Pork (ribs, shoulder, or belly), cut into pieces
1 tbsp Fish sauce (patis) or salt to taste
instructions: Marinate pork in pineapple juice, soy sauce, and pepper for 30–60 minutes.
Fry potatoes and carrots until edges are brown; set aside.
In the same pot, sear the pork until browned; remove and set aside.
Sauté garlic and onion. Add liver spread and tomato sauce.
Return pork to the pot and add water/marinade. Simmer for 40–50 minutes until tender.
Stir in peanut butter and chili peppers. Season with fish sauce.
Add fried potatoes, carrots, and bell peppers. Simmer for 5–8 minutes.
Top with grated cheese and serve hot with white rice.

name: Pork Stew
ingredients: 2 ½ lbs Pork Shoulder, cut into 1-inch cubes
1 tsp Salt & ½ tsp Black pepper
instructions: Dredge pork in flour, salt, and pepper. Brown in oil in a large pot; set aside.
Sauté onion and celery for 5 minutes. Add garlic and cook for 1 minute.
Deglaze with wine or broth. Stir in tomato paste, herbs, and bay leaves.
Return pork to pot and add broth. Simmer for 60 minutes.
Add potatoes and carrots. Simmer for 30–45 minutes until tender.
Stir in frozen peas and cook for 5 minutes.
Remove bay leaves and adjust seasoning before serving.

name: Pork Pozole
ingredients: 3 lbs pork shoulder, cut into 1-inch cubes
4 dried ancho chiles, stems and seeds removed
4 dried guajillo chiles, stems and seeds removed
2 cans (25 oz each) white hominy, drained and rinsed
Garnishes: Shredded cabbage, sliced radishes, diced onion, cilantro, lime wedges, avocado
instructions: Simmer pork, water, halved onion, garlic, bay leaves, and salt in a large pot for 1.5 to 2 hours.
Toast dried chiles, soak in hot water for 15 minutes, then blend with 1 cup broth, garlic, cumin, and oregano until smooth. Strain the sauce.
Remove onion halves and bay leaves from the pot. Add the chile sauce and hominy.
Simmer for another 30–45 minutes.
Serve in deep bowls and provide garnishes on the side for customization.

name: Pork Gumbo
ingredients: 1 to 1.25 lbs Pork shoulder or tenderloin, cut into cubes
instructions: Brown pork and sausage in a large pot; set aside.
Make a dark roux by whisking flour and oil over medium-low heat for 30–45 minutes until chocolate-colored.
Add onion, bell pepper, and celery to the roux. Sauté for 5–8 minutes, then add garlic.
Slowly whisk in stock. Add browned meats, tomatoes, seasoning, thyme, and bay leaves.
Simmer for 45–60 minutes until pork is tender.
Stir in okra and simmer for 15–20 minutes.
Remove bay leaves and serve over hot white rice.

name: Pork Ramen
ingredients: Garnishes: Green onions, cilantro, peanuts, lime wedges, soft-boiled eggs
instructions: Whisk together soy sauce, water, sesame oil, canola oil, honey, hoisin, peanut butter, vinegar, garlic, ginger, and chili paste.
Sear sliced pork in a large skillet until browned and cooked through.
Pour the sauce over the pork and stir to coat.
Cook ramen noodles in boiling water for 3 minutes; drain.
Toss noodles with the pork and sauce in the skillet.
Divide into bowls and add desired garnishes.

name: Pork Pho
ingredients: 1 lb pork tenderloin (half sliced thinly, half coarsely chopped)
1 bunch fresh cilantro (stems and leaves separated)
Garnishes: Lime wedges, bean sprouts, sliced red chiles, hoisin, sriracha
instructions: Brown bones (if using) in a pot. Sauté garlic, carrots, celery, onion, and ginger.
Add chopped pork, cilantro stems, salt, bay leaf, coriander seeds, star anise, and water. Simmer for 1 hour.
Strain the broth and discard solids. Bring clear broth back to a boil.
Add thinly sliced pork and rice vermicelli; cook for 8–10 minutes until tender.
Ladle into bowls and serve with fresh garnishes.

name: Pork Adobo
ingredients: 2 lbs (1kg) pork belly or pork shoulder, cut into 1.5-inch cubes
1 to 1 1/2 cups water or beef broth
instructions: Heat oil in a large pot over medium-high heat. Brown the pork cubes on all sides.
Add crushed garlic and sauté for 1-2 minutes until fragrant.
Pour in soy sauce, water (or broth), peppercorns, and bay leaves. Bring to a boil.
Lower heat, cover, and simmer for 40-50 minutes until pork is fork-tender.
Add vinegar and let boil for 2-3 minutes without stirring to cook off the raw acidity.
Add sugar (if using), stir, and simmer uncovered for 5-10 minutes until sauce reduces to desired thickness.
Remove bay leaves and serve hot over steamed white rice.
serving_size: 4-5 people (approx. 1 cup of pork and sauce per person)

name: Sweet and Sour Pork
ingredients: 1 lb (450g) pork loin or tenderloin, cut into 1-inch cubes
1 tbsp cornstarch mixed with 2 tbsp water (slurry)
instructions: Marinate pork with 1 tbsp soy sauce, wine, and salt for 15-20 minutes.
Dip pork in beaten egg, then coat with cornstarch. Deep fry at 350°F (175°C) until golden brown (5-8 mins). Drain and set aside.
Whisk water/juice, vinegar, ketchup, sugar, and 1 tbsp soy sauce in a bowl.
Stir-fry onion and bell peppers for 2-3 minutes. Add pineapple and toss.
Pour sauce mixture into the wok. Stir in cornstarch slurry and simmer until thickened and glossy.
Add fried pork back to the wok. Toss quickly to coat and serve immediately.

name: Pork Teriyaki
ingredients: 1 lb (450g) pork tenderloin or loin, cut into 3/4-inch pieces
2 cups mixed vegetables (bell peppers, snow peas, broccoli, carrots)
1 tbsp cornstarch mixed with 1 tbsp cold water (slurry)
Sesame seeds and green onions for garnish
instructions: Whisk together soy sauce, brown sugar, honey, garlic, ginger, and rice vinegar in a bowl.
Heat 1 tbsp oil in a skillet over medium-high heat. Brown pork for 5-7 minutes until it reaches 145°F (63°C). Remove and set aside.
Add remaining oil and stir-fry vegetables for 3-5 minutes until tender-crisp.
Return pork to skillet. Pour sauce over the mixture.
Stir in cornstarch slurry. Cook for 1-2 minutes until sauce thickens into a glossy glaze.
Garnish with sesame seeds and green onions. Serve with rice.

name: Char Siu
ingredients: 2 lbs (1 kg) pork shoulder or pork butt, cut into long strips 2 inches thick
2 tbsp honey (plus 1-2 tbsp extra for glaze)
2 cubes red fermented bean curd + 1 tbsp liquid (or red food coloring)
instructions: Whisk marinade ingredients. Marinate pork for 12-48 hours in the refrigerator.
Preheat oven to 400°F (200°C). Line a tray with foil, place a rack on top, and add water to the bottom of the pan.
Roast pork on the rack for 20 minutes.
Simmer reserved marinade for 3 minutes, then stir in extra honey. Baste pork, flip, and roast for another 15-20 minutes.
Broil for 2-4 minutes until edges are charred and sticky (internal temp 145°F).
Rest for 10 minutes, then slice against the grain.

name: Honey Garlic Pork
ingredients: Salt and black pepper to taste
instructions: Season pork chops with salt and pepper.
Heat oil and butter in a skillet. Sear pork chops for 4-5 minutes per side until golden and 145°F (63°C). Remove and keep warm.
Reduce heat to medium. Sauté garlic in the pan for 30-60 seconds.
Whisk in honey, vinegar, soy sauce, and red pepper flakes. Simmer for 3-5 minutes until thickened.
Return pork to the pan and spoon glaze over the meat.
Garnish with green onions and serve.

name: Orange Pork
ingredients: 2 lbs pork shoulder, cut into 1-inch cubes
1/4 cup cane or apple cider vinegar
Green onions and sesame seeds for garnish
instructions: Toss pork with salt and pepper. Let rest for 20 minutes.
Combine water, vinegar, and soy sauce in a saucepan and boil. Add garlic powder and brown sugar. Simmer until reduced by half.
Heat oil. Dredge pork in cornstarch and deep fry for 5-8 minutes until golden and crisp. Drain.
Toss fried pork in the glaze until evenly coated.
Garnish with green onions and sesame seeds. Serve over rice.

name: Pork Marsala
ingredients: 1 lb pork tenderloin (cut into 1-inch medallions) or 4 boneless chops
instructions: Season and dredge pork in flour. Sear in olive oil and 1 tbsp butter for 3-4 minutes per side. Remove and keep warm.
Sauté mushrooms in remaining 2 tbsp butter for 5-6 minutes. Add shallot/garlic and cook for 1 minute.
Pour in Marsala and broth, scraping the bottom. Simmer for 3-5 minutes until reduced by half.
Stir in heavy cream if using. Simmer for 1 minute.
Return pork to skillet and simmer for 1-2 minutes until heated through.
Garnish with parsley and serve.

name: Pork in Black Bean Sauce
ingredients: 1 lb (450g) lean pork, cut into thin strips
instructions: Whisk black bean paste, oyster sauce, soy sauce, sugar, stock, and cornstarch.
Stir-fry pork in 1 tbsp oil for 3-4 minutes until browned. Remove and set aside.
Heat remaining oil. Stir-fry garlic, ginger, peppers, and green onions for 2 minutes.
Return pork to pan. Pour sauce mixture over ingredients.
Cook, stirring constantly, for 1-2 minutes until sauce thickens.
Serve immediately over rice or noodles.

name: Braised Pork Belly
instructions: Blanch pork in boiling water for 3-5 minutes. Drain, rinse, and pat dry.
Caramelize sugar in oil over low heat until amber syrup forms.
Add pork and stir-fry for 2-3 minutes to coat in caramel.
Add ginger, scallions, star anise, cinnamon, wine, and soy sauces.
Add water to cover meat. Simmer on low, covered, for 1.5 - 2 hours until tender.
Uncover and reduce sauce on medium-high until thick and syrupy.

name: Pork Kare-Kare
ingredients: 1.5 tbsp cornstarch dissolved in 3 tbsp water (slurry)
instructions: Boil pork hock in water with salt for 60-90 minutes until very tender. Reserve 4 cups broth.
Sauté garlic and onion in oil. Add tenderized pork and sauté for 2 minutes.
Pour in reserved broth and annatto water. Bring to a boil.
Stir in peanut butter until dissolved. Simmer for 5-8 minutes.
Slowly stir in cornstarch slurry until thickened. Season with salt and pepper.
Add eggplant and string beans, cook for 5 minutes. Add bok choy and cook for 2 minutes.
Serve hot with steamed rice and shrimp paste on the side.

name: Pork Fried Rice
ingredients: 1/2 lb pork tenderloin or boneless pork chops, diced into 1/2-inch cubes
3 cups cooked long-grain white rice (ideally day-old and cold)
2-3 tbsp vegetable, peanut, or grapeseed oil
1 tsp fresh ginger, grated or minced
3 green onions (scallions), white and green parts separated and sliced
1 cup frozen peas and carrots, thawed
Salt and black pepper to taste
instructions: Heat 1 tbsp oil in a large skillet or wok over medium-high heat. Season pork with salt and pepper, then stir-fry until browned and cooked through (3-4 mins). Remove and set aside.
Add a splash of oil to the pan. Pour in beaten eggs and scramble until just set. Remove and set aside with the pork.
Add remaining oil to the pan. Sauté the white parts of the green onions, garlic, and ginger for 30 seconds until fragrant.
Add peas and carrots, stirring for 1-2 minutes. Add cold rice and break up any clumps with a spatula. Stir-fry for 2-3 minutes until heated through.
Pour soy sauce, oyster sauce (if using), and sesame oil over the rice. Stir well to coat.
Add the cooked pork and scrambled eggs back to the pan. Toss everything together for 1 minute.
Taste and adjust seasoning. Stir in the green parts of the scallions and serve immediately.

name: Pork Biryani
ingredients: 2 lbs pork shoulder or loin, cut into 1-inch cubes
1 tsp turmeric, 1 tsp chili powder, 2 tsp garam masala
1/4 cup chopped mint leaves, 1/4 cup chopped coriander (cilantro)
2 1/2 cups Basmati rice, rinsed and soaked for 30 minutes
Whole spices: 2 bay leaves, 4 green cardamoms, 4 cloves, 1 cinnamon stick, 1 star anise
Optional: pinch of saffron soaked in 2 tbsp warm milk
instructions: Marinate: Mix pork with yogurt, ginger-garlic paste, spices (turmeric, chili, garam masala, salt), mint, and coriander. Marinate for at least 30 minutes.
Rice: Boil a large pot of water with whole spices and salt. Add soaked rice and cook until 70-80% done (still has a slight bite). Drain and set aside.
Pork Base: Heat oil/ghee in a heavy-bottomed pot. Sauté cumin seeds and onions until golden brown. Add tomatoes and cook until soft.
Add marinated pork and cook for 5-8 minutes until browned. Add 1/2 cup water, cover, and simmer until pork is tender (30-40 mins).
Layering (Dum): Spread partially cooked rice evenly over the pork layer. Drizzle saffron milk and reserved fried onions on top.
Seal the pot with a tight lid and cook on very low heat for 15-20 minutes.
Rest for 10 minutes, then gently fluff and mix before serving with raita.

name: Pork Paella
ingredients: 1 lb boneless pork loin, cut into 1-inch cubes
1 cup uncooked short-grain white rice (like Arborio or Bomba)
1 large red bell pepper, sliced into strips
instructions: Heat olive oil in a paella pan or large skillet over medium-high heat. Add pork, paprika, and oregano. Brown pork on all sides (3-5 mins), then remove and set aside.
Reduce heat to medium. Sauté onion and bell pepper for 2-3 minutes. Add garlic and crushed tomatoes, simmering for 4-5 minutes until thickened slightly.
Stir in the uncooked rice to coat. Pour in 1.5 cups of chicken broth. Cover and cook for 12-15 minutes, stirring occasionally.
Add the browned pork back to the skillet with the remaining 1.5 cups of broth. Cover and simmer for 15 minutes until rice is tender and liquid is absorbed.
Stir in the peas and cook for 2 more minutes.
Garnish with fresh parsley and serve warm.

name: Pork Jambalaya
ingredients: 1 lb pork tenderloin or shoulder, cut into 1-inch cubes
1/2 lb to 3/4 lb smoked sausage (Andouille or Kielbasa), sliced
Salt and black pepper to taste
instructions: Toss pork with salt and pepper. Heat oil in a Dutch oven over medium-high heat. Brown pork and sausage (8-10 mins), then remove to a plate.
In the same pot, sauté onion, bell pepper, and celery for 5-7 minutes until softened. Stir in garlic and Cajun seasoning for 30 seconds.
Stir in the rice until coated. Pour in the broth and return the meat and juices to the pot.
Bring to a boil, then reduce heat to low and cover tightly. Simmer for 20-25 minutes without lifting the lid.
Remove from heat and let sit, covered, for 5 minutes.
Fluff with a fork, garnish with green onions, and serve.

name: Pork Risotto
ingredients: 1 lb pork loin filet or tenderloin, cut into bite-sized pieces
1 1/2 cups sliced mushrooms (baby bella or button)
Salt and black pepper to taste
instructions: Melt 1 tbsp butter in a large pot over medium-high heat. Add onion, mushrooms, and pork. Cook for 10 mins until pork is mostly cooked. Remove and set aside.
Melt remaining 1 tbsp butter in the same pan. Add Arborio rice and stir for 1 minute until edges are translucent.
Add warm broth 1 cup at a time, stirring frequently. Wait for each cup to be absorbed before adding the next. This takes 20-25 minutes total.
When the last addition of broth is nearly absorbed, stir the cooked pork, mushrooms, and milk back into the pot.
Stir in Parmesan cheese until melted. Season with salt and pepper and serve immediately.

name: Pork Carbonara
ingredients: 6-8 oz Guanciale, Pancetta, or thick-cut Bacon, diced
4 large egg yolks + 1 whole large egg
1 cup freshly grated Pecorino Romano or Parmesan
instructions: Boil pasta in salted water until al dente. Reserve 1 cup of starchy pasta water before draining.
While pasta cooks, sauté diced pork in a large skillet over medium heat until crispy (8-10 mins). Turn off heat but keep pork and fat in the pan.
Whisk egg yolks, whole egg, cheese, and pepper in a bowl until a paste forms.
Add drained pasta to the skillet with the pork and toss to coat in fat.
With the skillet off the heat, pour in the egg mixture and 1/4 cup of reserved pasta water.
Toss vigorously to create a creamy emulsion, adding more pasta water as needed for consistency.
Serve immediately with extra cheese and pepper.

name: Pork Pesto Pasta
ingredients: 4 lean pork loin steaks or shoulder steaks, cut into 1cm strips
1 jar (approx. 190g) prepared green or red pesto
Salt and black pepper to taste
instructions: Season pork strips with salt and pepper. Sear in a large lidded pan until browned.
Add broccoli, half the pesto, dried pasta, and hot water. Stir to combine.
Bring to a boil, then reduce heat to low. Cover and simmer for 15-20 minutes until pasta is tender and liquid is absorbed.
Stir in the remaining pesto to freshen the flavor.
Sprinkle with grated cheese and cover for a minute to melt (or grill for 2-3 mins until golden).
Let stand for 2 minutes before serving.

name: Pork Mac and Cheese
ingredients: 1 tsp salt, 1/2 tsp black pepper, 1/2 tsp garlic powder
instructions: Preheat oven to 350°F (175°C) and grease a 9x13 inch dish.
Cook pasta until al dente, drain, and set aside. Toss pulled pork with BBQ sauce.
Melt butter in a saucepan, whisk in flour for 1-2 mins. Slowly whisk in milk until thickened (5-7 mins).
Remove from heat and stir in Velveeta and 2 cups of cheddar until smooth. Add seasonings.
Fold pasta into cheese sauce. Pour half into the dish. Layer BBQ pork on top, then the rest of the pasta.
Top with remaining cheddar and breadcrumbs. Bake for 20-25 minutes until bubbly.
Rest for 5-10 minutes before serving.

name: Pork Pilaf
ingredients: 1.5 lbs pork (shoulder or loin), cut into 1-inch cubes
2 cups Basmati or long-grain white rice, rinsed
1 whole head of garlic (unpeeled, outer skin removed)
3-4 cups hot water or chicken stock
Salt and black pepper to taste
instructions: Heat oil in a Dutch oven. Sear pork until browned (8-10 mins).
Add onions and sauté until golden. Stir in carrots and cook for 5 minutes.
Add cumin, paprika, turmeric, salt, and pepper. Pour in 1-2 cups hot water to cover and simmer for 20 minutes.
Spread rinsed rice in an even layer over the meat. Do not stir.
Carefully pour remaining hot water over a spoon to 1 inch above the rice. Push the whole garlic head into the center.
Cook uncovered over medium-high until surface water is absorbed. Poke holes in rice for steam.
Cover tightly, reduce heat to lowest, and steam for 20-25 minutes.
Rest for 10 minutes, remove garlic, then mix and serve.

name: Pork Garlic Rice
ingredients: 1 tbsp minced garlic + 1 tsp minced garlic (separated)
1/4 cup soy sauce (or tamari) + 1 tbsp soy sauce (separated)
1/2 cup frozen peas, 1/2 cup frozen corn
instructions: Heat oil in a pan. Sauté 1 tbsp garlic and ginger for 30 seconds. Add ground pork and brown.
Add rice wine vinegar, 1/4 cup soy sauce, and brown sugar. Cook for 5 mins until caramelized and sauce thickens. Remove and set aside.
In the same pan, heat a little oil and sauté 1 tsp garlic. Add peas and corn for 1-2 mins.
Add cold rice and stir-fry for 1 minute until heated through.
Remove from heat and stir in spring onion, 1 tbsp soy sauce, and sesame oil.
Serve caramelized pork over the garlic rice, topped with a fried egg if desired.
serving_size: 4 servings (Approx. 1/4 of total recipe per serving)

name: Pork Shawarma
ingredients: 1.5 lbs (700g) pork tenderloin or pork shoulder, sliced into thin strips
Juice of 1 lemon (or 2 tbsp white wine vinegar)
Salt and black pepper to taste
Shredded lettuce, sliced red onions, diced tomatoes, and sliced cucumbers for serving
Tzatziki or garlic yogurt sauce (1 cup Greek yogurt, 1 minced garlic clove, 1 tbsp lemon juice, and chopped parsley)
instructions: In a large bowl, whisk together the olive oil, lemon juice, minced garlic, and all the spices. Add the pork strips and toss until thoroughly coated. Cover and refrigerate for at least 2 hours (overnight is best).

Cook the pork using your preferred method: Stovetop: Sear in a large skillet over medium-high heat for 3–5 minutes until browned and cooked through. Oven: Roast on a baking sheet at 400°F (200°C) for 15–20 minutes. Grill: Cook on medium-high heat for 4–6 minutes per side.
While the pork is cooking, chop the vegetables and prepare the yogurt sauce.
Warm the pita bread slightly. Spread a generous spoonful of sauce in the center, add a portion of the cooked pork, and top with the fresh vegetables.
Fold or roll the pita tightly and serve immediately.

name: Pork Burrito
ingredients: 3 cups pulled pork (leftover or freshly made)
1 1/2 cups cooked rice (white, brown, or Mexican-style)
1 1/2 cups black beans or pinto beans, drained and rinsed
1 cup shredded cheese (Cheddar, Monterey Jack, or Mexican blend)
1 tbsp olive oil or vegetable oil
Optional toppings: Shredded lettuce, sour cream, salsa or pico de gallo, guacamole, fresh cilantro, and lime wedges
instructions: Heat the oil in a large skillet over medium heat. Sauté the onion and garlic for 2–3 minutes until fragrant, then add the bell peppers and cook for another 5 minutes until softened.
Add the pulled pork and beans to the skillet. Stir well and cook for 3–5 minutes until the mixture is heated through. Add a splash of water or broth if the pork is too dry.
Warm the tortillas in the microwave for 15–20 seconds or in a dry skillet over medium heat to make them pliable.
Assemble the burritos: Lay a warm tortilla flat, place about 1/4 cup of rice in the center, top with 1/2 cup of the pork and bean mixture, add cheese and desired cold toppings.
Fold the left and right sides of the tortilla inward, then roll tightly from the bottom toward the top.
Optional: Place the rolled burritos seam-side down in a hot, lightly oiled skillet for 1–2 minutes per side until golden brown and crispy.

name: Pork Taco
ingredients: 3–4 lbs pork shoulder (pork butt), cut into 2-inch chunks
Toppings: 1/2 cup finely diced white onion, 1/2 cup chopped fresh cilantro, salsa verde or pico de gallo, lime wedges
instructions: Place the pork chunks, onion, garlic, orange juice and rinds, lime juice, water, and all spices into a slow cooker. Cover and cook on Low for 8–10 hours or High for 5–6 hours until fork-tender.
Remove the pork from the liquid and discard the onion, garlic, and orange rinds. Shred the meat using two forks.
Heat a large skillet over medium-high heat with a little oil. Add the shredded pork in a single layer and pour about 1/2 cup of the cooking liquid over it.
Cook undisturbed for 3–5 minutes until the bottom is golden and crispy, then toss and cook for another 2 minutes.
Warm the tortillas in a dry skillet or over an open gas flame for 30 seconds per side until soft.
Assemble by placing a generous spoonful of crispy carnitas onto each tortilla, topped with diced onions, cilantro, and salsa. Serve with lime wedges.

name: Pork Quesadilla
ingredients: 1 tsp onion powder, 1 tsp dried oregano, 1 tsp paprika, 1/2 tsp cumin, 1/2 tsp salt, 1/4 tsp chili powder, 1/4 tsp black pepper
2 cups shredded Mexican cheese blend (or cheddar/jack)
Optional toppings: Sour cream, salsa, guacamole, or fresh cilantro
instructions: Heat the oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat. Sauté the onion and garlic for about 2 minutes until softened.
Add the ground pork and cook for 6–8 minutes, stirring to break the meat into small crumbles, until fully browned. Drain any excess fat.
Reduce heat to medium and stir in the tomato paste, water, and the entire spice blend. Simmer for 2–3 minutes until the sauce thickens and coats the meat evenly. Remove from heat.
Assemble the quesadillas: Place one tortilla on a flat surface, sprinkle 1/4 cup of cheese, top with 1/2 cup of pork filling, another 1/4 cup of cheese, and a second tortilla.
Cook in a clean non-stick skillet over medium-low heat for 2–3 minutes per side. Press down lightly with a spatula to help the cheese melt and the tortilla become golden and crisp.
Transfer to a cutting board, let rest for a minute, and cut into wedges. Serve with toppings.

name: Pork Sandwich
ingredients: Salt and fresh ground pepper to taste
1 large loaf crusty French bread (or 8 individual rolls)
Honey Mustard Sauce: 1/4 cup mayonnaise, 2 tbsp stone ground mustard, 1 tbsp honey, 1/8 tsp fresh ground pepper
instructions: Preheat oven to 350°F (175°C). Rub the pork loin with oil and season generously with salt and pepper.
Roast the pork in a roasting pan for approximately 1 hour or until internal temperature reaches 145°F (63°C). Let the meat rest for 10 minutes before slicing thinly.
While the pork is roasting, sauté the sliced onions in a pan with a little oil over medium-low heat until soft and caramelized (15–20 minutes).
Whisk together the mayonnaise, stone ground mustard, honey, and pepper in a small bowl.
Slice the bread open and lightly toast the interior under a broiler until golden.
Layer the sliced pork and provolone onto the bread. Broil for 1–2 minutes until the cheese is melted and bubbly.
Top with caramelized onions and honey mustard sauce. Close the sandwich, slice, and serve immediately.

name: Pulled Pork Burger
ingredients: 1 tsp salt and 1 tsp black pepper
8–10 Brioche or sesame seed buns, lightly toasted
Toppings: Coleslaw, sliced pickles, red onion, or jalapeños
instructions: Mix the brown sugar, paprika, garlic powder, onion powder, cumin, salt, pepper, and cayenne in a small bowl. Rub the mixture all over the pork shoulder.
Place the seasoned pork in a slow cooker and pour the vinegar, chicken broth, and Worcestershire sauce around it.
Cover and cook on Low for 8–10 hours or High for 5–6 hours until 'fall-apart' tender.
Remove the pork and shred it using two forks, discarding excess fat.
Drain most of the liquid from the slow cooker (leave about 1/2 cup), return the shredded pork to the pot, and stir in the BBQ sauce. Let it sit on Warm for 15–20 minutes.
Assemble the burgers by piling a generous amount of pulled pork onto toasted buns, topped with coleslaw and pickles.

name: Pork Sliders
ingredients: 9–12 slices of cheese (Cheddar, Pepper Jack, or Monterey Jack)
Butter Glaze: 1/4 cup melted butter, 2 tsp Worcestershire sauce, 1/2 tsp garlic salt, 1/2 tsp poppy seeds or sesame seeds
Optional Coleslaw: 9 oz coleslaw mix, 1/2 cup mayo, 1 tbsp Dijon mustard, 1 tbsp apple cider vinegar, 1 tsp onion powder
instructions: Preheat oven to 350°F (175°C). Slice the entire 12-pack of buns in half horizontally and place the bottom half in a 9x13-inch baking dish.
Spread the pulled pork evenly over the bottom buns, drizzle with BBQ sauce, and layer the cheese slices on top.
Place the top half of the buns over the cheese.
Whisk together the melted butter, Worcestershire sauce, garlic salt, and poppy seeds. Brush this glaze evenly over the tops of the buns.
Cover the dish with foil and bake for 20 minutes. Remove foil and bake for another 5 minutes until the cheese is bubbly and tops are golden.
Slice into individual sliders and serve immediately with pickles or coleslaw.

name: Pork Empanada
ingredients: Filling: 1 kg ground pork, 4 cloves garlic (minced), 2 white onions (minced), 2 medium potatoes (cubed small), 1 carrot (cubed small), 1 red bell pepper (finely chopped), 1 cup green peas, 1/2 cup water, 3 tsp sugar, salt and black pepper, 4 tbsp oil
Pastry: 6 cups all-purpose flour, 1/2 cup white sugar, 1 tsp baking powder, 1 tsp salt, 2 cups cold butter (cubed), 3/4 cup cold water
instructions: Prepare the filling: Sauté onions and garlic in oil, add ground pork and brown it. Add potatoes, carrots, and water; simmer until vegetables are tender. Stir in peas, bell peppers, and sugar, and cook until liquid evaporates. Cool completely.
Make the pastry: Whisk together dry ingredients, cut in the cold butter until it resembles coarse crumbs, then gradually add cold water and knead until a smooth dough forms.
Divide the dough into balls and refrigerate for 20 minutes.
Flatten a dough ball into a circle, place 2–3 tablespoons of filling in the center, fold over to a half-moon shape, and seal the edges by crimping with a fork or braiding.
Deep-fry the empanadas over medium-low heat until golden brown, then drain on paper towels.

name: Siopao Asado
ingredients: Filling: 1 lb (500g) pork shoulder or loin (diced small), 3 cloves garlic (minced), 1 small onion (chopped), 2 tbsp soy sauce, 2 tbsp oyster sauce, 1 tbsp hoisin sauce, 3 tbsp brown sugar, 2 star anise, 2 tbsp cornstarch in 1/4 cup water (slurry), 1 cup water, 1 tbsp oil
Dough: 1 cup warm water, 2 1/4 tsp active dry yeast, 1/2 cup white sugar, 3 1/2 cups all-purpose flour, 3 tbsp shortening or oil, 1 tbsp baking powder, 1/2 tsp salt
instructions: Prepare the filling: Sauté garlic and onions, add pork and brown. Add water, sauces, sugar, and star anise; simmer for 20–30 minutes until tender. Remove star anise, stir in cornstarch slurry to thicken, and cool completely.
Make the dough: Activate yeast in warm water with 1 tbsp sugar. Mix with remaining dough ingredients, knead for 8–10 minutes until smooth, and let rise for 1 to 1.5 hours until doubled.
Divide dough into 10–12 pieces, roll into balls, then flatten into disks with thinner edges.
Place 1–2 tablespoons of filling in the center, pinch the edges to seal, and place on a square of parchment paper. Let rest for 15–20 minutes.
Steam on medium heat for 15–20 minutes (add vinegar to water). Wait 2–3 minutes before opening the lid to prevent collapsing.

name: Pork Lumpia
ingredients: 1 tsp salt and 1/2 tsp ground black pepper
30-50 thin lumpia or spring roll wrappers
instructions: Prepare the filling: Combine ground pork, minced carrots, onion, garlic, eggs, soy sauce, salt, and pepper in a bowl. Mix thoroughly.
Separate the lumpia wrappers and keep them covered with a damp towel.
Wrap the lumpia: Place about 1 to 1 1/2 tablespoons of filling on a wrapper, shape into a thin log, fold the bottom and sides, and roll tightly toward the top. Seal with the beaten egg.
Heat oil to 350°F (175°C) and fry the lumpia in batches for 4–6 minutes until golden brown and cooked through.
Drain on paper towels and serve hot with sweet chili sauce.

name: Lechon
ingredients: Salt and black pepper to taste
Evaporated milk or lemon-lime soda (for glazing/basting)
instructions: Clean the pig thoroughly and pat dry with paper towels.
Rub the inside and outside of the pig generously with salt and pepper.
Fill the belly cavity with lemongrass, scallions, garlic, onions, bay leaves, and star anise.
Sew the belly shut with kitchen twine to keep the aromatics inside.
Insert a long bamboo or metal pole through the length of the pig.
Slow-roast over hot charcoal for 4 to 6 hours, rotating constantly to ensure even cooking.
During the last hour of roasting, baste the skin with evaporated milk or soda to achieve a crispy, reddish-brown finish.
Rest for 20-30 minutes before carving and serving.

name: Pork BBQ Skewers
ingredients: 3 lbs pork shoulder, cut into 1-inch cubes
Bamboo skewers, soaked in water for 30 minutes
instructions: In a large bowl, whisk together soy sauce, soda, sugar, garlic, ketchup, vinegar, oyster sauce, and pepper.
Add the pork pieces and toss to coat. Marinate in the refrigerator for at least 4-6 hours, or overnight.
Thread 4-5 pieces of pork onto each soaked bamboo skewer, alternating between lean and fatty pieces.
Preheat the grill to medium-high heat and lightly oil the grates.
Grill the skewers for 3-5 minutes per side, for a total of 10-12 minutes.
Baste with extra sauce (reserved marinade or a mix of ketchup and oil) during the last few minutes of grilling until glazed and slightly charred.
Serve hot with steamed rice and spiced vinegar.

name: Grilled Pork Chops
ingredients: 4 bone-in pork loin chops (1 to 1.5 inches thick)
instructions: Pat the pork chops dry with paper towels.
In a small bowl, mix the brown sugar, salt, paprika, garlic powder, onion powder, and pepper.
Rub the olive oil over both sides of the chops, then apply the dry rub generously.
Preheat the grill to medium-high (400°F).
Sear the chops for 2 minutes per side without moving them.
Reduce heat to medium and continue cooking for 4-7 minutes per side until the internal temperature reaches 145°F.
Transfer to a plate and let rest for 5-10 minutes before serving.

name: Inihaw na Baboy
ingredients: 2 lbs pork belly (liempo), sliced into 1/2 inch thick pieces
instructions: Combine soy sauce, ketchup, liquid seasoning, garlic powder, calamansi juice, and pepper in a bowl.
Add the pork belly and marinate for at least 3 hours or overnight in the refrigerator.
Prepare the grill for medium heat.
Grill each side of the pork for 5-8 minutes.
Baste with a mixture of soy sauce, ketchup, and oil every few minutes until charred and cooked through.
Rest for 5 minutes, then slice into bite-sized pieces and serve with spicy vinegar dipping sauce.

name: Pork Satay
ingredients: 1.5 lbs pork tenderloin, cut into 1-inch strips
For Peanut Sauce: 1/2 cup peanut butter, 1/2 cup coconut milk, 1 tbsp soy sauce, 1 tbsp lime juice, 1/2 tsp chili sauce
instructions: Whisk together coconut milk, soy sauce, sugar, garlic, and spices in a bowl.
Add pork, toss to coat, and marinate for 2-8 hours.
Thread the pork onto soaked bamboo skewers.
For the sauce: Combine peanut butter, coconut milk, soy sauce, lime juice, and chili sauce in a saucepan over low heat; whisk until smooth.
Grill skewers over medium-high heat for 3-4 minutes per side until charred and cooked through.
Serve the warm skewers with the peanut dipping sauce.

name: Roast Pork Belly
ingredients: 1/2 cup coarse sea salt (for the salt crust)
instructions: Poke hundreds of tiny holes in the skin with a skewer without piercing the meat.
Rub the Shaoxing wine and the spice mix (five-spice, salt, pepper) on the meat side only.
Place skin-side up on a plate and refrigerate uncovered for at least 6-24 hours to dry the skin.
Preheat oven to 350°F. Wrap the sides and bottom of the pork in foil to create a boat.
Brush skin with vinegar and cover with a thick layer of coarse salt. Roast for 60 minutes.
Remove salt crust, increase oven temp to 425°F (or broil), and roast for 20-30 minutes until skin is puffed and crispy.
Rest for 15 minutes, then slice skin-side down into pieces.

name: Peri-Peri Pork
instructions: In a blender, pulse the roasted pepper, lemon juice, vinegar, garlic, and spices into a smooth paste.
Coat the pork steaks with the marinade and refrigerate for at least 1 hour.
Heat a grill or skillet over medium-high heat with a little oil.
Grill the steaks for 4-5 minutes per side until the internal temperature reaches 145°F.
Rest for 5 minutes before serving with rice or salad.

name: Garlic Herb Pork Roast
ingredients: 1 tbsp each fresh rosemary, thyme, and parsley, chopped
instructions: Preheat oven to 375°F. Pat the roast dry.
In a small bowl, mix garlic, herbs, oil, salt, pepper, and onion powder into a paste.
Rub the herb paste all over the pork roast.
Place the roast fat-side up in a roasting pan. Pour chicken broth into the bottom of the pan.
Roast uncovered for 50-70 minutes until the internal temperature reaches 145°F.
Transfer to a board, tent with foil, and let rest for 10-15 minutes before slicing.

name: Honey BBQ Pork
instructions: Preheat oven to 300°F (for ribs) or 350°F (for tenderloin).
Whisk together BBQ sauce, honey, vinegar, garlic powder, onion powder, salt, and pepper.
Rub the glaze generously over the pork.
If roasting ribs, wrap in foil and bake for 2 hours; then unwrap, baste, and broil for 10 minutes.
If roasting tenderloin, sear in a pan first, then roast in the oven for 25-35 minutes, basting every 5 minutes.
Rest for 10 minutes before slicing and serving.

name: Smoked Pork Ribs
ingredients: 2 racks of pork ribs (Baby Back or St. Louis style)
instructions: Remove the membrane from the back of the ribs.
Coat ribs with mustard and apply the dry rub. Let sit for 30 minutes.
Smoke the ribs at 225°F for 3 hours, bone-side down.
Remove from smoker and wrap tightly in foil with butter, brown sugar, honey, and apple juice. Smoke for another 2 hours meat-side down.
Unwrap the ribs, place back on the grill meat-side up, and brush with BBQ sauce. Smoke for 1 final hour until the sauce is tacky.
Rest for 15 minutes before slicing between the bones.

name: Pork Salad
ingredients: 1 can (15 oz) black-eyed peas, rinsed and drained
instructions: Preheat oven to 350°F (175°C). Season pork with salt and pepper.
Bake pork for 30-40 minutes until internal temperature reaches 160°F (71°C).
Let pork rest for 10 minutes, then cut into thin slices.
In a small bowl, whisk together yogurt, cilantro, lime juice, oil, sugar, and additional salt to make the dressing.
Arrange salad greens, bell peppers, mushrooms, and black-eyed peas on a platter or in bowls.
Top with pork slices and drizzle with the cilantro-lime dressing.

name: Pork Lettuce Wraps
ingredients: 1 head of Butter, Bibb, or Iceberg lettuce
1 can (8 oz) water chestnuts, drained and diced
Sliced green onions and fresh cilantro for garnish
instructions: Rinse and dry lettuce leaves, keeping them whole for wrapping.
Heat oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat. Brown ground pork until crumbled and cooked through. Drain excess fat.
Add onion to the skillet and sauté for 2-3 minutes. Stir in garlic and ginger, cooking for 1 more minute.
Stir in water chestnuts and shredded carrots. Cook for 2 minutes.
In a small bowl, whisk hoisin, soy sauce, rice vinegar, and sesame oil. Pour over the pork mixture and stir to coat.
Spoon the mixture into lettuce leaves and garnish with green onions and cilantro.

name: Pork Stir-Fry
ingredients: 1 lb pork tenderloin, cut into thin strips
instructions: Whisk together soy sauce, honey, vinegar, ginger, garlic, cornstarch, and broth to make the sauce.
Heat 1 tbsp oil in a wok or large skillet. Sear pork strips for 3-5 minutes until browned. Remove and set aside.
Add remaining oil and stir-fry broccoli, carrots, bell pepper, and onion for 5-7 minutes.
Return pork to the pan. Re-whisk the sauce and pour it over the mixture.
Cook for 1-2 minutes until the sauce thickens and coats everything evenly.
Serve immediately, optionally over brown rice or cauliflower rice.

name: Pork Veggie Bowl
ingredients: 2 cups cooked brown rice or cauliflower rice
Sliced green onions and sesame seeds for garnish
instructions: Brown the ground pork in a large skillet over medium-high heat. Drain fat and set aside.
Sauté onions, carrots, and mushrooms in the same pan for 3-4 minutes until softened.
Add garlic and ginger, cooking for 1 minute until fragrant.
Return pork to the pan. Add cabbage (or spinach), soy sauce, vinegar, and honey.
Stir for 2-3 minutes until the greens are wilted and everything is well-combined.
Divide rice into bowls and top with the pork and vegetable mixture. Garnish with green onions and sesame seeds.

name: Pork Quinoa Bowl
ingredients: 1 lb pork tenderloin, sliced into thin strips
2 cups broccoli florets or snap peas
instructions: Combine quinoa and broth in a pot. Bring to a boil, then simmer covered for 15 minutes until liquid is absorbed. Fluff with a fork.
Whisk soy sauce, honey, and garlic. Marinate pork strips in half of the mixture for 10 minutes.
Sauté pork in olive oil over medium-high heat for 4-5 minutes until cooked through. Remove and set aside.
In the same skillet, sauté broccoli, pepper, and carrots for 3-4 minutes. Add spinach and remaining sauce, cooking until wilted.
Divide quinoa into bowls, top with vegetables and pork, and serve.

name: Pork Zoodle Bowl
ingredients: Red pepper flakes and cilantro for garnish
instructions: Brown ground pork with garlic, ginger, and red pepper flakes in a large pot with oil for about 5 minutes.
Pour in the stock and bring to a simmer. Let cook for 10-15 minutes.
Add bok choy, green onion whites, soy sauce, and fish sauce. Simmer for 5 more minutes.
Place raw zoodles in serving bowls.
Ladle the hot pork and broth over the zoodles; the heat will soften them slightly.
Garnish with green onion tops and cilantro.

name: Steamed Pork
ingredients: 100g (3.5 oz) lean ground pork or thinly sliced tenderloin
Optional: pinch of white pepper and a few drops of sesame oil
instructions: Mix pork with soy sauce, cornstarch, and white pepper. Massage well to ensure tenderness.
Spread the pork thinly onto a shallow heat-proof dish (less than 1 inch thick).
Top the pork evenly with julienned ginger.
Steam on medium-high heat for 20-30 minutes until the pork is thoroughly cooked.
Garnish with fresh scallions and a drop of sesame oil before serving.

name: Poached Pork
ingredients: 1 lb pork tenderloin, trimmed of fat
1.5 cups low-fat milk or unsweetened almond milk
Optional: 1 tbsp cornstarch mixed with 1 tbsp water for sauce
instructions: Season pork with salt and pepper. Optionally sear in a skillet with olive oil for 2-3 minutes per side.
Add milk and minced garlic to a pot. Bring to a very gentle simmer (do not boil).
Add pork, cover, and simmer gently for 20-25 minutes until internal temperature reaches 145°F (63°C).
Remove pork and let it rest for 10 minutes.
Optional: Thicken the poaching liquid with cornstarch slurry to make a garlic sauce.
Slice into medallions and serve with the sauce.

name: Pork Cabbage Salad
ingredients: 1/2 lb pork shoulder or tenderloin, poached and sliced
1/2 cup fresh mint or cilantro, chopped
instructions: Poach pork in water with salt and ginger for 15-20 minutes. Cool and slice into thin strips.
Combine cabbage, carrots, and onions in a large bowl. Soak onions in ice water first to mellow them.
Whisk fish sauce, sugar, lime juice, vinegar, and garlic to make the dressing.
Toss the vegetables and pork with the dressing and half the herbs. Let sit for 5-10 minutes.
Garnish with remaining herbs and crushed peanuts before serving.

name: Pork Avocado Bowl
ingredients: Sliced green onions and sesame seeds for garnish
instructions: Cook ground pork in a large skillet over high heat until browned.
Stir in gochujang, mirin, garlic, ginger, soy sauce, and sesame oil.
Cook for 5-10 minutes, stirring frequently, until the pork is slightly crispy and caramelized.
Divide rice into four bowls.
Top each bowl with the caramelized pork and half an avocado (sliced).
Garnish with green onions and sesame seeds.

name: Pork Pot Pie
ingredients: 2 ½ cups frozen mixed vegetables (peas, carrots, corn)
1 can (14.5 oz) chicken or vegetable broth
instructions: Preheat oven to 425°F (220°C). Lightly grease an 11x7-inch baking dish.
In a large saucepan over medium heat, melt the butter. Stir in the flour until smooth and cook for 1 minute.
Gradually whisk in the broth and milk. Bring to a boil, stirring constantly, and cook for 1–2 minutes until thickened.
Stir in the cubed pork, frozen vegetables, shredded cheese, salt, and pepper. Cook until cheese is melted.
Pour the mixture into the prepared baking dish.
Roll out the puff pastry to fit the top of the dish. Place over the filling and press edges to seal.
Cut a few small slits in the top of the pastry and brush with the beaten egg.
Bake for 18–22 minutes until the pastry is puffed and golden brown.
Let stand for 5 minutes before serving.

name: Baked Pork Chops
ingredients: 4 bone-in or boneless pork chops (1 to 1.5 inches thick)
instructions: Preheat oven to 400°F (200°C). Line a baking sheet with parchment paper or foil.
Pat the pork chops dry with paper towels.
In a small bowl, mix the brown sugar, paprika, garlic powder, onion powder, salt, and pepper.
Brush both sides of the pork chops with olive oil, then rub the spice mixture generously over both sides.
Place the chops on the baking sheet and bake for 15 to 25 minutes depending on thickness.
Pork is done when it reaches an internal temperature of 145°F (63°C).
Remove from oven, top each chop with a small pat of butter, and let rest for 5 to 10 minutes.

name: Pork Casserole
ingredients: 4-6 medium potatoes, peeled and sliced into 1/4-inch rounds
1 can (10.5 oz) cream of mushroom soup
Salt and black pepper to taste
instructions: Preheat oven to 375°F (190°C). Grease a 9x13-inch baking dish.
Season pork chops with salt, pepper, and half the garlic powder and paprika.
Brown pork chops in a skillet with butter or oil for 2-3 minutes per side. Set aside.
In the same skillet, whisk together the cream soup, broth, and remaining seasonings.
Layer the sliced potatoes, carrots, and onions in the baking dish. Pour half the gravy over them.
Place the pork chops on top and pour the remaining gravy over the meat.
Cover tightly with foil and bake for 45-50 minutes.
Remove foil, sprinkle with cheese if using, and bake for another 10-15 minutes until vegetables are tender.
Let rest for 5 minutes before serving.

name: Pork Lasagna
instructions: Preheat oven to 375°F (190°C).
Brown ground pork with onion in a skillet. Drain excess fat. Add garlic and tomato paste; cook for 1 minute.
Stir in marinara sauce, oregano, and basil. Simmer on low for 15-20 minutes.
In a bowl, combine ricotta, egg, Parmesan, and parsley.
Spread 1 cup of meat sauce in a 9x13 inch dish. Top with 3-4 noodles.
Layer with 1/3 of the ricotta mixture, 1 cup of mozzarella, and 1 cup of meat sauce. Repeat layers.
Finish with noodles, remaining sauce, and a generous topping of mozzarella.
Cover with foil and bake for 25 minutes. Remove foil and bake for 15-20 minutes until bubbly.
Let stand for 15 minutes before slicing.

name: Pork Enchiladas
ingredients: 3-4 cups cooked shredded pork (pulled pork or carnitas)
instructions: Preheat oven to 350°F (175°C). Spread 1/2 cup of enchilada sauce in a 9x13 inch dish.
In a bowl, mix shredded pork, diced onions, green chiles, cumin, and 1/2 cup of the sauce.
Warm tortillas until pliable. Place 1/3 cup of filling in each, roll tightly, and place seam-side down in the dish.
Pour remaining enchilada sauce over the rolls and sprinkle with the remaining cheese.
Cover with foil and bake for 20 minutes.
Remove foil and bake for another 10-15 minutes until cheese is melted and bubbly.
Rest for 5 minutes and garnish with cilantro or sour cream.

name: Stuffed Pork Peppers
ingredients: 4-6 large bell peppers, tops removed and de-seeded
1 cup shredded mozzarella or cheddar cheese
Salt and black pepper to taste
instructions: Preheat oven to 375°F (190°C). Grease a 9x13 inch baking dish.
In a large skillet, brown the ground pork with the diced onion. Drain excess fat.
Add minced garlic and cook for 1 minute. Stir in cooked rice, tomato sauce, seasonings, and 1/4 cup of the cheese.
Stand the peppers upright in the baking dish and spoon the mixture into each pepper.
Cover tightly with foil and bake for 25-30 minutes.
Remove foil, sprinkle with remaining cheese, and bake for 5-10 minutes until cheese is melted.
Serve garnished with fresh herbs.

name: Pork Pie
instructions: Mix chopped pork shoulder, belly, bacon, and seasonings. Chill for a few hours.
Make pastry: Heat water and lard until boiled. Pour into flour and salt mixture. Stir until dough forms.
While dough is warm, mold into a cylinder shape (hand-raised) or press into a springform tin.
Pack meat filling tightly into the pastry. Roll out lid, place on top, and crimp edges to seal.
Cut a small hole in the center of the lid and brush with egg wash.
Bake at 180°C (350°F) for 30 mins, then reduce to 160°C (320°F) and bake for 60-90 mins.
Dissolve gelatine in warm stock. Once pie is cool but still warm, pour liquid stock through the hole.
Chill in the refrigerator overnight before serving cold.

name: Pork Bake
ingredients: 1 ½ cups long-grain white rice, uncooked
1 can (10.5 oz) cream of mushroom soup
Garlic powder, salt, and black pepper to taste
instructions: Preheat oven to 350°F (175°C) and grease a 9x13 inch baking dish.
In the dish, stir together uncooked rice, soup, broth, and half the onion soup mix.
Season pork chops with garlic powder, salt, and pepper. Place on top of the rice.
Sprinkle the remaining onion soup mix over the chops.
Cover tightly with foil and bake for 30 minutes.
Remove foil and bake for another 30 minutes until rice is tender and pork is cooked through.
Let rest for 5 minutes before serving.

name: Pork Shepherd’s Pie
instructions: Boil potatoes until tender. Mash with butter, milk, and half the cheese. Set aside.
Preheat oven to 375°F (190°C).
Sauté onion and carrot in a skillet. Add ground pork and cook until browned.
Stir in tomato paste, Worcestershire sauce, thyme, and stock. Simmer until thickened.
Stir in frozen peas. Transfer mixture to a baking dish.
Spread mashed potatoes over the top and sprinkle with remaining cheese.
Bake for 25-30 minutes until golden brown and bubbling.
Let rest for 10 minutes before serving.

name: Cheesy Baked Pork
ingredients: 1 can (10.5 oz) cream of mushroom soup
Salt, pepper, garlic powder, and paprika to taste
instructions: Preheat oven to 375°F (190°C). Grease a 9x13-inch baking dish.
Season and sear pork chops in a skillet until golden. Set aside.
Mix soup, milk, sour cream, garlic, and half the cheese.
Layer half of the potatoes and onions in the dish, pour half the sauce over. Repeat layers.
Cover with foil and bake for 30 minutes.
Remove foil, arrange pork chops on top, and sprinkle with remaining cheese.
Bake uncovered for 25-30 minutes until potatoes are tender and pork is done.
Let rest for 5 minutes before serving.

name: Pork Bulgogi
ingredients: 1.5 lbs (680g) Pork belly, shoulder, or butt (thinly sliced)
instructions: Prepare the Marinade: In a large mixing bowl, combine gochujang, gochugaru, soy sauce, sugar, mirin, garlic, ginger, sesame oil, pepper, and optional grated fruit. Whisk until smooth.
Marinate: Add the thinly sliced pork and the sliced onions to the bowl. Massage the marinade into the meat until every slice is well-coated. Let it sit for at least 30 minutes.
Cook: Heat a large skillet or wok over medium-high heat with 1 tablespoon of oil. Once hot, add the pork in a single layer (work in batches if necessary).
Sear: Let the pork sear for 2–3 minutes without moving it to get a nice char, then stir-fry for another 4–5 minutes until fully cooked and caramelized.
Finish: Toss in the green onions during the last minute of cooking.
Serve: Transfer to a plate and garnish with toasted sesame seeds. Serve over steamed white rice.

name: Tonkotsu Ramen
ingredients: 3 lbs (1.5kg) Pork bones (femurs, trotters, or neck bones)
2 lbs Pork belly (rolled and tied for chashu)
4–6 portions Fresh or dried ramen noodles
Garnish: Sliced green onions, wood ear mushrooms, nori (seaweed) sheets, black garlic oil
instructions: Prepare the Broth: Blanch pork bones in boiling water for 15 minutes. Drain and scrub the bones under cold water to remove scum. Return cleaned bones to the pot with aromatics and fresh water.
Simmer: Bring to a rolling boil, then reduce to a steady simmer for 12 hours. Keep bones submerged; add water as needed. Strain the liquid through a fine-mesh sieve once creamy and opaque.

Prepare Chashu & Eggs: Sear pork belly, then simmer in a pot with soy sauce, mirin, sake, sugar, garlic, and ginger for 2–3 hours. Boil eggs for 6.5 minutes, peel, and marinate in the chashu liquid for at least 4 hours.
Prepare Tare: Combine soy sauce, mirin, and salt in a small saucepan. Simmer for 5 minutes and set aside.
Assembly: Add 2–3 tablespoons of Tare to the bottom of each bowl. Pour about 1.5 cups of boiling hot Tonkotsu Broth into the bowl and stir.
Noodles & Toppings: Add cooked ramen noodles to the broth. Arrange slices of chashu, a halved marinated egg, green onions, and nori on top. Serve immediately.

name: Pork Yakitori
ingredients: 750g (1.6 lbs) Pork belly (skin removed, cut into 1-inch cubes)
2 bunches of Green Onions (negi), cut into 1.5-inch lengths
Bamboo skewers (soaked in water for 30 minutes)
instructions: Prepare the Sauce: Combine soy sauce, mirin, and sugar in a small saucepan. Simmer over medium heat for 10 minutes until it reduces to a glossy glaze.
Assemble Skewers: Thread the pork cubes onto the soaked skewers, alternating with pieces of green onion.
Grill: Preheat grill to medium-high. Place skewers on the grill and cook for 8–10 minutes total, turning every 2–3 minutes.
Basting: During the last 3 minutes of cooking, brush the skewers generously with the thickened sauce (tare) several times to create a caramelized crust.
Serve: Drizzle any remaining sauce over the skewers and serve hot. Optionally sprinkle with sesame seeds or shichimi togarashi.
serving_size: 4 people (2-3 skewers per person as appetizer)

name: Pork Katsu Curry
ingredients: 4 boneless Pork loin chops (1/2 inch thick)
Salt and black pepper to taste
100g Japanese curry roux (e.g., S&B Golden Curry)
instructions: Prepare the Curry Sauce: Sauté onions in a large pot until translucent. Add carrots and potatoes, sauté for 2 minutes. Pour in water/broth and simmer for 15 minutes until vegetables are tender.
Thicken Curry: Turn off heat, break the curry roux into the pot, and stir until dissolved. Simmer on low for another 5 minutes until thickened.
Prepare Pork Katsu: Tenderize pork chops and season with salt and pepper. Dredge in flour, dip in egg, and coat firmly with Panko.
Fry Katsu: Heat 1/2 inch of oil in a skillet to 340°F (170°C). Fry pork for 3–4 minutes per side until golden brown. Drain on a wire rack and slice into strips.
Assembly: Place rice on one half of a plate. Lay the sliced pork katsu next to the rice. Ladle the hot curry sauce over the other half of the plate.

name: Hainanese Pork Rice
ingredients: 500g Pork loin or shoulder (sliced into 1cm chops)
instructions: Marinate Pork: Pound pork slices to tenderize. Mix with soy sauce, five-spice, sesame oil, sugar, egg, and white pepper. Marinate for at least 30 minutes.
Bread Pork: Coat each piece of pork in flour, then dip into the egg wash, and finally press firmly into the cracker crumbs.
Fry: Heat oil to medium-high and fry the pork chops until golden and crispy (3–4 minutes per side). Slice into strips.
Make Gravy: Sauté onions until translucent. Add tomatoes and peas. Pour in chicken stock, ketchup, Worcestershire sauce, and sugar. Bring to a boil and thicken with cornstarch slurry.
Serve: Place sliced crispy pork over steamed rice and pour the hot gravy over the top.

name: Pork Korma
instructions: Sauté Base: Melt butter/ghee in a large pot. Fry onions until golden brown. Stir in ginger, garlic, chili powder, and ground coriander for 1 minute.
Brown Pork: Add pork cubes and cook until browned on all sides.
Add Yogurt: Stir in salt and 4 tablespoons of yogurt. Cook until liquid evaporates. Repeat this process, adding 4 tbsp at a time, until all yogurt is incorporated.
Finish Sauce: Mix ground almonds and cream together, then stir into the pork. Add cinnamon and mace.
Simmer: Bring to a gentle boil, then simmer covered on low for 25–30 minutes until pork is tender. Add a splash of water if the sauce is too thick.
Serve: Garnish with fresh cilantro and serve with basmati rice or naan.

name: Pork Manchurian
ingredients: Fresh coriander or spring onions (for garnish)
instructions: Marination: Mix pork with ginger, garlic, turmeric, chili powder, vinegar, oil, and salt. Marinate for at least 2 hours.
Pre-Cook: Cook marinated pork in a pot until tender. Reserve any liquid released during cooking.
Sauté: Heat oil in a wok. Sauté onions until translucent. Add ginger paste, garlic paste, and green chilies. Sauté for 2 minutes.
Build Sauce: Add capsicum (if using). Stir in cornstarch slurry and reserved pork liquid. Simmer until thickened, then add soy sauce and tomato sauce.
Combine: Toss the cooked pork into the sauce until well-coated. Cook for 5–10 minutes on low heat.
Serve: Garnish with coriander or spring onions and serve hot with fried rice or noodles.

name: Pork Laksa
ingredients: 1 bunch Baby bok choy (stems sliced, leaves shredded)
Fresh coriander and 1 sliced red chilli (for garnish)
instructions: Simmer Base: In a large saucepan, combine coconut milk and laksa paste. Cover and simmer for 5 minutes.
Build Broth: Add boiling water, citrus juice, and fish sauce. Cover and simmer for 3 minutes.
Prepare Noodles: Soak hokkien noodles in boiling water for 3 minutes. Drain and divide among four serving bowls.
Cook Pork & Greens: Add bok choy stems and diced pork to the laksa broth. Cook covered for 3 minutes. Stir in shredded leaves and cook for 2 minutes.
Assemble: Place half of the bean sprouts in the bowls. Ladle hot soup and pork over the noodles.
Serve: Top with remaining bean sprouts, coriander, and sliced chilli.

name: Pork Pad Thai
ingredients: 1 lb uncooked medium Rice stick noodles
1/4 cup Fresh lime juice and zest
instructions: Prepare Noodles: Soak rice noodles in hot water for about 30 minutes until softened but still firm. Drain.
Stir-Fry: Heat oil in a wok over high heat. Add pork and garlic, stir-fry for 3 minutes until cooked through.
Season: Stir in sugar, fish sauce, lime juice, and zest. Mix well.
Scramble Eggs: Push pork to the sides, pour eggs into the center, and cook for 2–3 minutes until set. Toss with pork.
Combine: Add drained noodles, bean sprouts, and tofu. Toss over medium-high heat until noodles are tender and dish is heated through.
Serve: Top with green onions and peanuts. Serve with lime wedges and cilantro.

name: Pork Sate Lilit
ingredients: Spice Paste: 2 onions, 4 garlic cloves, 4 bird's-eye chillies, 20g ginger, 20g galangal, 10g turmeric, 1 tsp peppercorns, 2 cloves, 1 nutmeg, 5g coriander seeds, 2 candlenuts
instructions: Prepare Spice Paste: Grind all spice paste ingredients into a smooth, fragrant paste using a food processor or mortar and pestle.
Combine: Mix minced pork with the spice paste, palm sugar, tamarind, coconut milk, lime leaves, and salt until sticky and well-combined.
Shape Satay: Take about 30g of the mixture and wrap it around the end of a skewer (lemongrass or bamboo), covering about 3 inches.
Grill: Heat a grill to medium-high. Grill skewers for 4–5 minutes per side until golden brown and cooked through.
Serve: Garnish with fried onion flakes and serve with Sambal Matah or jasmine rice.

name: Pork Omelette
ingredients: Salt and black pepper to taste
2 tbsp neutral cooking oil (canola, vegetable, or avocado oil)
instructions: In a large bowl, combine the ground pork with the minced garlic, shallots, fish sauce, salt, and pepper. Mix well.
Crack the 6 eggs into the bowl with the pork. Beat the eggs and pork together until the meat is broken up and evenly distributed. Stir in the chopped scallions.
Place a large (10-12 inch) non-stick skillet over medium-high heat and add the cooking oil.
Once the oil is hot, pour in the entire egg and pork mixture. Use a spatula to gently push the egg around the center for the first minute to help it cook faster.
Let the omelette sit undisturbed for 4-5 minutes until the bottom is golden brown and the top is mostly set.

If the omelette is too large to flip in one piece, cut it into quarters with your spatula and flip each section individually. Cook the second side for another 3-4 minutes until golden brown and the pork is fully cooked through.

name: Pork Scramble
ingredients: 10 oz package frozen home fries or diced potatoes
instructions: In a large skillet over medium heat, add the sausage, potatoes, and chopped peppers. Crumble the sausage as it cooks.
Continue cooking until the sausage is fully browned and the potatoes are tender and slightly crispy.
While the sausage mixture is cooking, whisk together the eggs and milk in a small bowl until well combined.
Pour the egg mixture directly over the sausage and potatoes in the skillet.
Stir gently and constantly until the eggs are cooked through and set.
Serve warm.

name: Pork Sandwich Melt
ingredients: 8 slices of Texas-style toast or sourdough
16 slices of Colby Jack or Cheddar cheese (4 per sandwich)
instructions: In a saucepan over low heat, combine the pulled pork and BBQ sauce. Stir often for about 15 minutes until heated through.
Butter one side of each bread slice.
Place 4 slices of bread butter-side down on a griddle or large skillet. Top each with 2 slices of cheese, 1/2 cup of the warm BBQ pork, 1/2 cup of coleslaw (if using), and 2 more slices of cheese.
Top with the remaining bread slices, butter-side up.
Cook over medium-low heat for 4-5 minutes per side until the bread is golden brown and the cheese is fully melted.

name: Pork Panini
ingredients: 4 slices (1/3" thick) cooked boneless pork roast (approx. 8 oz)
instructions: In a small bowl, stir together the mayonnaise and chopped fresh basil. Set aside.
Dip each slice of cooked pork into the beaten egg, then press into the breadcrumbs until evenly coated.
Heat olive oil in a large nonstick skillet over medium heat. Cook breaded pork for about 2 minutes per side until golden brown and crispy. Transfer to a plate.
Spread the basil mayo on one side of all 8 bread slices. On 4 of the slices, layer sliced tomatoes, the crispy pork, and mozzarella cheese. Top with remaining bread slices.
Lightly coat the skillet with cooking spray and heat over medium-low heat. Grill sandwiches for about 3 minutes per side, pressing down slightly, until toasted and cheese is melted.

name: Pork Flatbread
ingredients: 1 cup BBQ sauce (plus extra for drizzling)
2 cups shredded Cheddar or Monterey Jack cheese
Chopped green onions or fresh cilantro for garnish
instructions: Preheat oven or grill to 425°F (220°C).
In a bowl, toss the pulled pork with about 1/2 cup of BBQ sauce until evenly coated.
Lay flatbreads on a baking sheet. Spread a thin layer of BBQ sauce on each, then top with the sauced pork, shredded cheese, and red onions.
Bake for 10-12 minutes, or until the cheese is bubbly and the edges of the flatbread are crisp.
Remove from oven. Top with jalapeños, a drizzle of extra sauce, and a sprinkle of green onions or cilantro.

name: Pork Pizza
ingredients: 1/2 cup barbecue sauce (plus 1-2 tbsp for tossing)
1 to 1.5 cups cooked pulled pork, shredded
instructions: Preheat oven to 475°F (245°C).
Toss the shredded pulled pork with 1-2 tablespoons of BBQ sauce.
On a lightly floured surface, roll or stretch the dough into a 12-inch circle. Transfer to a pizza pan or parchment paper.
Spread 1/2 cup BBQ sauce evenly over the dough. Sprinkle about 1 cup of cheese over the sauce.
Distribute the pulled pork, red onions, and jalapenos over the cheese. Top with the remaining cheese.
Bake for 12-15 minutes until the crust is golden brown and the cheese is bubbly.
Garnish with green onions and cilantro. Let sit for 2-3 minutes before slicing.

name: Pork Toast
ingredients: 7 slices white sandwich bread, crusts removed and cut into 4 squares each
instructions: In a mortar and pestle or small food processor, pound garlic, cilantro, and white pepper into a fine paste.
In a mixing bowl, combine ground pork, the garlic paste, soy sauce, sugar, and the half-beaten egg. Mix until slightly sticky.
Spread about 1 teaspoon of the pork mixture onto each square of bread. Press down firmly. Sprinkle with sesame seeds if using.
Heat oil in a skillet to 350°F (175°C). Place bread meat-side down first. Fry for 1-2 minutes until pork is cooked.
Flip and fry the bread side for 30 seconds until golden. Drain on paper towels.
Serve immediately with sweet chili sauce or cucumber relish.

name: Pork Wrap Melt
instructions: Heat a skillet over medium-low heat and add butter. Add sliced onions and a pinch of salt. Cook, stirring occasionally, for 30-40 minutes until caramelized.
Mix the pulled pork with BBQ sauce until well coated.
Lay a tortilla flat. Spread 1/4 cup coleslaw in the center. Top with 1/4 of the pork mixture, 1/4 of the caramelized onions, and 1/4 cup cheese.
Fold the sides toward the center and roll tightly into a burrito shape. Place seam-side down.
Heat a skillet over medium heat with cooking spray. Grill wraps seam-side down first for 2-3 minutes per side until golden and cheese is melted.

name: Pork Rice Bowl
ingredients: 1/2 cup low-fat sour cream mixed with 1-2 tsp Sriracha
instructions: In a large skillet over medium-high heat, brown the ground pork until cooked through. Drain any excess fat.
In a small bowl, whisk together soy sauce, corn starch, ginger powder, garlic powder, and sugar.
Pour the sauce mixture into the skillet with the pork. Cook for 1 minute until thickened and coating the meat.
Divide cooked rice into 6 bowls.
Top each bowl with glazed pork, cucumber slices, shredded carrots, and cilantro.
Drizzle with the spicy sriracha-sour cream mixture and serve.

name: Pork Stir-Fry Noodles
ingredients: 1 lb ground pork or thinly sliced pork tenderloin
8 oz dried egg noodles or rice noodles
2-3 cups mixed stir-fry vegetables (broccoli, peppers, carrots)
instructions: Cook noodles according to package instructions. Drain and set aside.
Heat 1 tbsp oil in a large wok over high heat. Brown the pork until crispy (5-8 minutes).
Add remaining oil, garlic, ginger, and the white parts of green onions. Cook for 1 minute.
Add mixed vegetables and stir-fry for 3-4 minutes until crisp-tender.
Whisk together soy sauce, sweet chili sauce, and brown sugar. Pour over the pork and vegetables.
Add cooked noodles to the pan and toss for 1-2 minutes until well-coated.
Garnish with green onion tops and serve.

name: Chicken Fried Steak
ingredients: Salt and black pepper to taste
instructions: Pound the cube steaks with a meat mallet until they are about 1/4-inch thick.
In one shallow bowl, whisk together the flour, salt, pepper, garlic powder, paprika, and cayenne.
In a second bowl, beat the eggs and milk together.
Dredge each steak in the flour mixture, dip into the egg mixture, and then back into the flour mixture, pressing firmly.
Heat 1/2 inch of oil in a large skillet to 350°F. Fry steaks in batches for 3–4 minutes per side until golden brown.
To make gravy: Pour off all but 4 tbsp of oil. Whisk in 4 tbsp of flour and cook for 1-2 minutes.
Slowly whisk in the milk and simmer until thickened. Season with salt and plenty of black pepper.
Serve the steaks smothered in the hot gravy.

name: Beef Schnitzel
ingredients: 4 thin-cut beef steaks (top round or sirloin)
Lemon wedges and fresh parsley for garnish
instructions: Place beef steaks between plastic wrap and pound with a mallet to 1/4 inch thickness.
Set up a dredging station with three bowls: flour mixed with seasonings, beaten eggs, and breadcrumbs.
Coat each steak in flour (shake off excess), dip in egg wash, and press firmly into breadcrumbs.
Heat oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat.
Fry steaks in batches for 2–3 minutes per side until golden and crispy.
Drain on paper towels and serve immediately with fresh lemon wedges and parsley.

name: Crispy Beef Strips
ingredients: 1 lb beef (sirloin, flank, or ribeye), sliced into thin strips
Sesame seeds and green onions for garnish
instructions: Slice beef against the grain into very thin strips.
Toss beef with egg white, then dredge in cornstarch until well-coated.
Heat 1/2 inch of oil in a wok or skillet to 375°F. Fry beef in small batches for 2–3 minutes until very crispy.
Drain beef on paper towels.
In a separate skillet, whisk sauce ingredients together and bring to a simmer until thickened.
Quickly toss the crispy beef in the sauce until evenly coated.
Garnish with sesame seeds and green onions. Serve immediately.

name: Beef Milanese
ingredients: 1 lb top round or sirloin steaks, sliced very thin
instructions: Pound the steaks to about 1/8-inch thickness using a meat mallet.
Prepare breading station: flour mixed with salt/pepper, whisked eggs, and breadcrumbs mixed with garlic powder and Parmesan.
Dredge each steak in flour, dip in egg, and press firmly into the breadcrumb mixture.
Heat oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat.
Fry steaks for 2–3 minutes per side until golden brown.
Drain on paper towels and serve with lemon wedges or a side of pasta.

name: Breaded Beef Cutlets
ingredients: 1 1/2 lbs thin-sliced beef (top round or cube steak)
1 cup breadcrumbs or crushed saltine crackers
instructions: Pound the beef cutlets until thin and even. Season with salt, pepper, and garlic powder.
Set up three bowls for breading: flour, beaten eggs, and breadcrumbs/crackers.
Coat each cutlet in flour, then egg, and finally the breadcrumbs, pressing to adhere.
Heat oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat.
Fry cutlets for 3–4 minutes per side until the coating is golden and the meat is cooked through.
Drain on paper towels and serve warm.

name: Beef Tempura
ingredients: 1.3 lbs beef fillet or sirloin, cut into 1x3cm strips
instructions: Marinate beef strips with ginger and sesame oil for 20 minutes.
Heat oil in a deep pot to 350°F.
Whisk egg and ice-cold water together, then stir in the flour, cornstarch, and salt until just combined (lumps are okay).
Dust marinated beef strips in the extra flour, then dip into the tempura batter.
Carefully drop beef into hot oil and fry in batches for 3–4 minutes until light golden and crispy.
Drain on a wire rack to maintain crispness.

name: Crispy Chili Beef
ingredients: 1 lb sirloin or flank steak, sliced into thin strips
instructions: Toss beef strips with egg, salt, pepper, and five spice. Coat thoroughly with cornflour.
Heat oil in a wok and fry beef in batches until very crispy and dark brown (3-5 mins). Drain.
Remove most oil, leaving 1 tbsp. Sauté garlic, ginger, and chilies.
Add sauce ingredients and simmer until thickened and glossy.
Quickly toss the crispy beef in the sauce and serve immediately over rice.

name: Beef Croquettes
ingredients: Salt and black pepper to taste
instructions: Brown the minced beef in a skillet, then remove with a slotted spoon.
Sauté onion in the remaining fat until soft.
Stir in 3 tbsp flour, then gradually whisk in beef stock until it forms a thick sauce.
Mix in the beef, Worcestershire sauce, parsley, salt, and pepper. Chill the mixture for 1–2 hours.
Shape the chilled mixture into cylinders.
Dredge each cylinder in flour, dip in egg, and coat with breadcrumbs.
Fry in 350°F oil for 3–4 minutes until golden brown.
Drain on paper towels and serve with mustard or gravy.

name: Beef Nuggets
instructions: Mix ground beef with seasonings and Parmesan cheese.
Shape the mixture into small nugget sizes.
Bread each nugget by dredging in flour, dipping in egg, and coating in breadcrumbs.
Chill breaded nuggets for 30 minutes in the refrigerator.
Deep fry in 350°F oil for 3–5 minutes until golden brown and cooked through.
Drain on paper towels and serve with dipping sauce.

name: Beef Katsu
ingredients: For Katsu Sauce: 1/4 cup ketchup, 1 tbsp Worcestershire sauce, 1 tbsp soy sauce, 1/2 tsp sugar
instructions: Pound steaks to 1/2-inch thickness and season with salt and pepper.
Set up breading station with flour, beaten egg, and Panko.
Dredge steak in flour, dip in egg, and press firmly into Panko.
Heat 1-2 inches of oil to 350°F. Fry steaks for 2–3 minutes per side until golden.
Let rest for 1-2 minutes, then slice into 1/2-inch strips.
Serve with katsu sauce and shredded cabbage.

name: Beef Bourguignon
ingredients: 3 lbs (1.4 kg) beef chuck or brisket, cut into 2-inch chunks
6 oz (170g) thick-cut bacon or lardons, chopped
3 cups dry red wine (Pinot Noir, Merlot, or Burgundy)
1 lb (450g) fresh mushrooms (cremini or button), halved or quartered
Salt and freshly ground black pepper to taste
instructions: Pat the beef chunks dry with paper towels. Season with salt and pepper.
In a large Dutch oven, heat olive oil over medium heat. Crisp the bacon, then remove and set aside.
Increase heat to medium-high and sear the beef chunks in batches until browned. Remove and set aside.
Sauté the diced onion and sliced carrots for 5–8 minutes in the same fat. Add garlic and cook for 1 minute.
Stir in tomato paste and flour. Cook for 2 minutes, stirring constantly.
Slowly pour in the red wine while scraping the bottom of the pot. Return beef and bacon to the pot.
Add enough beef stock to just cover the meat. Add thyme and bay leaves.
Cover and cook in a preheated oven at 325°F (160°C) for 2.5 to 3 hours, or simmer on very low heat on the stovetop.
While the stew simmers, sauté mushrooms and pearl onions in butter in a separate skillet until golden brown.
Stir the mushrooms and pearl onions into the stew 20 minutes before it is finished.
Remove bay leaves and thyme sprigs. Garnish with fresh parsley and serve over mashed potatoes or egg noodles.

name: Beef Stroganoff
ingredients: 1.5 lbs (700g) Sirloin steak, sliced into thin strips against the grain
8 oz (225g) Cremini or white button mushrooms, sliced
1/2 cup full-fat sour cream (at room temperature)
Salt and black pepper to taste
instructions: Season beef strips with salt and pepper. Heat olive oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat.
Sear the beef quickly in batches until browned. Remove and set aside.
Melt butter in the same skillet. Sauté onions and mushrooms for 5–7 minutes until golden brown.
Add minced garlic and cook for 30 seconds. Sprinkle flour over the mixture and stir for 1–2 minutes.
Slowly pour in beef broth, Worcestershire sauce, and Dijon mustard. Simmer for 3–5 minutes until thickened.
Reduce heat to low. Stir the browned beef and its juices back into the skillet for 1 minute.
Remove from heat and stir in the sour cream until smooth. Do not boil once sour cream is added.
Serve over hot egg noodles and garnish with fresh parsley.

name: Bulalo
ingredients: 2 lbs (1 kg) beef shank (bulalo cut) with bone marrow
2 ears of sweet corn, cut into pieces
3–4 tbsp fish sauce (patis), adjusted to taste
instructions: Parboil beef shanks for 5–10 minutes. Drain and rinse the meat to remove impurities.
Return cleaned beef to the pot with fresh water, onions, and peppercorns. Bring to a boil.
