Lower heat and simmer for 1.5 to 2.5 hours until meat is fork-tender (or 30-45 mins in a pressure cooker).
Periodically skim off any foam or excess fat from the surface.
Add corn and potatoes. Simmer for 10–15 minutes until cooked through.
Season with fish sauce and salt to taste.
Add Baguio beans and cabbage. Cook for another 2–3 minutes until vegetables are tender-crisp.
Serve hot with steamed white rice and a side of fish sauce with calamansi and chili.

name: Sinigang na Baka
ingredients: 1 kg (2.2 lbs) beef brisket, ribs, or shank, cubed
1 bundle string beans (sitaw), cut into 2-inch lengths
1 bundle water spinach (kangkong) or bok choy
1 packet (40g) tamarind soup base mix (Sinigang mix)
Fish sauce (patis) and black pepper to taste
instructions: Combine beef and water in a large pot. Bring to a boil and skim off the scum.
Add onions and tomatoes. Cover and simmer for 1.5 to 2 hours until beef is very tender.
Add taro and radish. Cook for 8–10 minutes until taro is soft.
Stir in the tamarind soup mix, fish sauce, and black pepper. Adjust seasonings to taste.
Add string beans, okra, and green chilies. Simmer for 5 minutes.
Add water spinach or bok choy. Turn off heat immediately and cover to wilt the greens.
Serve hot with steamed white rice and fish sauce with chili.

name: Nilagang Baka
ingredients: 2 lbs (1 kg) beef shank, brisket, or ribs
2 pieces corn on the cob, cut into parts (optional)
instructions: In a large pot, combine beef, water, onion, and peppercorns. Bring to a boil.
Skim off the gray foam from the surface for a clear broth.
Lower heat, cover, and simmer for 1.5 to 2 hours until beef is fork-tender.
Add corn and potatoes. Cook for about 10 minutes until potatoes are nearly soft.
Pour in fish sauce and add salt to taste.
Add green beans, cabbage, and pechay. Cover and cook for 2–3 minutes.
Serve hot with steamed white rice and fish sauce with calamansi.

name: Beef Pho
ingredients: 4–5 lbs beef bones and 1 lb beef brisket or chuck
2 medium yellow onions, halved and charred
4-inch piece of ginger, sliced and charred
5 star anise, 1 cinnamon stick, 4 cloves, 1 tbsp coriander seeds
1 oz rock sugar or 2 tbsp brown sugar
1 lb sirloin or flank steak, sliced paper-thin
Bean sprouts, Thai basil, lime wedges, jalapeños for garnish
instructions: Parboil bones for 10 minutes, then drain and rinse thoroughly.
Char onions and ginger. Toast star anise, cinnamon, cloves, and coriander seeds.
Return bones to pot with 6 quarts of water, charred aromatics, toasted spices, brisket, sugar, and salt.
Simmer for 3–6 hours, skimming fat periodically. Remove brisket after 1.5 hours, chill and slice.
Cook rice noodles according to package instructions and divide into bowls.
Top noodles with brisket slices, raw thin beef slices, onions, scallions, and cilantro.
Bring broth to a rolling boil and ladle over the beef to cook it instantly.
Serve immediately with a side platter of fresh herbs, sprouts, lime, and sauces.

name: Beef Ramen
ingredients: 1 lb (450g) sirloin or flank steak, thinly sliced
2 cups baby spinach or bok choy
Sliced green onions and sesame seeds for garnish
instructions: Boil eggs for 6-7 minutes for a soft yolk, then peel and slice in half.
Heat oil in a pot and sear steak slices for 1-2 minutes. Remove and set aside.
Sauté garlic, ginger, and mushrooms for 2-3 minutes.
Add beef broth, soy sauce, fish sauce, and sugar. Simmer for 5 minutes.
Add ramen noodles to the broth and cook for 2-3 minutes. Stir in spinach at the last minute.
Divide noodles and broth into bowls. Top with seared beef, egg, green onions, and sesame seeds.

name: Goulash
ingredients: 2 lbs beef chuck or shanks, 1-inch cubes
Salt and black pepper to taste
instructions: Brown beef cubes in fat in a large Dutch oven. Set aside.
Sauté onions until starting to brown. Add garlic and bell peppers; cook for 2 minutes.
Remove from heat briefly and stir in the paprika.
Return beef to pot. Add tomatoes, broth, caraway seeds, and bay leaves. Bring to a boil.
Lower heat, cover, and simmer for 1.5 to 2 hours until beef is tender.
Stir in potatoes and carrots. Simmer for another 20–30 minutes until soft.
Remove bay leaves and serve with a dollop of sour cream and crusty bread.

name: Beef Caldereta
ingredients: 2 lbs (1kg) beef chuck, brisket, or short ribs, cubed
1 red and 1 green bell pepper, cut into squares
½ cup grated cheese (cheddar or quick-melt)
Salt, pepper, and chili peppers (optional) to taste
instructions: Brown the beef chunks in oil in a large pot. Remove and set aside.
Sauté garlic and onions until translucent.
Return beef to pot. Add tomato sauce, paste, soy sauce, fish sauce, bay leaves, and broth.
Simmer for 1 to 1.5 hours until beef is fork-tender.
Add potatoes and carrots. Simmer for 10–15 minutes until soft.
Stir in liver spread and grated cheese until sauce is creamy and thick.
Add bell peppers, peas, and olives. Cook for 3–5 minutes.
Adjust seasonings and serve hot over steamed white rice.

name: Beef Kare-Kare
ingredients: 2 lbs (900g) beef chuck, short ribs, or shank, cubed
2 tbsp annatto powder dissolved in water
1 bunch string beans (sitao), cut into pieces
Salt and black pepper to taste
instructions: Boil beef in water until fork-tender (1.5 to 2 hours). Reserve the broth.
Sauté garlic and onions in a separate pan. Add beef and sauté for 2 minutes.
Pour in 4 cups of the reserved broth and bring to a simmer.
Stir in peanut butter and annatto water until fully incorporated.
Slowly pour in the rice flour slurry and simmer for 5–10 minutes until thickened.
Add string beans and eggplant. Simmer for 5 minutes.
Add bok choy and cook for 2–3 minutes until tender but vibrant.
Serve hot with a side of sautéed shrimp paste and steamed rice.

name: Beef Adobo
ingredients: 2 lbs beef chuck, brisket, or short ribs, cut into 1.5-inch cubes
1 whole head of garlic, crushed and peeled
⅓ cup white vinegar (cane or coconut vinegar)
Optional: coconut milk for Adobo sa Gata
instructions: Marinate the beef in soy sauce, crushed garlic, peppercorns, and bay leaves for at least 30 minutes.
Heat oil in a large pot over medium-high heat. Remove beef from marinade (reserve liquid) and sear in batches until browned.
Pour reserved marinade and water into the pot. Bring to a boil, then reduce heat to low. Cover and simmer for 1 to 1.5 hours until beef is fork-tender.
Pour in the vinegar and simmer undisturbed for 2–3 minutes to allow the harsh acidity to cook off.
Stir in the brown sugar and simmer uncovered for 10–15 minutes until the sauce thickens and becomes glossy.
Adjust taste with more sugar or salt if needed. Serve hot over steamed white rice.

name: Beef Rendang
ingredients: 2 lbs (1 kg) beef chuck or short ribs, cut into 1.5 to 2-inch cubes
Spice Paste: 10–12 dried red chilies (soaked), 6–8 shallots, 5 cloves garlic, 2-inch galangal, 2-inch ginger, 1-inch turmeric, 2 stalks lemongrass (white parts only)
1 tbsp tamarind paste mixed with water
2 tbsp palm sugar (Gula Melaka) or brown sugar
Kerisik: ½ cup toasted grated coconut pounded into an oily paste
instructions: Blend all Spice Paste ingredients into a smooth paste.
Heat oil in a heavy-bottomed pot. Sauté the spice paste, cinnamon, star anise, and bruised lemongrass until oil separates and it smells fragrant.
Add beef cubes and cook for 5 minutes until no longer raw on the outside.
Pour in coconut milk, tamarind water, kaffir lime leaves, and turmeric leaf. Bring to a gentle boil, then reduce heat to low.
Cover loosely and simmer for 2 to 2.5 hours, stirring every 20 minutes.
Stir in kerisik, palm sugar, and salt once the sauce has thickened and beef is tender.
Remove lid and continue cooking on low, stirring frequently, until liquid evaporates and oil separates. The beef should 'fry' in the spices until dark brown (30–45 mins).
Discard whole spices and serve with coconut rice or jasmine rice.

name: Beef Teriyaki
ingredients: 2 lbs flap meat, skirt steak, or sirloin, sliced across the grain into 1-inch strips
¼ cup granulated sugar + 3 tbsp brown sugar
½ tsp garlic powder or 2 cloves minced garlic
½ tsp ground ginger or 1 tsp fresh grated ginger
1½ tsp cornstarch whisked with 1 tbsp cold water (slurry)
Garnish: Sesame seeds and sliced green onions
instructions: In a small saucepan, combine soy sauce, water, mirin, vinegar, sugars, garlic, and ginger. Bring to a simmer.
Whisk in the cornstarch slurry and simmer for 5–10 minutes until thickened. Let it cool slightly.
Stir-fry method: Heat oil in a large skillet or wok over high heat.
Add beef strips in batches and sear for 1–2 minutes per side until browned.
Pour the teriyaki sauce over the beef and toss for 1 minute until glazed and bubbly.
Alternatively, broil the beef for 5–7 minutes on a foil-lined tray.
Serve immediately over rice, garnished with sesame seeds and green onions.

name: Mongolian Beef
ingredients: 1 lb (450g) flank steak, sliced thinly against the grain
1 tsp fresh ginger, minced or grated
Garnish: 2–3 green onions (1-inch pieces), sesame seeds
instructions: Toss sliced beef with cornstarch until evenly coated. Let sit for 5–10 minutes.
Whisk together soy sauce, brown sugar, water, ginger, garlic, and red pepper flakes.
Heat oil in a large skillet or wok over medium-high heat. Sear beef in batches for 1–2 minutes per side until crispy. Remove and set aside.
Pour sauce mixture into the skillet and bring to a boil. Simmer for 2–3 minutes until it thickens into a glaze.
Add seared beef back to the skillet and toss to coat.
Stir in green onions and cook for 1 minute until softened.
Serve immediately over steamed white rice.

name: Beef Bulgogi
ingredients: 1.5 lbs (700g) ribeye or top sirloin, thinly sliced against the grain
2 tbsp brown sugar (or 1 tbsp sugar + 1 tbsp honey)
½ Asian pear, grated or blended (or red apple/mirin)
Garnish: Toasted sesame seeds, extra green onions
instructions: In a large bowl, whisk together soy sauce, brown sugar, sesame oil, garlic, ginger, pear, gochujang, and pepper.
Add beef, onions, and scallions to the marinade. Massage and refrigerate for at least 2 hours (preferably overnight).
Heat a cast-iron skillet or wok over high heat with a small amount of oil.
Cook beef in single-layer batches for 2–3 minutes per side until slightly charred and cooked through.
Garnish with sesame seeds and green onions.
Serve with steamed rice and lettuce wraps (ssam).

name: Beef Tapa
ingredients: 1.5 lbs (700g) beef sirloin, flank steak, or tenderloin, thinly sliced
instructions: Whisk together soy sauce, calamansi juice, minced garlic, sugar, salt, and pepper.
Add beef slices and massage the marinade into the meat. Refrigerate for 6–24 hours.
Heat a large skillet over medium heat with 1 tbsp oil.
Add beef and marinade. Simmer until liquid has almost completely evaporated.
Add another tbsp of oil, increase heat, and fry for 3–5 minutes until browned and caramelized.
Serve as part of Tapsilog with garlic fried rice and a fried egg.

name: Beef Mechado
ingredients: 2 lbs (1kg) beef chuck roast, cut into 2-inch cubes
1 red bell pepper, sliced into strips
instructions: Marinate beef in soy sauce, calamansi juice, and pepper for at least 30 minutes.
Heat oil in a large pot. Sear beef cubes in batches until browned. Remove and set aside.
Sauté onions and garlic in the same pot until translucent.
Return beef to the pot. Add reserved marinade, tomato sauce, broth, and bay leaves.
Bring to a boil, then simmer covered for 1 to 1.5 hours until beef is tender.
Add potatoes and carrots; simmer for 15–20 minutes.
Add bell peppers and cook for another 3–5 minutes. Adjust seasoning and serve hot with rice.

name: Bistek Tagalog
ingredients: 1.5 lbs (700g) beef sirloin or flank, sliced very thinly
¼ cup calamansi juice (or lemon juice)
2 large white or yellow onions, sliced into rings
Optional: 1 tsp sugar, 1 large fried potato
instructions: Marinate beef in soy sauce, calamansi juice, garlic, and pepper for at least 30 minutes.
Sauté onion rings in oil until slightly softened but crunchy. Remove and set aside.
Drain beef (reserve marinade) and pan-fry slices in batches until browned. Remove and set aside.
Pour reserved marinade and water into the skillet. Bring to a boil and simmer for 5 minutes.
Return beef to the pan and simmer for 2–3 minutes until tender and sauce is absorbed.
Transfer to a platter and top with the sautéed onion rings. Serve with steamed rice.

name: Beef Curry
ingredients: 1 red bell pepper and 1 green bell pepper, chopped
instructions: Preheat oven to 160°C / 325°F.
Soften onions in an ovenproof pan for 5 minutes.
Add garlic, ginger, and all spices. Fry for 1 minute.
Add passata, coconut milk, beef, and peppers. Bring to a boil.
Cover and cook in the oven for 2.5 hours until beef is tender.
Stir in fresh coriander before serving with rice and naan.

name: Massaman Beef Curry
ingredients: 1 lb (450g) beef chuck or stew meat, diced
1 tbsp palm sugar or brown sugar
Optional whole spices: 1 cinnamon stick, 3-5 cardamom pods, 3 cloves
instructions: Sear beef chunks in oil until browned. Remove and set aside.
Fry curry paste (and whole spices) in oil for 1–2 minutes until fragrant.
Return beef to pot. Add coconut milk and broth. Simmer covered for 1.5 to 2 hours until beef is tender.
Add potatoes, onions, and half the peanuts. Simmer for 20–25 minutes until potatoes are soft.
Season with tamarind, sugar, and fish sauce. Adjust to balance sweet, salty, and sour.
Garnish with remaining peanuts and serve over jasmine rice.

name: Spaghetti Bolognese
ingredients: 1 can (28 oz / 800g) Crushed tomatoes or tomato passata
1/2 cup Dry red wine (e.g., Cabernet or Merlot)
1 tsp Dried oregano or Italian seasoning
Salt and black pepper to taste
instructions: Heat olive oil in a large skillet or Dutch oven over medium heat. Add onion, carrot, and celery. Cook for 5-7 minutes until soft. Add garlic and cook for 1 minute.
Increase heat to medium-high. Add ground beef and cook until browned, breaking it into crumbles. Drain excess fat.
Pour in red wine and simmer for 2-3 minutes, scraping the bottom of the pan.
Stir in tomato paste, crushed tomatoes, oregano, bay leaves, sugar, and milk (if using). Reduce heat to low and simmer for at least 20-30 minutes (ideally 1.5-2 hours).
While sauce simmers, cook spaghetti in salted boiling water until al dente. Reserve 1/2 cup of pasta water before draining.
Remove bay leaves from the sauce. Toss cooked spaghetti into the sauce, adding reserved pasta water if needed to coat perfectly.
Serve topped with Parmesan cheese and fresh parsley.

name: Beef Lasagna
instructions: Prepare Meat Sauce: Brown ground beef and onion in a large skillet. Drain fat. Add garlic, tomato sauce, crushed tomatoes, tomato paste, water, sugar, Italian seasoning, basil, salt, and pepper. Simmer for 30 minutes.
Prepare Cheese Mixture: Combine ricotta, egg, parsley, Parmesan, 1 cup of mozzarella, and 1 tsp Italian seasoning in a bowl.
Cook Noodles: Boil regular noodles until al dente and drain. (Skip for oven-ready noodles).

Assemble: Preheat oven to 375°F (190°C). Spread 1/2 cup sauce in a 9x13 dish. Layer: noodles, 1/3 ricotta mixture, 1/3 meat sauce. Repeat twice. Top with final noodles, remaining sauce, and 2 cups mozzarella.
Bake: Cover with foil and bake for 25 minutes. Remove foil and bake for 20-25 minutes until bubbly. Rest for 15-20 minutes before serving.

name: Beef Biryani
ingredients: 1.5 lbs (700g) stewing beef or chuck, 1-inch cubes
Whole spices: 2 bay leaves, 1 cinnamon stick, 4 green cardamoms, 4 cloves, 1 star anise
3 cups Basmati rice, soaked for 30 minutes
A pinch of saffron or yellow food coloring mixed with 2 tbsp milk
instructions: Prepare Beef Masala: Fry onions in oil until golden brown. Reserve half. Add whole spices, beef, and ginger-garlic paste to remaining onions; sauté 5 mins. Add tomatoes and ground spices. Simmer covered until beef is tender (45-60 mins). Stir in yogurt, garam masala, and half the herbs.
Prepare Rice: Boil water with whole spices and salt. Add rice and parboil until 70-80% done. Drain.
Layering: In a heavy pot, layer half the rice, then all the beef masala, fried onions, and herbs. Top with remaining rice. Drizzle saffron milk and lemon juice.
Steam (Dum): Cover tightly and cook on lowest heat for 15-20 minutes. Fluff gently before serving.

name: Beef Fried Rice
ingredients: 1 lb ground beef or thinly sliced flank steak
3 cups cold, cooked white rice (jasmine or long-grain)
1 cup frozen peas and carrots, thawed
instructions: Brown the beef in 1 tbsp oil until cooked through. Remove and set aside.
Scramble eggs in the same skillet until set. Remove and set aside.
Sauté onion, garlic, ginger, and peas/carrots in remaining oil until tender.
Add cold rice to the skillet. Stir-fry for 3-5 minutes, breaking up clumps.
Add beef and eggs back to the pan. Pour in soy sauce, sesame oil, and oyster sauce. Toss thoroughly for 1-2 minutes.
Garnish with green onions and sesame seeds. Serve warm.

name: Beef Paella
ingredients: 1.5 lbs (700g) Beef (sirloin or ribeye), cut into 1-inch chunks
1 tsp Saffron threads, soaked in 2 tbsp warm broth
Salt and black pepper to taste
instructions: Sear beef in olive oil in a wide pan until browned. Remove and set aside.
Sauté onion and bell pepper until softened. Add garlic for 1 minute.
Stir in tomatoes and paprika; cook for 3-4 minutes until thickened.
Add rice and stir to coat. Pour in beef broth and saffron liquid. Do not stir again.
Simmer uncovered on low-medium heat for 15-18 minutes.
Nestle beef and peas into the rice when liquid is almost gone.
Once liquid is absorbed and a crust (socarrat) forms on the bottom, turn off heat.
Cover with a towel and rest for 5-10 minutes.
Garnish with parsley and serve with lemon wedges.

name: Beef Risotto
ingredients: Salt and black pepper to taste
instructions: Fry the steak in olive oil to desired doneness. Remove and rest.
In the same pan, melt butter and sauté onion and garlic until soft.
Add mushrooms and red pepper; fry for 2 minutes.
Stir in Arborio rice and tomato paste. Season with salt and pepper. Toast for 1-2 minutes.
Add hot beef stock one ladle at a time, stirring frequently until liquid is absorbed before adding more.
Continue for about 30 minutes until rice is tender and creamy.
Stir in Parmesan and basil.
Slice rested steak and serve over the risotto.

name: Beef Jambalaya
ingredients: 1/2 tsp Dried thyme or file powder
Sliced green onions and parsley for garnish
instructions: Toss beef and sausage with Cajun seasoning. Brown in 1 tbsp oil; remove and set aside.
Sauté onion, bell pepper, celery, and garlic in remaining oil for 7-10 minutes.
Stir in tomatoes, broth, Worcestershire, salt, thyme, red pepper, and bay leaf. Add meat back in.
Bring to boil, then simmer covered for 20-25 minutes.
Discard bay leaf. Stir in cooked rice and simmer 3-5 minutes until liquid is absorbed.
Stir in hot sauce and garnish with green onions and parsley.

name: Beef Pilaf
ingredients: 1.5 lbs (700g) beef chuck or sirloin, 1-inch cubes
2.5 to 3 cups long-grain rice (Basmati or Jasmine)
1 whole head of garlic, top sliced off
4-5 cups hot water or beef broth
instructions: Brown beef in oil in a Dutch oven for 7-10 minutes.
Add onions and sauté until golden. Add carrots and cook 5 minutes.
Add spices and 2 cups hot water. Simmer covered for 40-45 minutes until meat is tender.
Spread rinsed rice evenly over meat and veggies. Do not stir.
Pour remaining hot water over rice (1 inch above rice). Add remaining salt and bay leaves.
Push garlic head into the center of the rice.
Boil, then reduce to low. Cover tightly and cook 15-20 minutes.
Rest covered for 10 minutes. Remove garlic and bay leaves, then toss and serve.

name: Beef Yakisoba
ingredients: 10 oz (300g) flank steak or sirloin, thinly sliced
Garnish: pickled red ginger, sesame seeds
instructions: Whisk together Worcestershire sauce, soy sauce, oyster sauce, ketchup, and sugar.
Loosen fresh noodles with warm water or cook dried noodles.
Sear beef in 1 tbsp oil over high heat until browned. Remove and set aside.
Sauté onions and carrots for 2 minutes. Add cabbage and mushrooms; cook 2-3 minutes.
Add noodles and beef back to the pan. Toss together.
Pour sauce over the mixture. Toss for 2-3 minutes until coated and sauce thickens. Stir in green onions.
Serve garnished with pickled ginger and sesame seeds.

name: Beef Nasi Goreng
ingredients: 2 tbsp fish sauce or soy sauce
Spring onions, coriander, lime wedges for garnish
instructions: Marinate beef in 1 tbsp kecap manis for 10 minutes.
Sear beef in 1/2 tbsp oil over high heat until browned. Remove and set aside.
Sauté onion, garlic, chili, ginger, and turmeric in 1 tbsp oil until fragrant.
Add chilled rice, 2 tbsp kecap manis, fish sauce, carrots, and bean sprouts. Stir-fry 2-3 minutes on high heat.
Return beef to the wok and stir for 1 minute.
Serve topped with a fried egg and garnished with spring onions, coriander, and lime.

name: Beef Shawarma
ingredients: 1.5 lbs flank steak, flap steak, or top sirloin (sliced very thinly against the grain)
1/4 cup white wine vinegar (or apple cider vinegar)
Tahini sauce (tahini paste, lemon juice, garlic, and water)
Diced tomatoes, cucumbers, and fresh parsley for serving
Optional: Pickled cucumbers, pickled red onions, or hummus
instructions: Slice the beef into thin, bite-sized strips (about 1/4-inch thick) against the grain.
In a large bowl, whisk together olive oil, vinegar, lemon juice, zest, and all spices. Add beef, sliced onions, and minced garlic. Toss to coat and marinate for at least 1 hour.
Heat a large cast-iron skillet or grill pan over high heat. Cook beef strips in batches for 8–12 minutes until browned and slightly charred.
Warm the pita bread. Spread tahini sauce or hummus inside, add cooked beef, followed by tomato-cucumber salad and pickles.
Drizzle with extra tahini sauce and a squeeze of fresh lemon. Serve immediately.

name: Beef Burrito
ingredients: Salt and black pepper to taste
Optional: Cooked rice, black beans, shredded lettuce, diced tomatoes, sour cream, salsa, or guacamole
instructions: Heat a large skillet over medium-high heat. Brown ground beef, breaking it into small crumbles. Drain excess fat.
Add chopped onion and sauté for 3–5 minutes until softened. Stir in minced garlic and cook for 30–60 seconds.
Stir in cumin, paprika, chili powder, salt, pepper, tomato paste, and beef stock. Simmer for 5–10 minutes until thickened.
Briefly warm tortillas in a dry skillet or microwave to make them pliable.
Place about 1/2 cup of beef mixture in the center of a tortilla. Add cheese and optional toppings.
Fold the sides over the filling, then fold the bottom edge tightly and roll away from you to seal.
Optional: Toast rolled burritos seam-side down in a hot skillet for 1–2 minutes per side until golden.

name: Beef Taco
ingredients: Salt and black pepper to taste
8–10 hard taco shells or small tortillas
Toppings: Shredded lettuce, diced tomatoes, shredded cheddar cheese, sour cream, lime wedges
instructions: Heat oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat. Brown ground beef for 3–5 minutes. Drain excess fat.
Add diced onion and cook for 2–3 minutes. Stir in garlic and cook for 30 seconds.
Add chili powder, cumin, paprika, garlic powder, onion powder, oregano, salt, pepper, and tomato paste. Stir to coat.
Pour in water or broth. Reduce heat and simmer for 3–5 minutes until thickened.
Warm taco shells or tortillas according to package instructions.
Fill each shell with beef mixture and add desired toppings. Serve with lime wedges.

name: Beef Quesadilla
ingredients: 2 cups shredded cheese (Monterey Jack, Cheddar, or Mexican blend)
1–2 tbsp butter or oil for the pan
Optional: 1/2 bell pepper (diced), 1/2 cup corn kernels
instructions: In a large skillet, brown ground beef with onions (and peppers if using). Drain excess fat.
Add garlic, taco seasoning, tomato paste, and water. Simmer for 2–3 minutes until liquid has mostly evaporated.
Wipe the skillet clean and return to medium heat with a small pat of butter.
Place a tortilla in the pan. Sprinkle cheese over the surface, then spread 1/2 cup beef mixture over one half.
Once cheese begins to melt, fold the empty half over the beef and press down.
Cook for 2–3 minutes per side until golden brown and crispy. Repeat for remaining tortillas.

name: Beef Burger
ingredients: Salt and black pepper to taste
4 slices of cheese (Cheddar, American, or Swiss)
Toppings: Lettuce, sliced tomato, red onion rings, pickles
instructions: Sauté chopped onions for 3–5 minutes until soft. Let cool.
In a bowl, combine beef, cooled onions, egg, herbs, salt, and pepper. Mix until just combined; do not overwork.
Divide into 4 equal portions and shape into patties about 3/4-inch thick.
Make a shallow indentation in the center of each patty with your thumb.
Optional: Refrigerate patties for 30 minutes to help them stay together.
Heat oil in a skillet or preheat grill to medium-high. Cook patties for 5–6 minutes per side.
Place a slice of cheese on each patty during the last minute of cooking to melt.
Assemble burgers by placing lettuce on the bottom bun, followed by the patty, tomato, onion, and condiments.

name: Beef Sliders
ingredients: 12-count package Hawaiian rolls or mini slider buns
6–8 slices of Cheddar, American, or Havarti cheese
1 tsp sesame seeds or Everything Bagel seasoning
Optional: Pickle chips, shredded lettuce, sliced tomatoes
instructions: Preheat oven to 350°F (175°C). Mix beef with diced onions, salt, pepper, garlic powder, and Worcestershire sauce.
Press the beef mixture into a 9x13 inch baking pan in an even layer. Bake for 15–20 minutes. Drain excess grease.
Slice the entire pack of rolls in half horizontally. Place the bottom half on a baking sheet.
Slide the cooked beef patty onto the buns, layer with cheese, and place the top half of the rolls on top.
Brush tops with melted butter and seeds. Bake for 5 minutes until cheese is melted and buns are toasted. Cut into individual sliders.

name: Beef Empanada
ingredients: Salt and black pepper to taste
1 egg, beaten with 1 tbsp water (egg wash)
instructions: Heat olive oil in a skillet. Sauté onion for 5 minutes, then add garlic and cook for 1 minute.
Add ground beef and cook until fully browned. Drain excess fat.
Stir in cumin, paprika, oregano, salt, and pepper. Simmer for 2–3 minutes.
Remove from heat and stir in olives and parsley. Let filling cool for 10 minutes.
Place a spoonful of filling in the center of an empanada disc.
Fold dough over filling to form a half-moon. Crimp edges shut with a fork.
Preheat oven to 400°F (200°C). Place empanadas on a lined baking sheet and brush with egg wash.
Bake for 20–25 minutes until golden brown and crispy.

name: Beef Siopao
ingredients: 500g beef chuck or brisket, finely cubed or ground
2 tbsp cornstarch dissolved in 1/4 cup water (slurry)
Optional: Hard-boiled egg wedges, star anise, bay leaf
instructions: Sauté garlic and onion, add beef and brown. Simmer with soy sauce, oyster sauce, sugar, and water until meat is tender. Thicken with slurry and cool completely.
Activate yeast in warm water and sugar. Mix with flour, remaining sugar, salt, oil, and baking powder. Knead until smooth and let rise for 1 hour.
Divide dough into 12–15 balls. Flatten each, add a spoonful of beef filling (and egg wedge), and pinch to seal.
Place buns on parchment paper squares and let rest for 15–20 minutes.
Steam on medium heat for 15 minutes. Let rest for 2 minutes before opening the lid to prevent collapsing.

name: Beef Lumpia
ingredients: Salt and black pepper to taste
instructions: In a bowl, combine beef, carrot, onion, garlic, egg, soy sauce, and oyster sauce. Season with salt and pepper and mix well.
Place 1 tablespoon of mixture on a lumpia wrapper. Fold in sides and roll tightly. Seal the edge with egg wash.
Heat oil to 350°F (175°C). Fry rolls in batches for 4–6 minutes until golden brown and meat is cooked through.
Drain on paper towels. Serve with sweet chili sauce or spicy vinegar.

name: Philly Cheesesteak
ingredients: 4–8 slices of White American or Provolone cheese
Salt and black pepper to taste
Optional: Garlic powder, green bell peppers, mushrooms
instructions: Freeze steak for 30–60 minutes, then slice against the grain as thinly as possible.
Sauté onions (and peppers if using) in a skillet until softened and lightly caramelized. Remove from pan.
Add more oil and cook steak on high heat until browned. Season with salt, pepper, and garlic powder.
Mix onions back with the meat. Divide into portions and top with cheese to melt.
Scoop cheesy meat into sliced hoagie rolls and serve immediately.

name: Beef Steak
ingredients: 2 tbsp high-smoke point oil (e.g., canola or grapeseed)
instructions: Remove steaks from the refrigerator 30-45 minutes before cooking to reach room temperature. Pat dry thoroughly with paper towels.
Season both sides generously with salt and pepper.
Heat oil in a heavy cast-iron skillet over high heat until it just begins to smoke.
Carefully place steaks in the pan. Sear for 3-4 minutes per side without moving them to develop a deep brown crust.
Reduce heat to medium-high. Add butter, garlic, and herbs to the skillet.
Once butter is melted and foaming, tilt the pan and use a large spoon to continuously baste the steaks with the flavored butter for another 2-3 minutes.
Check for desired doneness (135°F for medium-rare).
Transfer steaks to a cutting board and let rest for 5-10 minutes before slicing to retain juices.

name: Prime Rib
ingredients: 1 Standing Rib Roast (5–10 lbs, bone-in or boneless)
instructions: Temper the meat by removing it from the fridge 2 hours before cooking.
Preheat oven to 500°F (260°C).
In a small bowl, mix olive oil, garlic, salt, pepper, and herbs to create a rub.
Pat the roast dry and rub the herb paste over the entire surface.
Place roast in a heavy roasting pan, fat side up. Bake at 500°F for 15 minutes to create a crust.
Reduce oven temperature to 325°F (165°C) without opening the door.
Roast until internal temperature reaches 125°F for medium-rare (approx. 12-15 minutes per pound).
Remove from oven and tent loosely with foil. Let rest for 20-30 minutes before carving.

name: Roast Beef
ingredients: 3–4 lb beef roast (Top Round, Eye of Round, or Sirloin Tip)
instructions: Bring the beef to room temperature for 60 minutes and pat dry.
Mix oil, garlic, salt, pepper, and herbs into a paste; rub all over the roast.
Preheat oven to 450°F (230°C).
Place roast on a rack in a roasting pan and sear in the oven for 15 minutes.
Lower heat to 325°F (165°C) and continue roasting until internal temp hits 135°F (medium-rare) or 145°F (medium).
Remove from oven, tent with foil, and rest for 15-20 minutes.
Slice thinly against the grain for serving.

name: Beef BBQ
ingredients: 1 tsp each: garlic powder, onion powder
instructions: Mix brown sugar, garlic powder, onion powder, salt, and pepper; rub over the roast.
Optional: Sear the roast in a hot skillet for 2 minutes per side for extra flavor.
Place roast in a slow cooker. Add beef stock, vinegar, and Worcestershire sauce around the meat.
Pour 1 cup of BBQ sauce over the top.
Cook on Low for 8-10 hours (or High for 5-6 hours) until fork-tender.
Transfer meat to a bowl and shred with two forks, removing excess fat.
Return shredded beef to slow cooker, add remaining BBQ sauce, and warm for 15 minutes before serving.

name: Beef Satay
ingredients: 1.5 lbs flank steak or sirloin, sliced into thin 1-inch wide strips
1 tbsp each: brown sugar, lime juice, fish sauce (optional)
1 tsp each: minced ginger, turmeric powder, ground coriander
Peanut Sauce: 1/2 cup peanut butter, 1/2 cup coconut milk, 1 tbsp red curry paste, 1 tbsp lime juice
instructions: Whisk marinade ingredients and toss with beef. Refrigerate for at least 1 hour (up to overnight).
Soak bamboo skewers in water for 30 minutes.
Thread beef onto skewers in an accordion fashion.
Make peanut sauce: Combine sauce ingredients in a pan over medium-low heat; stir until smooth and thickened.
Preheat grill or grill pan to medium-high. Oil the grates.
Grill skewers for 2-3 minutes per side until charred and cooked through.
Serve hot with warm peanut sauce.

name: Carne Asada
ingredients: 2 lbs skirt steak or flank steak
instructions: Whisk oil, soy sauce, citrus juices, garlic, cilantro, and spices in a bag or bowl.
Add steak and marinate in the fridge for 2-4 hours (no more than 8).
Preheat grill to high heat (450°F).
Remove steak from marinade (discard marinade) and grill for 3-5 minutes per side for skirt steak (5-7 for flank).
Aim for internal temp of 130°F-135°F.
Let rest for 10 minutes under foil.
Slice thinly against the grain and serve in tortillas.

name: Beef Kebabs
ingredients: 1.5 to 2 lbs top sirloin, cut into 1-inch cubes
instructions: Soak wooden skewers for 30 minutes.
Whisk oil, soy sauce, Worcestershire, garlic, and seasonings. Marinate beef for at least 30 minutes.
Thread beef and vegetables onto skewers, alternating pieces.
Preheat grill to medium-high (400°F).
Grill for 8-10 minutes, turning every few minutes, until beef is cooked to preference.
Rest for 3-5 minutes before serving.

name: Churrasco
ingredients: 2.5 to 3 lbs Picanha (Top Sirloin Cap with fat cap)
Optional: 2 cloves garlic and 1 tbsp olive oil for rub
Chimichurri (optional): Parsley, garlic, red wine vinegar, olive oil, red pepper flakes
instructions: Slice Picanha into 2-inch thick steaks, cutting with the grain.
Fold steaks into a 'C' shape (fat cap out) and thread onto a long metal skewer.
Rub generously with coarse salt; let sit for 10 minutes then shake off excess.
Grill over high heat (charcoal preferred), searing fat side first then rotating.
Once browned, shave thin slices directly off the skewer for serving.
Return skewer to grill to continue cooking internal layers.

name: Beef Yakitori
ingredients: 1 lb beef sirloin or flap meat, 1-inch cubes
instructions: Simmer soy sauce, sugar, mirin, sake, garlic, and ginger for 5-8 minutes until slightly thickened into a glaze (Tare).
Thread beef and green onions onto soaked bamboo skewers.
Preheat grill or broiler to medium-high.
Grill for 2-3 minutes per side.
In the last 2 minutes of cooking, baste generously with the Tare sauce.
Remove and garnish with sesame seeds. Serve with extra sauce.

name: Smoked Brisket
ingredients: Spritz: 1 cup apple cider vinegar mixed with 1 cup water
instructions: Trim brisket, leaving 1/4 inch of the fat cap. Apply mustard then generous salt and pepper rub.
Preheat smoker to 225°F (107°C) using oak or hickory.
Place brisket in smoker (fat side down) and smoke until internal temp reaches 160°F-165°F (approx. 6-8 hours).
Spritz with vinegar mixture every hour after the first 3 hours.
Wrap tightly in peach butcher paper or foil once 'bark' is set (around 165°F).
Continue smoking until internal temp reaches 203°F and is probe-tender.
Rest in a cooler for at least 2 hours before slicing against the grain.
serving_size: 14-16 servings (1/2 lb cooked meat per person)

name: Beef Salad
ingredients: 1 tbsp brown sugar or palm sugar
1/2 tsp red chili flakes or 1 fresh Thai bird's eye chili, finely minced
1 head Romaine lettuce or 4 cups mixed greens
instructions: Rub the steak with soy sauce, oil, salt, and pepper. Grill or pan-sear over high heat for 3–4 minutes per side (for medium-rare).
Let the meat rest for 5–10 minutes before slicing thinly against the grain.
In a small bowl, whisk together lime juice, fish sauce, sugar, garlic, and chili until sugar dissolves.
In a large bowl, combine lettuce, cucumber, tomatoes, red onion, cilantro, and mint.
Add the sliced beef to the bowl, drizzle with dressing, and toss gently.
Sprinkle with crushed peanuts before serving.

name: Thai Beef Salad
ingredients: 1 lb sirloin, flank, or ribeye steak
Salt and freshly ground black pepper
2 tsp palm sugar or brown sugar
1-2 Thai bird's eye chilies, finely sliced
4 cups mixed salad greens or chopped romaine lettuce
1 English cucumber, halved and thinly sliced
1/2 small red onion, very thinly sliced
1/2 cup fresh mint leaves, roughly torn
Crushed roasted salted peanuts or toasted rice powder (optional)
instructions: Season the steak with salt, pepper, fish sauce, and lime juice. Grill or pan-sear over medium-high heat until medium-rare (3–4 minutes per side).
Transfer steak to a plate and let it rest for 10 minutes.
Whisk together the dressing ingredients: fish sauce, lime juice, sugar, garlic, and chilies until sugar dissolves.
In a large mixing bowl, combine greens, cucumber, tomatoes, red onion, cilantro, and mint.
Slice the rested steak thinly across the grain.
Add beef and any juices to the salad bowl, pour dressing over, toss gently, and serve immediately.

name: Beef Lettuce Wraps
ingredients: 1 tbsp olive oil or sesame oil
1 can (8 oz) water chestnuts, drained and finely chopped
1 head Butter, Bibb, or Romaine lettuce
instructions: Whisk together soy sauce, rice vinegar, brown sugar, sesame oil, red pepper flakes, and cornstarch. Set aside.
Heat oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat. Brown the ground beef, breaking it into crumbles, until cooked through (5-7 minutes). Drain excess grease.
Add red bell pepper, water chestnuts, garlic, and ginger to the skillet. Sauté for 3–4 minutes.
Pour the sauce over the beef mixture and stir for 1–2 minutes until thickened and evenly coated.
Remove from heat and stir in green onions.
Spoon the beef mixture into lettuce leaves and serve immediately.

name: Beef Stir-Fry
ingredients: 1 lb sirloin or flank steak, thinly sliced against the grain
2 tbsp vegetable or avocado oil, divided
4 cups mixed vegetables (broccoli, carrots, bell pepper, snap peas)
instructions: Whisk together broth, soy sauce, honey, cornstarch, and sesame oil in a small bowl.
Heat 1 tbsp oil in a large skillet or wok over high heat. Sear beef strips for 2–3 minutes until browned, then remove and set aside.
Add remaining oil and stir-fry mixed vegetables for 4–5 minutes until crisp-tender.
Add garlic and ginger, cook for 30 seconds, then return beef to the pan.
Whisk the sauce again and pour over the beef and veggies. Stir for 1–2 minutes until sauce thickens.
Serve immediately over rice or noodles.

name: Beef Veggie Bowl
ingredients: 2 cups cooked white or brown rice
instructions: Brown and crumble ground beef in a large skillet over medium-high heat. Drain excess grease.
Add zucchini, carrots, bell pepper, and most of the green onions. Sauté for 7–10 minutes until tender-crisp.
Stir in brown sugar, soy sauce, sesame oil, Gochujang, ginger, and garlic powder.
Simmer for 5 minutes to let flavors meld and sauce thicken.
Divide rice into bowls, top with beef mixture, and garnish with remaining green onions.

name: Beef Quinoa Bowl
ingredients: Salt and black pepper to taste
instructions: Combine quinoa and water/broth in a pot. Bring to a boil, cover, and simmer for 15 minutes. Fluff with a fork.
Heat oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat. Brown the beef and drain excess fat.
Stir in garlic and ginger, cooking for 1 minute.
Whisk together brown sugar, soy sauce, sesame oil, and red pepper flakes. Pour over beef.
Simmer for 3–5 minutes until sauce thickens. Season with salt and pepper.
Divide quinoa into bowls, top with beef, and garnish with green onions and sesame seeds.

name: Beef Zoodle Bowl
ingredients: 1 lb extra lean ground beef or thinly sliced steak
2 tbsp low-sodium tamari or soy sauce
instructions: Whisk together tamari, sesame oil, honey, garlic, ginger, and red pepper flakes.
Brown the beef in a large skillet over medium-high heat (about 5 minutes). Drain excess fat.
Pour the sauce over the beef and add zoodles to the skillet.
Flash sauté for 2–3 minutes until zoodles are heated through but still firm.
Remove from heat, stir in green onions, and sprinkle with sesame seeds.

name: Steamed Beef
ingredients: 1 lb flank steak, thinly sliced against the grain
instructions: Toss the sliced beef with soy sauce, minced ginger, cornstarch, oil, and sugar. Let marinate for 15 minutes.
Spread the beef thinly and evenly on a heatproof plate.
Place the plate in a steamer over boiling water.
Steam on high heat for 8–10 minutes until just tender.
Garnish with fresh scallions and a drizzle of hot peanut oil if desired.

name: Poached Beef
ingredients: 600g–800g beef eye fillet, trimmed of fat
1 liter low-sodium beef or chicken stock
Salt and black pepper for seasoning
instructions: Combine stock, onion, carrots, bay leaves, rosemary, thyme, peppercorns, and wine in a large pot. Simmer for 10-15 minutes.
Season the beef fillet with salt and pepper.
Gently lower the beef into the shimmering liquid, ensuring it is submerged.
Poach for 18–20 minutes for medium-rare (adjust time for desired doneness).
Remove beef and let it rest for 10 minutes before slicing into medallions.
Serve with the poached carrots and a light herb vinaigrette.

name: Beef Avocado Salad
instructions: Season the steak with salt, pepper, garlic powder, and chili powder.
Cook in a skillet over medium-high heat for 5-6 minutes per side (medium-rare).
Let steak rest for 5 minutes, then slice thinly against the grain.
Whisk together olive oil, lemon juice, parsley, salt, and pepper for the dressing.
In a large bowl, toss lettuce, cucumber, and green onions.
Top with avocado and steak strips, drizzle with dressing, and serve.

name: Beef Pot Pie
ingredients: 1 ½ lbs sirloin or chuck roast, cut into ½-inch cubes
2 carrots, peeled and sliced into rounds
1 tsp salt (plus more for seasoning beef)
1/2 cup whole milk or heavy cream
instructions: Preheat oven to 375°F (190°C).
In a large skillet, brown the beef in batches with oil/salt/pepper; remove and set aside.
In the same pan, melt butter and sauté onion, carrots, and celery for 5 minutes. Add garlic and cook for 30 seconds.
Sprinkle flour over vegetables and stir for 1 minute. Slowly whisk in beef broth and milk; simmer until thickened (3-5 minutes).
Stir in the beef, potatoes (par-boiled if preferred), peas, Worcestershire sauce, and herbs. Adjust seasoning and remove from heat.
Place one pie crust into a 9-inch pie plate. Pour in the filling and top with the second crust.
Trim and crimp the edges to seal. Cut 3-4 slits in the top crust.
Brush with egg wash and bake for 35-45 minutes until the crust is golden and filling is bubbling.
Let rest for 10 minutes before slicing.

name: Meatloaf
ingredients: Glaze: 1/4 cup ketchup, 1 tbsp brown sugar, 1 tsp mustard
instructions: Preheat oven to 350°F (175°C). Grease a loaf pan or line a baking sheet.
In a large bowl, combine beef, breadcrumbs, eggs, milk, onion, garlic, 1/2 cup ketchup, Worcestershire, and seasonings.
Mix until just combined (do not overmix).
Shape into a loaf in the pan or on the baking sheet. Bake for 40 minutes.
Whisk glaze ingredients together. Spread over the meatloaf after the initial 40-minute bake.
Bake for another 15-20 minutes until internal temperature reaches 160°F (71°C).
Rest for 10 minutes before slicing.

name: Beef Casserole
ingredients: 800g (1.75 lbs) beef chuck or stewing steak, 3cm cubes
Salt and black pepper to taste
instructions: Toss beef chunks in flour, salt, and pepper until coated.
In a Dutch oven, brown the beef in oil over medium-high heat; set aside.
Sauté onions, carrots, and celery for 5 minutes. Stir in tomato paste and cook for 1 minute.
Deglaze the pan with wine or stock, scraping up the browned bits.
Return beef to the pot. Add potatoes, stock, Worcestershire, and herbs. Ensure liquid covers ingredients.
Cover and bake at 160°C (325°F) for 2.5-3 hours, or simmer on low on the stovetop for 2-2.5 hours.
Remove bay leaves and adjust seasoning before serving.

name: Moussaka
ingredients: 1 cup tomato passata or crushed tomatoes
Béchamel: 4 tbsp butter, 5 tbsp flour, 2.5 cups milk, 1/2 cup grated cheese, 1/4 tsp nutmeg, 1-2 egg yolks
instructions: Salt eggplant slices and let sit for 30 minutes. Rinse and pat dry.
Roast or fry eggplant and potato slices until softened; set aside.
In a pan, brown beef with onion and garlic. Drain excess fat.
Stir in tomato paste, wine, passata, stock, and spices. Simmer for 30-40 minutes until thick. Stir in parsley.
For béchamel: Melt butter, whisk in flour. Gradually add milk until thick. Remove from heat and stir in cheese, nutmeg, and egg yolks.
Layer potatoes (if using), then eggplant, then the meat sauce in a 9x13 dish.
Pour béchamel over the top and sprinkle with extra cheese.
Bake at 350°F (180°C) for 45-50 minutes until golden brown.
Rest for 20-30 minutes before serving.

name: Beef Enchiladas
ingredients: 2 cups shredded cheese (Cheddar or Monterey Jack)
Optional: 4 oz diced green chiles, black beans, sour cream, cilantro
instructions: Preheat oven to 350°F (175°C). Grease a 9x13 baking dish.
Brown beef and onion; drain fat. Add garlic and cook for 1 minute.
Stir in taco seasoning and water; simmer for 3-5 minutes until evaporated.
Spread 1/2 cup of sauce in the bottom of the dish.
Fill tortillas with beef mixture and cheese; roll tightly and place seam-side down in dish.
Top with remaining sauce and cheese. Cover with foil.
Bake for 20 minutes covered, then 5-10 minutes uncovered until bubbly.
Rest for 5 minutes before serving.

name: Stuffed Bell Peppers
ingredients: 1 cup shredded mozzarella or cheddar cheese
instructions: Preheat oven to 350°F (175°C). Cut tops off peppers and remove seeds.
Brown beef and onion in a skillet; drain grease. Add garlic and cook for 1 minute.
Stir in rice, half of the tomato sauce, and seasonings. Mix well.
Stand peppers in a baking dish. Spoon filling into each pepper.
Pour remaining tomato sauce over peppers and add 1/2 cup water to the bottom of the dish.
Cover with foil and bake for 35 minutes.
Remove foil, add cheese to the tops, and bake uncovered for 10 minutes until cheese is melted and peppers are tender.

name: Shepherd’s Pie
instructions: Boil potatoes until tender (15-20 mins). Drain and mash with butter and milk. Season with salt/pepper.
Preheat oven to 400°F (200°C).
Brown beef in a large skillet; drain fat. Add onion and carrots, sauté for 5-7 mins.
Stir in garlic, thyme, and rosemary (1 min). Stir in tomato paste and flour (2 mins).
Gradually add beef broth and Worcestershire; simmer until thickened. Stir in peas.
Transfer filling to a baking dish and spread mashed potatoes over the top. Create ridges with a fork.
Bake for 25-30 minutes until golden brown and bubbly.

name: Beef Bake
ingredients: 2 lbs round steak or stew meat, 1-inch cubes
instructions: Preheat oven to 400°F (200°C).
In a casserole dish, toss beef cubes with flour until coated.
Bake uncovered for 20 minutes.
Remove from oven and stir in onion soup mix, carrots, mushrooms, tomatoes, and water.
Cover tightly and bake at 350°F or 400°F for 1.5 hours until beef is fork-tender.
Serve over hot cooked noodles or rice.

name: Pastitsio
ingredients: Pasta binder: 2 egg whites, 1 cup feta or parmesan cheese
Béchamel: 1/2 cup butter, 3/4 cup flour, 4 cups warm milk, 2 egg yolks, 1/2 cup grated cheese, 1/4 tsp nutmeg
instructions: Cook pasta until just under al dente. Drain and toss with egg whites and cheese.
Brown beef with onion and garlic. Add tomatoes, paste, cinnamon, cloves, salt, and pepper. Simmer for 30-40 mins until thick.
For béchamel: Melt butter, whisk in flour. Gradually whisk in warm milk until thick. Add yolks, cheese, and nutmeg.
Layer pasta in a greased 9x13 dish. Spread meat sauce (cinnamon stick removed) over pasta.
Top with béchamel and extra cheese.
Bake at 350°F (175°C) for 45-55 minutes until golden brown.
Rest for 30-45 minutes before slicing to ensure clean squares.

name: Beef Tamale Pie
ingredients: Optional: black beans, sour cream, jalapeños
instructions: Preheat oven to 400°F (200°C). Grease a 9x9 baking dish.
Brown beef, onion, and bell pepper; drain grease.
Stir in taco seasoning, tomatoes, and corn. Simmer for 5 minutes.
In a bowl, mix corn muffin mix, egg, and milk until just combined.
Pour beef mixture into the dish. Spread cornbread batter evenly over the top.
Bake for 15-20 minutes until the cornbread is golden brown.
Sprinkle cheese on top and bake for 2-3 minutes until melted.
Rest for 5 minutes before serving.

name: Beef Bulgogi
ingredients: 1.5 lbs (700g) Ribeye or Top Sirloin, thinly sliced
instructions: Whisk together the grated pear, soy sauce, brown sugar, sesame oil, garlic, ginger, mirin, and black pepper in a large bowl.
Add the thinly sliced beef and sliced onions to the bowl. Massage the marinade into the meat. Cover and refrigerate for at least 2 hours (overnight preferred).
Heat a cast-iron skillet or grill pan over high heat with vegetable oil until smoking hot.
Cook the beef in batches in a single layer for 2-3 minutes until browned and slightly caramelized. Flip and cook for another minute.
Toss in the green onions during the last minute of cooking the final batch.
Transfer to a platter and garnish with toasted sesame seeds and extra green onions.

name: Beef Sukiyaki
ingredients: 1 lb (450g) high-quality beef (ribeye or sirloin), paper-thin slices
8.5 oz Shirataki noodles, rinsed and drained
2 stalks Negi or green onions, sliced diagonally
1 tbsp vegetable oil or beef tallow
instructions: Whisk together soy sauce, mirin, sake, sugar, and water to make the broth (warishita).
Parboil shirataki noodles for 1 minute, then drain.
Heat a shallow skillet or sukiyaki pot over medium-high heat with oil or tallow.
Sear a few slices of beef quickly, sprinkling with a pinch of sugar.
Pour half the broth over the beef and push beef to one side.
Arrange tofu, mushrooms, noodles, and vegetables in separate sections of the pot and add remaining broth.
Cover and simmer for 5-10 minutes until vegetables are tender.
Serve with individual bowls of whisked raw egg for dipping.

name: Beef Hot Pot
ingredients: 1.5 lbs thinly sliced beef (ribeye or sirloin)
instructions: Combine water, soy sauce, sake, mirin, sugar, and chili oil in a bowl.
Heat 3 tbsp oil in a large pot over medium-high heat. Sauté green onions and tofu until browned.
Add cabbage, mushrooms, and noodles. Pour the broth over ingredients.
Bring to a simmer, cover, and cook for 10 minutes.
Stir in greens until wilted. Add thinly sliced beef and cook for 2-3 minutes until done.
Serve immediately, traditionally with raw egg yolk for dipping.

name: Mapo Tofu with Beef
ingredients: 1.5 lbs soft or medium-firm Tofu, 1-inch cubes
1.5 tbsp Fermented Black Beans (Douchi), rinsed and chopped
1 tsp Sichuan Peppercorns, toasted and ground
1 tbsp Cornstarch mixed with 2 tbsp cold water
instructions: Simmer tofu cubes in salted water for 1-2 minutes, then drain.
Toast Sichuan peppercorns in a dry wok, then grind into powder.
Heat oil in a wok over high heat and brown the ground beef until crispy.
Reduce heat to medium, add ginger, garlic, and scallion whites. Sauté until fragrant.
Add doubanjiang and fermented black beans. Stir-fry until the oil turns red.
Add Shaoxing wine, soy sauces, sugar, and chicken stock. Bring to a boil.
Carefully add tofu and simmer for 3-5 minutes.
Stir in cornstarch slurry in stages until the sauce thickens.
Off heat, stir in half the ground peppercorns and scallion greens.
Serve topped with remaining peppercorn powder.

name: Beef Korma
ingredients: 1 kg Beef chuck or gravy beef, 3cm cubes
Fried Cashews or toasted Almonds for garnish
instructions: Heat ghee in a large pan and brown beef in batches. Set aside.
Reduce heat, sauté onions until softened, then add garlic for 1 minute.
Add korma paste and sauté for 1 minute until fragrant.
Return beef to pan. Stir in water/stock, yogurt, and brown sugar. Season with salt.
Simmer covered on low heat for 1 to 1.5 hours until beef is tender.
Stir through coriander and serve over basmati rice.

name: Beef Vindaloo
ingredients: 1 kg (2.2 lbs) boneless Beef (chuck or stewing), 1-inch cubes
instructions: Toast cumin, mustard seeds, peppercorns, cloves, cinnamon, and bay leaf until fragrant, then grind into powder.
Blend ground spices with chillies, vinegar, garlic, and ginger into a smooth paste. Marinate beef in this paste for at least 2 hours.
Fry onions in oil until deep golden brown. Add beef and sear.
Stir in vindaloo paste and fry for 3-5 minutes until oil separates.
Add 1 cup water, bring to boil, then simmer covered for 45-60 minutes until beef is tender.
Season with salt and sugar. Simmer uncovered to thicken if needed.

name: Beef Laksa
ingredients: 850g Gravy Beef or chuck steak, 3cm pieces
Spring onions, Coriander, Mint, Lime wedges for garnish
instructions: Brown beef in oil in batches. Return all beef to the pan.
Add laksa paste, lemongrass, onion, garlic, and chilli. Cook for 2 minutes.
Add beef stock and coconut milk. Simmer covered on low for 2 to 2.5 hours until beef is tender.
Stir in lime juice and fish sauce.
Cook rice noodles according to packet instructions and divide into bowls.
Ladle soup and beef over noodles. Garnish with bean sprouts, herbs, and lime.

name: Beef Pad Thai
ingredients: 1 lb Sirloin, flank steak, or ribeye, thinly sliced
8 oz Rice Noodles (dried wide or medium)
3 tbsp Brown Sugar or coconut sugar
Crushed peanuts, Lime wedges, fresh Cilantro for garnish
instructions: Soak rice noodles in hot water for 20-30 minutes until al dente. Drain.
Whisk tamarind, fish sauce, sugar, oyster sauce, and sriracha to make the sauce.
Sear beef in 1 tbsp oil over high heat for 1-2 minutes per side. Remove and set aside.
Add remaining oil, sauté garlic and ginger for 30 seconds.
Push aromatics aside, scramble eggs until just cooked.
Add noodles and sauce. Toss for 2-3 minutes, adding water if too dry.
Return beef to pan, add bean sprouts and scallions. Toss for 1 minute.
Garnish with peanuts, cilantro, and lime.

name: Beef Sate Lilit
instructions: Blend shallots, garlic, chilies, ginger, galangal, turmeric, coriander, sugar, and tamarind into a paste. Sauté for 2-3 minutes, then cool.
Combine ground beef, coconut, coconut milk, lime leaves, and spice paste in a bowl. Mix until tacky.
Trim and lightly crush the bulbous end of lemongrass stalks.
Wrap a portion of meat mixture around the bulbous end of each lemongrass stalk, pressing firmly.
Grill skewers over medium heat for 3-5 minutes per side until cooked and charred.
Serve with sambal matah or sweet soy sauce.

name: Beef Teppanyaki
ingredients: 1 lb (450g) Sirloin, Ribeye, or NY Strip, thinly sliced
1 tsp Garlic powder or 2 cloves minced garlic
1 bunch Asparagus or sliced bell peppers (optional)
1 tbsp Soy Sauce for seasoning vegetables
instructions: Whisk soy sauce, mirin, garlic, pepper, and ginger. Marinate beef for at least 1 hour.
Heat a large flat griddle or heavy skillet over medium-high heat until very hot.
Drizzle 2 tbsp oil. Sear beef in a single layer for 1-2 minutes per side. Remove and set aside.
Add remaining oil and stir-fry optional vegetables for 3-5 minutes. Season with soy sauce.
Return beef to pan for 30 seconds to combine and toss with juices.
Serve hot with steamed rice or dipping sauce like ponzu.

name: Beef Omelette
ingredients: 2 oz (approx. 1/4 cup) ground beef (80/20 or lean)
½ tbsp butter or cooking oil
Optional: Shredded cheddar cheese, chopped cilantro, or sliced avocado
instructions: Cook the Beef Filling: Place a small skillet over medium-high heat. Add the ground beef and chopped onions. Cook until the beef is thoroughly browned and the onions are translucent (about 3–5 minutes).
Season with a pinch of salt, pepper, and chili powder. Drain any excess grease and set the filling aside in a small bowl.
Prepare the Eggs: In a separate bowl, crack the eggs and whisk them vigorously with a pinch of salt and pepper until the yolks and whites are fully combined and slightly frothy.

Cook the Omelette: Wipe the skillet clean and return it to medium heat. Add the butter or oil. Once the butter is melted and bubbling slightly, pour in the egg mixture. Tilt the pan to ensure the eggs cover the entire bottom.
Use a spatula to gently lift the edges, allowing the raw egg to flow underneath.
Assemble: When the eggs are mostly set but the top still looks slightly moist, spoon the warm beef filling onto one-half of the omelette. If using cheese, sprinkle it over the beef now.
Fold and Serve: Carefully fold the empty half of the omelette over the beef filling using a spatula.
Let it sit for another 30–60 seconds to ensure the center is warm and any cheese is melted.
Slide the omelette onto a plate and garnish with cilantro or avocado if desired.

name: Beef Scramble
ingredients: 3 cups frozen hash browns (shredded or cubed)
4 slices sharp cheddar cheese (or 1 cup shredded)
Optional: Chopped cilantro or green onions for garnish
instructions: Brown the Beef: In a large skillet over medium heat, cook the ground beef and diced onion until the meat is no longer pink. Drain any excess fat.
Season: Stir in the paprika, salt, and pepper.
Add Potatoes: Add the frozen hash browns to the skillet. Cook, stirring occasionally, until the potatoes are thawed and starting to soften.
Simmer: Pour in the water and the undrained diced tomatoes. Bring the mixture to a boil, then reduce the heat to low. Cover and simmer for about 5–10 minutes until the potatoes are tender.
Scramble the Eggs: Increase the heat to medium-low. Pour the beaten eggs over the beef and potato mixture. Stir gently and cook until the eggs are fully set to your preference.
Melt the Cheese: Lay the cheese slices (or sprinkle shredded cheese) over the top. Cover the skillet for 1–2 minutes until the cheese is melted and gooey.
Serve: Garnish with fresh cilantro or green onions if desired. Serve hot.

name: Beef Sandwich Melt
ingredients: 1 lb sliced or shredded roast beef (deli-style or leftover)
8 slices of bread (Sourdough, Rye, or thick-cut White)
8–16 slices of cheese (Sharp Cheddar, Swiss, or Provolone)
Salt and black pepper to taste
Optional: Caramelized onions, horseradish mayo, or Dijon mustard
instructions: Warm the Beef: Heat a large skillet over medium heat. Add the roast beef and season with salt and pepper. Toss for 2–3 minutes until heated through. Remove the beef and set it aside.
Prepare the Bread: Wipe the skillet clean. Butter one side of each slice of bread.

Assemble: Place 4 slices of bread in the skillet, buttered-side down. Top each with a slice of cheese, a generous portion of the warm roast beef, and another slice of cheese. Top with the remaining bread slices, buttered-side up.
Grill: Cook for 3–4 minutes per side over medium heat until the bread is golden brown and the cheese is completely melted.
Serve: Remove from the pan, slice diagonally, and serve immediately.

name: Beef Panini
ingredients: 2 slices of hearty artisan bread (Sourdough, Ciabatta, or Focaccia)
¼ lb thinly sliced deli roast beef
2 slices of Provolone, Swiss, or Sharp Cheddar
Small handful of baby spinach or arugula
½ tbsp olive oil or softened butter
instructions: Preheat: Turn on your panini press or heat a grill pan over medium-high heat.
Prep the Bread: Lightly brush the outside of each bread slice with olive oil or butter.

Layer the Inside: Place one slice of bread oiled-side down. Spread the mayonnaise (and horseradish, if using) on the top side. Layer on one slice of cheese, followed by the roast beef, tomatoes, and spinach. Top with the second slice of cheese and the final slice of bread, oiled-side up.
Grill: Place the sandwich in the panini press. Close the lid and cook for 3 to 5 minutes, or until the bread is golden brown and the cheese has completely melted.
Serve: Carefully remove the panini, let it rest for one minute, cut it diagonally, and enjoy!

name: Beef Flatbread
ingredients: 1 cup shredded mozzarella or crumbled feta
Optional: Fresh parsley, cherry tomatoes, tzatziki sauce
instructions: Preheat: Preheat your oven to 400°F (200°C). Line a large baking sheet with parchment paper.
Cook the Beef: In a large skillet, heat 1 tbsp olive oil over medium-high heat. Add the ground beef and cook until browned (about 5–7 minutes). Drain any excess fat.

Sauté Aromatics: Add the sliced red onion to the skillet with the beef. Cook for 3–4 minutes until the onion is softened. Stir in the minced garlic, cumin, coriander, oregano, salt, and pepper. Cook for 1 more minute until fragrant, then remove from heat.
Assemble the Flatbreads: Place the flatbreads on the prepared baking sheet. Evenly distribute the beef and onion mixture across the four flatbreads.
Add Cheese: Sprinkle the mozzarella or feta cheese over the beef mixture.
Bake: Bake for 8–10 minutes, or until the cheese is melted and bubbly and the edges of the flatbread are golden brown.
Garnish and Serve: Remove from the oven and top with fresh parsley, cherry tomatoes, and a drizzle of tzatziki if desired. Slice and serve immediately.

name: Beef Pizza
ingredients: 1/2 lb to 1 lb lean ground beef
Optional: Sliced bell peppers, black olives, or fresh basil
instructions: Preheat Oven: Preheat oven to 500°F (260°C).

Cook the Beef: In a skillet over medium-high heat, cook ground beef with chopped onion and garlic. Season with oregano, salt, and pepper. Cook for 3–5 minutes until browned. Drain excess fat and set aside.
Prepare the Dough: Roll or stretch the dough into a 12-inch circle. Transfer it to a greased pizza pan.
Assemble: Spread pizza sauce evenly over the dough. Distribute the cooked beef mixture over the sauce. Sprinkle mozzarella and cheddar cheeses over the top.
Bake: Bake for 10–12 minutes, or until the crust is golden brown and the cheese is bubbling.
Serve: Let the pizza rest for 2–3 minutes before slicing.

name: Beef Toast
ingredients: 1 jar (2.25 oz) or 1 pouch (3 oz) dried chipped beef, chopped
4–6 slices of sturdy white bread, sourdough, or Texas toast
Optional: pinch of cayenne or garlic powder
instructions: Rinse the Beef (Optional): Rinse the chopped beef under cold water and pat dry before cooking to reduce saltiness.
Make the Roux: In a medium skillet over medium heat, melt the butter. Whisk in the flour and cook for 1–2 minutes until slightly nutty.
Build the Sauce: Slowly whisk in the milk, a little at a time. Continue whisking until the sauce thickens and begins to simmer.
Combine: Stir in the chopped beef and black pepper. Let it simmer for 2–3 minutes until the beef is heated through and the sauce is creamy.
Serve: Toast your bread until golden brown. Spoon the warm beef and gravy generously over the toast and serve immediately.

name: Beef Wrap Melt
ingredients: 1 cup Shredded Cheddar or Monterey Jack
Salt and Black Pepper to taste
instructions: Prepare Sauce: Stir together the sour cream, cream cheese, and hot sauce. Microwave for 20–30 seconds to soften, then stir until smooth.
Cook Filling: Heat a large pan over medium-high heat. Add the ground beef and sliced peppers. Season with salt and pepper. Cook until browned (about 5–7 minutes). Drain any excess grease.
Assemble: Spread the creamy sauce over one half of each tortilla. Top with the beef and pepper mixture, then sprinkle generously with cheese. Fold the tortillas in half.
The Melt: Melt 1 tbsp of butter in the pan over medium heat. Place the folded wraps in the pan and cook for 3–4 minutes per side until the tortilla is golden brown and the cheese is fully melted.
Serve: Cut into wedges and serve immediately.

name: Beef Rice Bowl
ingredients: ¼ tsp crushed red pepper flakes (optional)
2–3 cups hot cooked rice (Jasmine or Calrose)
instructions: Brown the Beef: In a large skillet over medium-high heat, cook the ground beef and minced garlic until browned and no longer pink (about 5–7 minutes).
Drain: Carefully drain any excess grease from the skillet.
Prepare the Sauce: Whisk together the soy sauce, brown sugar, sesame oil, ginger, red pepper flakes, and black pepper.
Simmer: Pour the sauce over the cooked beef. Let the mixture simmer over medium-low heat for 2–3 minutes until the sauce thickens slightly.
Assemble: Divide the hot rice into four bowls. Top each with a generous portion of the beef mixture.
Garnish: Sprinkle with sliced green onions and sesame seeds before serving.

name: Beef Stir-Fry Noodles
ingredients: 1 lb Top Sirloin or Flank Steak, sliced thinly against the grain
8 oz Noodles (rice noodles, udon, or ramen)
instructions: Prepare the Sauce: Whisk together the soy sauce, minced garlic, grated ginger, brown sugar, sesame oil, cornstarch, and optional oyster/chili sauce.
Cook the Noodles: Prepare your noodles according to the package instructions. Drain and set aside.
Sear the Beef: Heat 1 tbsp oil in a large wok or skillet. Add the beef slices and cook for 2–3 minutes until browned. Remove the beef and set aside.
Stir-Fry Vegetables: Add the remaining tablespoon of oil. Add the onions and cook for 2 minutes. Add the bell peppers, carrots, and broccoli. Stir-fry for 3–5 minutes until crisp-tender.
Combine: Return the cooked beef and noodles to the pan with the vegetables.
Thicken: Pour the sauce over the mixture. Toss for 1–2 minutes until the sauce has thickened and evenly coats the noodles and beef.
Serve: Garnish with sesame seeds or sliced green onions and serve immediately.
